Research can be used to codify the generic, action-oriented and aesthetic terms on this basis, avoiding only emotional-frequency statistics.

sources.title: Global literary research frontier: theory, methodology, region, case overview
sources.filename: Global Literary Research Front — Regional Case Panorama of Theory Approaches.docx

blocks.text: Global Literary Research Front
New directions, new theories, new approaches, regional knowledge and case maps
The academicly detailed version of the data up to July 13, 2026 Theory, Methodology, Region, Case
Summary and Guide
Summary

The report, which focuses on the “what is the front line of global literature research in the 2020s”, examines systematically nine regions of North America, Europe, mainland China and Hong Kong, Japan and South Korea, South and South-East Asia, Latin America, Africa, the Middle East and the Arab world, Russia-Ukraine-Central and Eastern Europe, and provides balanced coverage of digital humanities and computational literature, AI and literature, environmental humanities, post-colonialism and decolonisation, world literature and translation, emotional and emotional movements, cognitive literature and neuro aesthetics, gender-coole-crossing gender, memory and trauma, medical humanities, voice and media archaeology. Information retrieval and verification was completed on 13 July 2026; for conferences, presentations, projects or books still under way in 2026, only as “agenda signals” and not as academic findings that have been developed.

The core judgement of the report is that contemporary literary research has not entered an era of some kind of new “ism” that has replaced old theory, but rather a phase of reorganization of objects, devices and responsibilities. The literature audience is no longer just a stable print text, but also includes differences in versions, language libraries, model outputs, tips and editing chains, networks of translators, platform logs, sound, short video, manuscript images, clinical narratives, environmental infrastructure and wartime webshots; accordingly, the most interpretive new theory is often generated from infrastructure and local practice. Bichitra makes “works” a web of content, KITAB makes “books” a web of text, ALMEDA makes metadata classification a colonial knowledge issue, and SUCHO turns preservation speed, sensitivity and cultural survival into a literary research method.

Five transregional shifts were most significant. First, the shift from “theoretical application” to “distributive” production: pluriverse, AI-Mundo in Latin America, submersionalization with submersible mechanisms, hydrocolonism in Africa, re-during for water and animist materialism in Hong Kong, unruly comparison in South Asia, Dalit testimonio in South Asia and declassification, inter-imperiality in Central and Eastern Europe, and colonial mechanisms are not local examples of English-American theory. Second, the shift from “digital tools” to infrastructure criticism: copyright of language, classification, language cover, servers, maintenance work, version and failure records became part of the case. Thirdly, the shift from “AI Whether or not to a training spectrum, author responsibility and measurement effectiveness: Model memory may disguise network visibility as a literary understanding, and distributed authorship does not mean responsibility disappears. Fourth, there is a shift from abstract “human” to more human beings, health and dependency: water, energy, bacteria, animals, plants, pollution, disability and care to rewrite literary subjects. Fifthly, the shift from “expanding visibility” to the ethics of visibility: openness, translation, empathy and preservation can all lead to secondary extraction, and silence, ambiguity, withdrawal and hierarchical access become legitimate methods.

Methodologically, the report presents an eight-step workflow of “problem positioning-material spectrometry-ethical governance-multiscale analysis-referral-reverse-repeated expression-community feedback”. Accomplishment, experiment or generation AI constitutes reliable human evidence only when combined with a linguistic validation, detailed reading, historical context and uncertainty report. OpenAlex trend maps only include precise conceptual labels in 2010-2025 title/summar, showing an overall increase in the visibility of metadata from digital, environmental, emotional, memory, medical and sound research, but this indicator is not equivalent to the full volume of publications, much less to be used to rank regions or theories. (Priem et al. 2022)

The report finally gives a vision of the confidence-sharing for 2026-2030. High faith orientations include: AI Training and Rights Audit of Literary Research, Multilingual Programming Materials and Maintenance Work, Institutional Decolonisation Audit; Medium Confidence Directions, Environmental-Health-Energy-AI Infrastructure Co-location, Medical Humanization to Disability and Coproduction; Medium Faith Directions, Emergency Humanism Institutionalization, Long Texts of Cognitive Literature and Intercultural Experiments. All projections include counter-arguments that may reverse it.

Keywords: Global literary research; digital humanities; AI and literature; colonization; environmental humanities; world literature; emotional theory; cognitive literature; gender and the Cool; memory research; medical humanities; sound research
How to use the present report
Documental overview: Read chapters I and II of the conceptual and methodological boundary, then enter the subject under part II, and finally correct regional differences with part III.
Design papers or topics: Use the “Methodologies-materials-risk” matrix in chapter II and the subject template in chapter XXV; do not push the question back from “I want to use a tool”.

Postgraduate courses: Four sets of questions can be organized according to “how the object is made - how the method is to say that it knows - who bears the cost - what should not be completely public”, rather than a weekly newism.

Assessment hot spots: Matching peer review results, periodical/conference agendas, project infrastructure, dissemination of methods with at least two types of signals on public issues; banning a meeting, a keyword or a star scholar from writing into a regional consensus.

Check source: The text uses the author-year quote; each reference at the end of the text retains the evidence keys (e.g. (Priem et al., 2022)) to facilitate the checking with the source matrix. Dynamic web pages and project information are marked as institutional/agenda evidence and do not contain theoretical findings beyond page contents.

Read the alert: The regional heat map indicates the “valitable evidence intensity” of the report, not the academic output, quality or impact ranking; the total regional score should not be calculated. OpenAlex charts the metadata visibility of the conceptual label, not the total output of the field.
Twelve key findings.

The “post-theory” is not a retreat from theory. The ambitious paradigm is being transformed into a meso-level mechanism that can be traced: classification, translation, training data, infrastructure, emotional obligations, memory systems and accountability chains.
Infrastructure itself production theory. Databases, versions, interfaces, licences and catalogues are not neutral containers, but determine what can be literary objects.

The front line of the computational study is the responsibility to interpret. Large samples are not a requirement; high density small language, parameter sensitivity, abnormal value rereading and remnant chains are equally important. (Underwood, 2019; Bode, 2023)

AI The weakest problem with literary research is “likes like human beings”. The more important question is what the model has seen, who has contributed data and editing, who can withdraw, who bears the copyright and ecological costs. (Hayles, 2018; U.S. Copyright Office, 2025)

The environment has shifted from a “natural theme” to a colonial history, infrastructure and more human relations. The “human world” has been constantly revised by black human beings, blue humanities, anti-colonial pollution research and the common world approach. (Chakrabarty, 2021; Yusoff, 2018; Liboiron, 2021)

The most test form of colonization is governance. Who defines the headings, hosts, decides to open, signs translation and gains in employment, is better able to distinguish between material change and rhetorical decoration than “adding to the Southern Text”. (Smith, 2021; Tuck et al., 2012; Mignolo et al., 2018)

World literature is moving from “cross-border” to “circulation mechanisms”. The universal nature of the production of the works produced jointly by the version, translation, representation, funding, book reviews, courses and platforms; non-translation and translation violence are not external issues in circulation. (Damrosch, 2003; Apter, 2013; Walkowitz, 2015; Cheah, 2016)

The emotional study has moved away from the “emotional more real” assumption. The political economy of emotion and the obligations of readers are revealed by the lack of feeling, rejection, embarrassment, twilight, attachment and formality. (Sedgwick, 2003; Berlant, 2011; Ngai, 2020)

The reliable future of cognitive literature is a multi-method rather than a brain-zone decision. The prediction process, 4E Cognitive, Eye Action/Activity, Physical Indicators and History in fine readings need to be mutually binding; long text, cross-language reproduction and negative results are key indicators. (Jacobs, 2015; Kukkonen, 2020)

Gender, Cool and Transgender Research is a re-enactment of identity towards institutional, labour, body and time. The “rehabilitation” itself has been the subject of controversy, as has the history of the trans malayjustment, care networks, incarceration and the trans-gender Internet. (Butler, 2006; Malatino, 2020; Awkward-Rich, 2022; Dame-Griff, 2023)

Memory research has shifted from community ownership to cross-media, intergenerational, ecological and related relationships. The subjects included both memory recipients, beneficiaries, platforms, algorithms and urban/environmental carriers. (Hirsch, 2012; Rothberg, 2009; Rothberg, 2019)

Medical humanities and voice research together challenge the “transparent subject”. Patient stories, hearing experiences, handicapped authors and listening politics show that narratives and voices are always shaped by systems, technologies and accessibility. (Charron, 2006; Woods, 2011; Stern, 2022; Robinson, 2020)

Scope of reporting and statement of responsibility

This report is a cutting-edge overview of research and methodological manual for higher education literary researchers and graduate students, and is not an exhaustive systematic overview and is not a substitute for reading original literature, original language and local language studies. The pool consists of peer-reviewed papers, academic monographs, official research projects, data sets, university/scientific pages and a small 2026 agenda signal; different sources assume different validation functions. Information on dynamic changes is based on the date of the deadline for information.
Part I. General remarks
Chapter 1 Model shift: a five-fold shift in global literature research in the 2020s
1.1 What is "latest" "hot" and "front"

“The latest” is the concept of time, “the hottest” is the concept of attention, “frontier” is the judgement of the production capacity of the problem, and the three cannot be interchangeable. A 2026 paper may be just a new case of the old framework; a 1997 monograph may also be a front-line resource in the 2020s, as global gender theory is re-examined. To avoid treating publication dates as theoretical innovations, the report breaks down each candidate ' s four categories:

rows: Is there an explanation mechanism previously lacking?
Original text proposed + at least one subsequent adoption/disputing signal
Why is the classic concept being re-enacted under new conditions?
Cool timing, panthematic materialism, decolonizing language politics.
Source text + Reinterpretation of new objects/methods in the 2020s
Has the method changed the question that can be asked when it enters literary research?
LLM Memory Audit, Text Re-entry Network, Eye Action and Long Text Reading
Method description + Error/Empirity Boundary + literary interpretation back
Are periodicals, conferences, funding and platforms being reorganized to study ecology?
Multilingual material infrastructure, emergency digital humanities, critical medical humanities
At least two types of institutional signals; meetings may not be shown to be mature alone degrees

blocks.text: The hotspots were judged to be five dimensions: recent peer review publication and links to topics, research centres/projects/data infrastructure, methodological innovations, cross-regional proliferation, and public issues. The internal code for each 0-2 is used only to identify the relative shapes in the evidence pool of this report, which cannot be added across regions or extrapolated from the “world ranking”. The ongoing meetings, conferences and visits in 2026 are only going to the "front radar"; the final complete trend year is 2025.

The “five-fold shift” below is an explanatory composite proposition of cross-regional evidence, not a statistical fact obtained mechanically by the five indicators, nor does it claim that all countries, languages and schools are moving in tandem. The minimum conditions for their establishment are: reciprocal change of object, reorganization of methods or dispute of responsibility in at least two regions and two types of evidence; and the retention of the reverse and uncovered areas by the regional chapters and chapter 24. The role of the framework is thus to compare the organization with the presentation of rebuttable questions, rather than to declare that the uniform paradigm has been completed.

This distinction is particularly important as the metadata platform systematically rewards English, DOI, open abstract and large institutions. Open Alex is an open and recapable academic index, but its coverage and conceptual recognition are still influenced by sources and languages; the trend map that follows in this report is based on an answer to “How much is a concept label in English visible in the title/summary” and not “how many studies are there in the world”. (Priem et al. 2022)
1.2 Quints turned.
Figure 1 Five-fold shift from text, theory, figures, human subjects and interpretation activities
1.2.1 From stable text to portfolio of evidence

Traditional literature research has never studied isolated texts, of course, but digitization, platformization and multimedia objects make the “text boundary” more difficult to assume. A web-based novel study may require both versions, comments, referral mechanisms, audioization, IP adaptation and platform work; an AI novel case contains at least training in ignorance, models and dates, tips, candidate outputs, manual editing, signature and reader acceptance; and a digital original work also includes code, plugin, hardware, interactive trajectory and history of lapse. At this point, the subject is more accurately a collection of evidence (evidence assemblage) linked to the system.

This shift does not amount to a disappearance of literature. Instead, it requires the interpreter to indicate more precisely which layer of material supports which level of conclusion. The frequency of words can prove differences in distribution and cannot be directly justified; the Platform ' s report can describe industry ecology and cannot independently prove causes and consequences; brain and behavioural indicators can detect partial attention or emotional mechanisms and cannot rule on historical significance; and a missing list of archives may also prove how the preservation system shapes the classics. The challenge to the computational literature “large-scale evidence” suggests that the amount of data is not a substitute for effective research design and rebuttable reasoning. (Underwood, 2019; Da, 2019; Bode, 2023)
1.2.2 From Theory Centre - Local Application to Distributive Concept Production

The theoretical globalization of the end of the twentieth century has often been described as the spread of the concepts of France, Germany, the United States and the United States to other regions. More interpretative maps for the 2020s should document where concepts are presented, translated, rewrited and institutionalized. Africa's “water for water” is not a region where Anglo-American ecological criticism is applied to South Africa; it is a region where literature is rewritten by ports, customs, water pipelines, sea distress and water privatization. Latin America pluriverse is not synonymous with post-colonial theory; they set the method of autonomy, extraction zones and the world of relationships. Dalit testimonio, a South Asian co-author, rewritten the testimony and co-authors in the position of caste-based knowledge, while Hong Kong unruly comparison made non-conformity the starting point for comparison.

“Striking” does not mean that each concept is completely indigenous. The concept changes when it moves into a new context through translation, exile, publishing, courses, conferences and social movements; the key is to rebuild its circulation chain rather than to divide theory into purely “original” and “impact”. Multilingual digital humanities studies further remind that the global division of knowledge still often places Southern institutions in data-processing positions and leaves the theoretical signature to the North Centre; hence, author contributions, financial flows, servers and long-term governance are also theoretical. (Fiormonte et al. 2022)
1.2.3 From the introduction of digital tools to infrastructure criticism

The early public image of digital humanities is often reduced to “reading many books with computers”. The front line projects place greater emphasis on how language is formed, how standards are negotiated, how tools are adapted, who maintains and who is excluded. Underwood argues for the value of reading from a distant perspective by testing the history of literature; Bode points out that digital databases select and convert the literature to a reproducing object through a bibliographical search. (Underwood, 2019; Bode, 2023) Data feminism has integrated classification, visualization, labour and power maintenance throughout the project cycle; multilingual digital criticism requires the inclusion of servers, electricity, language resources, colonial catalogues and intellectual sovereignty. (D'Ignazio et al., 2020; Fiormonte et al., 2022)

Infrastructure criticism is not about refusing to establish databases, but about turning “how the database claims to represent the world” into a question that can be examined. A reliable project should be made public at least: the range and absence of language, version and approval, text conversion, language error, code/model version, contributing role, maintenance plan, sensitive data rules and exit mechanism. If the copyrighted text cannot be made available to the project, the features, data dictionary, code and tectonic processes should also be opened; “not open” may be an ethical choice but cannot be an excuse for non-transparent methods.
1.2.4 From abstract human subjects to more than human beings, health and dependency

Environmental, post-human, medical and disability studies are forming a common chain of questions: how do literary subjects rely on water, energy, micro-organisms, animals, plants, medicines, sensors, assistive technologies, caregivers and institutions? Chakrabarty brought the scale of the conflict between climate and capital history into the human literature; Yusuf criticized the abstract “human world” from the point of view of geology, slavery and ethnicization; DeLoughey linked islands, oceans, militarization and colonial catastrophes; and Liboiron put the anti-colonial approach of pollution research into research relationships and laboratory practice. (Chakrabarty, 2021; Yusoff, 2018; DeLoughey, 2019; Liboiron, 2021)

Similar dependent perspectives change health narratives. Narrative medicine emphasizes careful reading and listening, but critical medical humanities refuse to use humanities only as a tool to improve the mutual relationship of doctors; research on disability and neurodiversity further asks what “normal” authors and credible patients are required by the medical, publishing and academic rhythms. Thus, health, ecology and technology are not three separate topics, but rather research into how the body is made up of different entry points by the environment and institutions.
1.2.5 From interpretation of sovereignty to accountable knowledge production

Literary interpretation is not value neutral, but “taking a stand” does not automatically amount to reliable methods. When research involves Aboriginal knowledge, trauma testimony, war archives, medical experience, cross-gender groups or platform users, public and visible work can lead to re-identification, extraction, surveillance or emotional work. The “denial” in colonial research reminds us that the subject and the community have the right not to be fully translated into data; indigenous research methods require research relationships, feedback and co-control to enter design. (Smith, 2021; Tuck et al, 2012)

Accountability does not mean that all projects must have the same ethical template. It requires that decisions be recorded: who decides who can access the material, who can translate/mark/maintain, who can explain the difference, who can withdraw, who has the derivatives and what information is delayed. The generation AI also adds layers of responsibility for training data copyrights, author affiliation, platform fraud and energy work. The 2025 report of the American Copyright Agency clearly distinguished between the human role in determining the elements of expression and mere reminder; such legal judgements were not the end point of literary theory, but forced author research to establish an auditable generation chain. (U.S. Copyright Office, 2025)
1.3 The 11th theme is not 11 islands.
The 11 themes can be divided into three groups by the dominant level, but they are highly cross-cutting:

rows: How power, subjects, relationships and knowledge are organized
Environmental humanities, world literature/translation, memory/trauma, medical humanities, sound/media
What changes have occurred in the literary objects and their socio-material conditions?
Literature research on how to obtain, measure and validate evidence
All themes; in particular copyright, deviation and replicability

blocks.text: This classification is not a physical delimitation. For example, AI and literature are both objects of research and tools and post-human theoretical issues; sound research analyses both the hearing media and develops methods of listening as an ethical approach; and world literature is both a history of object circulation and a theoretical debate at a comparative scale. The function of the hierarchy is to prevent the “theme, theory, methodology” from being mixed into a list of hot words.
1.4 Theoretical spectrometry for the 2020s: continuity, modification and cross-cutting
Figure 2 Theorem spectroscopy from post-colonial, gender, memory, cognitive and digital humanities to the 2020s Yes

The timeline shows that many of the so-called “new shifts” have a long stream. Butler's discussion of gender performance, Hirsch's post-rememberance, Rothberg's multi-way memory, Damrosch's framework of world literature, Charon's narrative medicine, Sedgwick's paranoid/reparative reading, Jacobs' neurobiology are all the language of today's debate. The changes in the 2020s were not to eliminate these sources but to modify them in three ways:
Targeting amendments: Extension of printed text to platforms, models, voices, clinical and environmental infrastructure.

Geo-revision: expansion of theoretical production from the Euro-American Centre to Latin America, Africa, Asia, the Middle East and Central and Eastern Europe, with records translated and untranslated.
Methodological amendments: Require conceptual and negative nuclear materials, data spectrum and ethical decision-making.

Theoremic history should therefore not be written as linear “activismal change”, but rather as a regrouping of problems. For example, emotional theory and gender research intersect with “who is asked to express identifiable emotions”; memory and environmental humanities intersect with “how a human being preserves a disaster”; world literature and digital humanities intersect with “what is comparable to what is translated into databases and machines”; and cognitive research intersect with colonization “whether the universal brain model erases linguistic and cultural differences”.
1.5 Global common issues and non-conformity

Cross-regional research needs to avoid both mistakes. The first is universalism: defining the front line of the world with the concept of English-American journals, database coverage or experimental material. The second is local fundamentalism: each region is written as a closed, pure and self-contained tradition. Better use of “shared issues—mechanisms decompose—local names” is three steps: first, identifying common issues, such as language repression, data disaggregation, platform concentration, environmental extraction; then decompose specific mechanisms; and finally, retaining local terminology, history and incompletely legible.

For example, Latin America's decolonization and the centralization of the empires of Central and Eastern Europe discuss sovereignty, language and intellectual hierarchy, but their colonial forms, formation of States, settlement and legal isolation; the multilingual digital archives of Africa and the Japanese national ancient AI deal with low-resource scripts but have completely different infrastructure sovereignty; and narrative medicine emphasizes systems and co-production in North America/Europe, and may have to read in Latin America alongside pollution, Territory and First Nations health. Comparability is not a priori, but a result negotiated by the research team and allowed for failure.
Chapter 2 Methodological maps: from detailed reading to computation, from interpretation to empirical evidence
2.1 Method is not a tool menu, but a doctrine-evidence contract

The primary question of methodology is not “what software is used”, but “what is the basis for this conclusion from these materials”. The methodology is understood in the present report as a contract between the claim and the evidence. Any study should answer four questions: What is the research unit? How does the material get into the sample? How can the analytical steps change the material? What result would overturn the explanation?
Figure 3 Materials-scale-validation relationships for reading, archiving, computing, experimentation, participatory and media methods

rows: How narratives, rhetoric, rhythms, voices and ambiguities work
Clear version, cross-reference, language and language skills
Regional total frequency or causal effect
How concepts, classics, authors and publishing systems are formed
Name, missing and file power statement
Complete experience of groups outside the archives
How the form and concept are changed across languages
Original language/translation, book reviews, courses, publishing chain
Uncomparable logs, translation spectrum, multilingual teams
How patterns, anomalies and relationships are distributed at a larger scale
Language spectrum, language validation, parameter scanning, code version
Historical significance and value of the relevant model
How models are generated, classified or memory are involved in literary processes
Model/date, repeated call, contamination probe, non-LLM baseline
Partial mechanisms for attention, anticipation, emotion and aesthetic response
Pre-registration, sample/efficiency, ecological materials, individual differences
The history of literature and the holistic interpretation of cultural power
How experiences and systems are shared
A single voice representing all members
How technology, sound, interface and failure make up a work
Operating environment, version, interactive video, loss list
The media decision theory, which is a departure from history.

blocks.text: 2.1.1 Detailed intellectuals: demonstrated skills, not mystical intuition or fixed algorithms

John Guillory defines reading in detail (*On Close Reaging*) as “cultural technology”: researchers can demonstrate how to take note of words, phrases, quotations, formal relationships and context, or can train such attention, but they cannot exhaust judgement as a set of mechanical rules; Scott Newstok, the author's public signature, prepares an annotated bibliography for the book, rather than co-authorship. The criticism was further reduced to verifiable writing: accuracy of quotations, fairness of interpretation, a summary of circumstances, selectivity or implausibility, body judgement supported by text details, and genuine handling of retrogression. (Kramnick, 2023) Gillory ' s Collection of Criticism: Organization of Literary Studies (*Prosessing Criticism: Essays on the Organization of Legal Studies*) also reminds that the authority of the methodology is shaped by faculties, curricula, professional labour and public writing systems. (Guilloly, 2022)

This triangle amends the pseudo-relationship of “detailed-calculation”: reading provides a local judgement that can be argued publicly, and computing provides a measure, distribution and anomaly, both of which must demonstrate a chain of reasoning from material to claim. They cannot introduce “detailed reading as the only way” or miswritten literary judgements as a complete replica of the natural sciences; the more appropriate criteria are availability of the version, availability of evidence, exemplifying steps, retrogression, reasonable peer review.
2.2 Eight-step research stream
Figure 4 From problem definition, material acquisition and ethical governance to counter-proofing, replicability and community feedback
Step 1: Issues and position description

(c) Identify the target, the scale, the language, the time and the location of the researcher. The study of “Asian literature” must describe how Asia is defined; the study of Russian-language works cannot automatically be classified as Russian literature; and the study of indigenous materials should describe the relationship between the researcher and the community. The question should be answered by evidence, rather than packaging a grand value judgement into a search mission.
Step 2: Material acquisition and data spectrum Yes

Record databases, collections, projects, search terms, dates, permits and screening rules. Digital text should preserve version/reading information; web pages and platform information should record access dates and possible failures; machine-generated materials should preserve models, parameters, tips, output selection and manual editing. The data spectrum is not an appendix decoration, but a basis for interpretation deviations.
Step 3: Ethics and governance

Public disclosure does not automatically mean that it can be used arbitrarily. The CARE principles emphasize collective benefit, control, responsibility and ethics, which complement, inter alia, the searchable, accessible, interoperable and reusable of FAIR. For indigenous people, medical, trauma and war-time materials, sensitivity classification, withdrawal, ambiguity, delayed opening and local copies should be included in the design. The objective of research ethics is to make power decisions visible, rather than replacing consultations with a list.
Step 4: Multiscale analysis

Mixing under the cluster approach, rather than for the “mixed approach”. The anomalies identified in the calculations require careful reading; the proposed form mechanism can be validated by version/text; the experimental indicator requires historical interpretation; and the differences in participatory research itself can be the result. At least two independent pieces of evidence do not mean two softwares, but rather two lines of reasoning that do not share the same source of deviation.
Step 5: Interpretation triangulation

(b) Between the “text/materials-model/code-historic/systems” triangle. If the word frequency model disappears after changing the syllables, text or parameters, the intensity of the conclusion should be reduced; if the participant explains the conflict with the researchers ' classification, the conflict should be recorded rather than eliminated at an average consistent rate; and if the theoretical reading only explains the supporting case, the initiative should be to find a reverse example.
Step 6: Counter-proofing and uncertainty

Literary research need not be disguised as natural sciences, but it can be clear what evidence will change judgement. Negative results, alternative explanations, missing years, low resource language errors, copyright blind areas and inconsistent labels. The “evidence” “indication” “agenda signals” is used to distinguish between different intensity and avoid unfounded absolute words such as “evidence of the “first time” “sole”.
Step 7: Recapable expression

Recurrence is not the same as giving anyone exactly the same interpretation, but the ability of others to rebuild material selection and analysis steps. Minimum requirements include search formats, dates, sub-standards, code/tool versions, data dictionarys, parameters, tabular footnotes and reasons for not being available. For LLM, temperature and closed models do not guarantee a verbatim recurrence, and therefore multiple output and decision logs should be kept.
Step 8: Return to Community and Long-term Maintenance

Rendering the results to language experts, file keepers, participants or authors; correcting and withdrawing records; planning domain names, servers, format migrations and software dependency. Without maintenance responsibilities, short-term “opening” could create new losses. Co-authors and contributing roles should reflect language presentations, translations, labelling, codes, project management and public participation in labour.
2.3 Minimum reporting norms for studies of computing literature

The controversy in computing literature is often simplified to mean “advance or oppose reading”. More crucial is effectiveness. Da criticized the study of computing literature, warning that complex models may mask vague concepts, data leakage and unexplained classifications; rather than withdrawing from calculations, the response is to place literary issues, text design and misdiagnostic diagnosis before the model. (Da, 2019) The following minimum norms are recommended:
Language material: Clarity total, sampling, copyright, language, version, quality of OCR, age and gender/geographical deficit.
Features: An explanation of why it is literary and does not replace the theory with model convenience.
Validation: Language baseline, set-up, parameter sensitivity, manual blinding, abnormal value back.
Explanation: Distinguishing related, classification performance, mechanisms and historical causes and consequences; prohibition of direct naming of genres by a cluster.
Revert: Codes, environment, random seeds, data dictionary, unopenable parts and alternative materials.
Team: Open language presentation, language expertise, role as contributor to labelling and maintenance.

Hicke, in collaboration with Mimno, the Living with Machines collection of literature translations in non-English, and the practice of “programmable language” suggest that the logical sequence of cross-language studies is usually “internal validation of the — minimal public metadata — cross-language comparisons — back to the interpretation of the publication history” rather than machine translation into English before uniform classification. (Hicke et al., 2025; Beelen et al., 2025; Koeser et al., 2025)
2.4 Six audits of LLM literary research
Training in pollution auditing: Is it possible that the model has seen a work? Scattered tests of name masking, disturbance, public/copying/low-visibility text.
Version audit: Record provider, model name, date of call, system hint, parameters and interface; model update may change conclusions.
Baseline audit: Compared with simple word bags, traditional taxonomy, known language models from open sources and manual judgement.
Repeated audits: the same reminder is called repeatedly; reports are distributed and failed, and the most beautiful output is not selected.
Author chain audit: Distinguishing data authors, model developers, prompters, candidates, manual editing, publishing and marketing.
Rights and Eco-Assessment: A description of the known boundaries of copyright, privacy, culturally sensitive materials, computing/energy and invisible labour.

The literary benchmarking study has shown that the performance of large models in narrative understanding, style and cultural knowledge is influenced by text, title design and pollution; the high scores between different benchmarks cannot automatically be translated into “deep reading capability”. Sowon Park's discussion of AI and literature suggests that literature can be a place for testing meaning, emotional and ethical complexities, but it must be prevented from being used to avoid verifiable standards. (Park, 2026)
2.5 Cognitive, experimental and neurobehaviour: triple, not resurgent

Cognitive literature can raise testable questions about attention, prediction, body simulation, emotional and aesthetic judgement. Jacobs' neurocognitive poetry, Kukkonen's predictive processing narrative studies and empirical literature studies represent three complementary pathways. (Jacobs, 2015; Kukkonen, 2020; Hugentobler et al., 2021; Corcoran et al., 2023) High-quality design requires the following three links:
Modeling layers: Pre-state irritant characteristics, assumptions and indicators, rather than ex post-brain-area stories to explain everything.
Empirical level: keep open memory, reader differences, rereading and long text processes, without considering average reactions as the only reader.
Historical: The meaning, the literature, the education, the language and the political discourse determine how stimulation is understood.

Eye, reaction or neurometric indicators are valuable to local mechanisms, but cannot answer why a work enters the classics, how a metaphor is involved in colonial history or why a certain emotion is being subjected to institutional requirements. Intercultural experiments should prioritize the development of local language materials and language inefficiency rather than treating translations as a no-lost incentive. (Chen et al., 2022; Giovanni et al., 2026)
2.6 Trends: What can be said, what cannot be said
Figure 5 Number of annual results retrieved by the precise phrase of title/summary; individual maps using a stand-alone axis

The trend map uses Open Alex Works API to retrieve 11 English-language conceptual labels by title/absorption precise phrases in 2010-2025. Each small map uses a separate axis to avoid a quantitative variation that obscures a smaller direction; the 2022 dotted line only marks the significant point of time for the generation of AI public proliferation, without implying that all trends are causal. 2025 is the final full year, but may still be affected by the index lag.

blocks.text: Data can only serve as a macro signal “are they worthy of further study” and cannot be used to judge theoretical excellence, national production or reference effects. The most robust use is cross-cutting with journal topics, societies, research centres and representative outcomes; any query should be linked to a list of public words, dates and interfaces and allow readers to recalculate by an alias.
2.7 Quality Levels and Reporting Languages
The report divides evidence into three types of functions, without placing humanities machines in a single evidence pyramid:
Evidence of results: Peer-reviewed papers, academic monographs, traceable data sets to underpin theory, methodology and specific research findings.
Ecological evidence: pages of universities, research centres, societies, projects and official courses to show who is organizing research, training on what methods and building of infrastructure.
Evidence of the agenda: meetings, callouts, recruitments, funding advisories, used to identify possible directions; they must be marked as premature.

The corresponding language strength is divided into “substantiated/substantiated” “expanding” “credible nodes” “inventory evidence” for the current round. “The evidence for this round” cannot be written as “no research in this area”. When the source is only a summary or summary of the publisher, the report avoids reading the chapter in detail in lieu of the original author; when the source is self-scoping of the project, the figures describe the project and do not extrapolate the impact or effect.
Part II. Thematic front

The title provides a summary of theoretical sources, 2020 increments, methodological tool chains, representation of scholars and cases, and clear boundaries of debate and evidence at the end of each chapter, organized by the 11 themes. The order of chapters is not the heat ranking; the issues of numbers, decolonization, environment, gender, cognitive, memory, health and media are highly intersecting in practical research.
Chapter 3 Digital humanities and computational literature studies
3.1 Sources and 2020s focus

The spectrum can be summarized as: digital editing/speech library and bibliographic — dilist reading — and hysteria — computational literary history and cultural analysis — critical data research/missing research — interpretation of LLM and multi-modular computational humanities. Underwood represents the mature stage of “the presentation of literary history propositions with digital evidence”; Da questions the statistical and interpretative effectiveness of several computational literary arguments, while Bode points out that the controversy cannot be dissociated from the issue of linguistic construction and literary history (Underwood, 2019; Da, 2019; Bode, 2023). The real new changes are not larger algorithms, but rather the primary question of why the language exists, what is not digitized, how the date is uncertain, whether the model remembers the original language (Hicke et al., 2025; Beelen et al., 2025; Koeser et al., 2025).

Globalization has also shifted from “transplanting English tools to the world” to multilingual OCR, local knowledge mapping, indigenous data governance, infrastructure and inequality in the South. Global Data in the Digital Humanities put African, Latin American, South Asian, China and Russian connections in the Centre for Theory; Data Feminism requires auditing data projects from power, binary classification, missing data, labour and co-design (Fiormonte et al., 2022; D'Ignazio et al., 2020).
3.2 New theories and arguments

Language is not neutral: the digital collection is a product of choice, copyright, commercial cooperation, preservation and OCR performance. Beelen and others presented the Environmental Scan in the British press archives and opposed the Panorama (Beelen et al., 2025) by “oligoptic data”.

How the results of the model become literary evidence: high classification accuracy only supports the scoring of characteristics, and does not automatically indicate their literary historical significance. Qualified papers are to report baselines, time/author confusion, data leakage, category imbalances, text errors and alternative theories (Da, 2019; Bode, 2023; Hicke, etc., 2025).

Uncertainty is not about cleaning noise: Undate retains uncertainty about human time in spatial, multi-chronological and incomplete dates, and demonstrates “informing theoretical judgement into data structures and publicly describing” (Koeser et al., 2025).

Interpretable and memory pollution: LLM may learn both style and popular text. The hint source identification, training, text disturbance and internal detection must be combined, and the black box accuracy cannot be called “understanding” (Hicke et al., 2025).
3.3 Methodological tool chain
Language protocol: Time, language, type, sub-band, copyright, expected aggregate and digital coverage; source and version reserved for each text.

Collection and quality inspection: OCR/HTR sample-by-layer character error rate, TEI/XML or structured JSON retention chapter, page, page number; undetermined date use interval rather than fictional single point (Koeser et al., 2025).

Exploration and modelling: The word frequency/key is the starting point, can be measured in a stylistic manner, subject/input, supervised classification, person network, geographic information and serial model; simple baseline and time-specification is required.
Validation: Cross-certification divided by work/author rather than by random sentence; label replacement, digestion, artificial blind core, sensitivity analysis and negative cases.
Returning to literature: extracting text from model defects and anomalies, reading their rhetoric, style and publishing context; translating “statistical differences” into rebuttable literary titles.
Revert: Jupyter/R Markdown, environmental lock-up, random seeds, data dictionary, model cards, version codes; features published when copyrighted, ID and reconstruction scripts.
3.4 Representational outcomes, nodes and cases

blocks.text: Application issues: Long-term changes in body structure, proliferation of types, translation networks, person relations, periodical ecology, spatial narratives, variations in versions. Not applicable/high risk: language is not known in general but claims to be “representative of all literature”; very small language uses high-parametric models; no cross-language comparison by language specialists; protected text is reissued. The "Calculating Discovery - Archiving - Detailed Interpretation" triangle is preferred.
3.5 Theoretical mechanism in-depth description: the model is not a conclusion, but a binding observation position

Conceptual distinction. “distant reading” refers to observing distribution, variation and anomalies on a scale that goes beyond the read-through capability of materials; “computational literature studies” further uses classification, measurement, network or semantic models for literary issues; and “critical digital humanities” asks why text materials exist, who is digitalized and who sets classification criteria. The three cannot be compressed into "reading in software." Core mechanisms. Ted Underwood, in *Distant Horizons: Digital History and Literary Change*, interprets the model as a “view device”: researchers first turn literary history questions into observable features, then examine them in different language, parameters and time-script patterns, and finally return to the missing interpretation of the work, publication history and archives. The value of the model is not to pronounce meaning on behalf of others, but to expose a certain historical intuition to the opposite. Katherine Bode's “What's the Matter with Computational Literary Studies?” also incorporated language construction itself into the case: the database is not a transparent container of literature in general, but a subject of co-production of bibliographies, preservation systems and text processing. Most critical. Nan Z. Da, in “The Computation Case against Computational Limitedes”, noted that complex statistics may mask conceptual confusion, data leakage, miscalculation or “proven common sense by expensive methods”. This criticism has hit not the calculation itself, but an unspecified leap between model performance and literary interpretation. The border applies. Clusters do not automatically name genres, word frequency differences do not provide a separate proof of ideology, nor do valid features of English language material be used directly across languages. Microcases. Rebecca M. M. Hicke and David Mimno, in “Looking for the Inner Music: Probing LLMs' Understanding of Literary Steel”, combined traditional baselines, language models, memory probes and disturbance experiments, show that the key to literary style research has shifted from “moderations are accurate” to “it is based on what, whether it has seen the original language, and under what conditions the results have failed”.
Chapter 4 AI and Literature: Generating, collaborating and critical AI research
4.1 Sources and frontier

The creation of literature did not start with ChatGPT. The computer generated text files of 1953-2023 show long-term coexistence of rules, randomness, combinations, interaction and machine learning (Bettram et al., 2024). The key theoretical changes in the 2010s were the shift from “magnetic reading” to a collective composition of people, models, interfaces and systems (Hayles, 2018). After 2022, the front line was centred on four lines: generating narrative quality, author support and creative processes, LLM as a literary analytical/evaluation tool, copyright and cultural sovereignty governance (Tian et al., 2024; Chhun et al., 2024; Teleki et al., 2025; U.S. Copyright Office, 2025; UNESCO Expert Group on Artificial Intelligence and Culture, 2025).
4.2 New theory and main controversy

The author ' s identity has changed from duality to a contribution spectrum: pure human, pure machine, mixed only with a rough label; and more importantly, who controls the conception, hint, selection, translation, rewrite, sorting and publication. The United States Copyright Agency considers that the ancillary use of AI under current United States law does not prevent the original parts of humanity from being protected, but pure AI material or material under which humans have inadequate control over the expression element is not protected; mere provision of tips is usually insufficient (U.S. Copyright Office, 2025). This is evidence of United States policy and should not be extrapolated to a globally uniform law.

Generating abilities and narrative understandings cannot be confused: Tian, for example, uses storytelling, turning points, awakening and value-for-money comparison of human stories, and finds that LLM stories in tests are more homogenous in direction, suspense and structural diversity; and visible integration feature can significantly improve indicators (Tian et al., 2024). This indicates that the middle-level narrative is valid and does not prove that the machine has human experience.

The automatic evaluation cycle deviation: Chhun and others found in 1,056 stories and multiple tips that LLM ratings are better than old automatic indicators at the system level, but that they are difficult to provide sufficient and consistent interpretation; models may also prefer text that is closer to pre-trained distribution (Chhun and others, 2024). Therefore, homologue models alone cannot be used to generate and score.

Collaboration is not automatic: Teleki and others distinguish between two paradigms of " independent generation " and " author-assisted " (Teleki and others, 2025). The 41 blind reviews of the UCB 2024 contest show that four pure AI works are in the middle and the winners work together; some collaboration takes longer (Park, 2026). This is an exploratory case and cannot be declared a general superiority for mixed writing.

Cultural diversity and value chains: The UNESCO expert report places algorithm homogeneity, linguistic inequality, creator remuneration, cultural sovereignty and vulnerable heritage risks at the core of governance (UNESCO Expert Group on Artificial Intelligence and Culture, 2025). Literary AI research must report on the language of training, geographical gaps and environmental/labour costs.
4.3 Minimum credible tool chain
Freeze model name, version, provider, date, temperature, Top-p, system program, context and random torrent; save all hints-output-manual editing logs.

Establishment of a human baseline with at least one simple model baseline; continuation of known text, source identification and repeat of footage checks to assess contamination by author/work segregation training and testing (Hicke et al., 2025).

Quality evaluation includes at least: blind ratings, multi-reviewer consistency, specific dimensions metrics, ranking and reasons for openness; automatic indicators can only be supported. The generator and the evaluator use different model families and report location deviations and self-preferences (Chhun et al., 2024).

The narrative studies add mid-level labels: event, cause, effect, turn, focus, target, suspense, emotional trajectory; and which level of improvement is the result of profiling validation (Tian et al., 2024).
The retention of the creative research versions differs from AI recommendations, author adoption, refusal to rewriting; the cognitive labour and power relations of interview writing authors.
Ethical list: training/output copyright, private text, style imitation, injury to active authors, cultural exclusiveness, retention of model supplier data, revocability and disclosure.

blocks.text: Areas of application: Generating literary history, computational narratives, translation collaboration, writing processes, literature education, author rights and platform governance. Primary limitations: The non-recurrence of model updates; the lack of transparency in training packages; the bias of English/high resource languages; the drift of evaluation concepts; and rapid policy change. The findings must be modelled and dated, preferably with outputs and tips as auditable appendices.
4.5 Theoretical institutional insulation: Authorism should be rewritten as a traceable contribution chain

Conceptual distinction. The AI literature research consists of at least three objects: modeling as a label, translation or comparison tool; modeling as a research subject for the training language deviation and cultural orthodox; and modeling as a participatory device in the creative process. Combining the three into “can the machine write” would obscure a truly researchable mechanism. Core mechanisms. In “Human and Machine Cultures of Reaing: A Cognitive-Assemblage Approach”, Katherine Hayles proposed “cognitive assembly” (cognition assemblage): Production of meaning is distributed among people, interfaces, algorithms, systems and the environment. Thus, the creation of the work is not the expression of a single subject, but rather the result of the continuous role of training materials, model structures, system tips, tips, candidates, manual editing, signature, publication and marketing. The author's analysis should record who made the expressive decision at each point, rather than simply “person” or “AI writing”. Lillian-Yvonne Bertram and Nick Montfort, editor-in-chief *Output: An Anthony of Computer-Generated Text, 1953-2023* also put writing back to 70 years of procedural literature, avoiding ChatGPT as a false zero. Most critical. First, humanized languages misrepresent the production of statistics as meaning, feeling or understanding; second, popular literature may have entered training materials and model performance is high by memory; and third, automatic indicator or model judges can easily recognize their own preference cycle as literary value. The border applies. The experimental conclusions must bind model names, call dates, tips, repetitions, non-linguistic model baselines and manual judgements; nor can the United States copyright rules be extrapolated to a global unified answer. Microcases. Sowon Park's “Creative Writer and AI: A 2024 Case Study” collaborates with evaluation materials in a specific teaching and evaluation process, and the most valuable point is not to rank AI's work, but to separate “concepts, generation, screening, rewrite and evaluation”, thereby turning the abstract author's competition into an auditable creative process.
4.5.1 Four-tier history of machine writing: combination devices, software, models and responsibilities

Dennis Yi Tenen, The Theory of Robotics: How Computers Learn for Writers: How Computers Learned to Writer*, explains that “mechanical writing” does not begin suddenly before templates, rhetorical combinations, formalism and early calculations are brought to the production model. (Tenen, 2024) Matthew G. Kirschenbaum, Official Academic Signature, Revising Signs: A Literary History of Word Processing*, incorporates document formats, software interfaces, revised trajectories and digital author files into the literary history, thereby evidencing that writing tools change the preservation, visible and attributable creation process, but cannot be attributed to a software alone. (Kirschenbaum, 2016) Rossi Braidotti's Post-Humans (* The Posthuman*) also requires a distinction between three often confused routes: post-humanism critical of unity, autonomous “humans”, post-anthropocentricism that transcends species-planet relations, and super-humanism that pursues technological enhancement. (Bradotti, 2013)

Thus, AI literature research should at least be accompanied by a reconstruction of the history of intellectual devices, history of writing software, history of modelling infrastructure and contribution - history of responsibility; only by entering these four layers will the “new and novel text” not be able to distort history into a technical breakdown.
Chapter 5 Environment: ecological criticism, human life and blue/energy humanities
5.1 Theory Spectrograph Yes

There are four possible extensions: writing of nature and the ecological criticism of the wilderness Environmental justice/post-colonial ecological criticism Humans and multi-species/materials shift to planetary nature, geo-racialization, the humanities of blue and energy, empirical ecological criticism. Chakrabarty distinguishes between the global, centred human system, and the planetary, which decentrizes human beings, and requires a parallel reflection on climate universality and historical inequality (Chakrabarty, 2021). DeLoughey explains in Caribbean and Pacific island text/visual culture that the human world cannot be described from continental centres, abstract global scales (DeLoughey, 2019).

Two of Yusoff's books place geology, slavery, ethnicization and resources in the same file, first critical of “one human world” as a way to level the black history and then track how white geology shapes the world through measurements, classifications and pricing (Yusoff, 2018; Yusoff, 2024). Liboiron goes further by defining pollution as a colonial land relationship requiring consent, responsibility and non-etilation in the methodology (Liboiron, 2021). This spectrum means that ecological criticism no longer analyses only “natural images”, but analyses infrastructure, extraction, debris, oceans, toxicity, sensors and data politics.
5.2 New areas and disputes in the 2020s

Planetary vs Global: Planetary scale avoids national centres, but may also level off responsibilities; research needs to map carbon capital, imperial history, class/race and species scales simultaneously (Chakrabarty, 2021).
Geology Humanities and Grail Geology: Rocks, minerals, mapping and classification are cultural-political archives, not literary backgrounds (Yusoff, 2018; Yusoff, 2024).

Blue/island/hydrography: the ocean is not a blank passage, nor is the island a climate fable; water should be read from shipping, nuclear testing, sea level, slave trade and islander knowledge (DeLoughrey, 2019).

Experienced ecological criticism (ecocricism): linking environmental narrative analysis to questionnaires, experiments, interviews, testing of whether and how narratives change perceptions, emotions and actions (Schneider-Mayerson et al., 2023). The point is whether short-term attitude tables represent long-term political behaviour.

Citizen perception and environmental data: Gabriels examines how community sensory toolkits generate a “civil world”, asking questions about sensor standards, visibility and governance rights (Gabrys, 2022).
Archives and Extinction: Cohen/Yates reveals in “Ark-Archives” what preservation necessarily chooses to enter the future and what is abandoned (Cohen et al., 2023).
5.3 Methodological tool chains and cases

Multiscale text analysis: fine reading of rhetoric/tact, with production of time lines for extraction, shipping, disasters, migration or pollution; and matching narrative time with geological/policy time.
Environmental archives: corporate reports, maps, colonial mapping, meteorological records, oral history, literature and visual materials; records of the file silence and who manufactures the data.
Space/sensor approach: GIS and sensor data must be combined with community knowledge, calibration error and data governance, and maps cannot replace land experience (Gabrys, 2022).

Empirical ecological criticism: pre-registration stimulus, random conditions, baseline attitudes, instant/delayed measurements and open reactions; distinguishing narrative transport, emotions from action, avoiding “like novels = taking climate action” (Schneider-Mayerson, etc., 2023).
Non-retracted fields: common definitional issues, dynamic consent, return outcomes, open level of data for community decisions on sensitive locations (Liboisron, 2021).
