blocks.text: Figure 6 Text to public consequences: complete chain of responsibility for literary research
X.2 Publication, platform and literary work: value is not added after publication Objects

Simone Murray, in Digital Literature Fields (* The Digital Library: Reening, Writer, and Selling Books in the Internet Era*, 2018), placed author's online image, digital marketing, literary festival, web book review and reading discussions in the same domain; Sarah Brouillette, Literature and Creative Economy (*Literature and the Creative Economy*, 2014), explained how the image of author “free, flexible, self-expression” fits into unstable labour and cultural policies. Together, they change the research unit: the text type, length, cover, frequency of updates and even the pace of narratives of the work may be formed in conjunction with the structure of the platform ' s classification, contract, marketing and returns.

Melanie Walsh and Maria Antoniak, The Goodreads “Classics”: A Popular Standard of Readers, Amazon, and Crowdsourced Amateur Critical, 2021) analysed over 120,000 comments, showing that the “classic” of the user-playing exhibition also carries the platform's demographic structure and its orthodox bias. A replicable research design should combine the identity of the same work in the course catalogue, the publisher case, the Goodreads label, the star review and the recommended page, and consider the commentator's interpretation work as a platform-translable data rather than a natural and public “readers' voice”.
X.3 Native intellectual sovereignty: Access is not tantamount to arbitrary use

The CARE Principles for First Nations Data Governance (The CARE Principles for Indigenous Data Government, 2020), presented by fourteen authors, including Stephanie Russo Carroll, seek to link data rights to people, land and self-determination by simply pursuing the open logic of discovery, accessibility, interoperability and reuse, in collective interest, control authority, responsibility and ethical correction; and Tahu Kukutai and John Taylor, edited by the Chief Editor, First Nations Data Sovereignty: Towards an Agenda (*Indigenous Data Management: Toward an Agenda*, 2016). Theoretically, the shift has been from “university holding of materials for communities” to “community deciding what can be named, made public, commercialized, trained or withdrawn”.

The Local Communities and Mukurtu Content Management System provide operational examples of traditional knowledge labels and biocultural labels expressing seasonal, gender, etiquette, sacred and commercial conditions; and Mukurtu allows multiple community records and permissions from public to strictly restricted to the same object. Researchers can model the story in a set of oral stories from the general open storage and Mukurtu, comparing who named the author, who approved the publication, who decided on model training and commercial use. The conclusion cannot be that “unfully open” is a technical backwardness, as limitations themselves may be the realization of intellectual sovereignty.
X.4 Copyright, text and data mining: law is also an cognitive variable

Copyright and Artificial Intelligence, Part II: Copyright (Copyright and Artificial Intelligence, Part 2: Copyratitis, 2025) of the United States Copyright Authority states that artificial intelligence as a support tool does not automatically exclude overall protection, but that input of tips is usually insufficient to prove that users have decided on the protective expression in the output; Part III, Generating Artificial Intelligence, Part 3: Advance Text, 2025, does not provide a single answer to the phrase “training is ipso facto lawful” or “absorbing of course”.

The European Parliament (European Parliament) and the Council of the European Union Directive on Copyright and Related Rights in the Digital Single Market (EU) 2019/790) further show that the research text and data mining, legal access, secure copy retention and the readable machine of the right-holder have different conditions under different articles. A truly reversible computing literature project should establish “language jurisdiction passports” for each work: access, licences/contracts, research jurisdictions, retention of rights, copy retention, reviewers, publicly available derivatives and records of artificial intelligence contributions. The sample is thus shaped by legal conditions; the law is not a decorative description after the study has been completed.
X.5 Interactive narratives, games and cross-media: reading units from page to run

Astrid Ensslin's Literary Gaming*, 2014) links "Literary-Spectrograph System" to super-media novels, interactive novels, poetry games and author-style games; Emily Short notes in Narrative and Interactivity, 2024 that the form of interaction is being redefinition of the plot, author, reader and role, using tools such as Twine and cross-media practice. The procedural detail therefore cannot only extract visible text, but also records branch, state variable, failure path, randomity, interface and playback differences.

Will Luers, Hazel Smith and Roger Dean's digital novel *novelling* (2016) creates a narrative of reorganization every six minutes, every 30 seconds, in text, images and sound. The researchers run at least multiple-wheeled video screens, coded the sequence of the clips, sound, click points and narratives, and criticized the code/interface, the creator ' s description and traditional close readings of the cross-reference. The case reminds that the creation of literature does not start with a large-language model; algorithms and reader action have long made “works” an event that is run again.
X.6 Children and youth literature: age is also a structure of interpretation

Ebony Elizabeth Thomas, reading novels, video, fan novels and online communities in The Dark Fantastic: Race and the Imagination from Harry Potter to the Hunger Games*, 2019, points out that the issue of representation is not just the number of roles, but also the number of actors who can enter the fantasy world, gain a future, become a centre of reader recognition. The Clementine Beauvais, " Strong children " (* The Light Child: Time and Power in Children's Limited*, 2015), interprets the relationship between adult authors, editors, buyers and child implicit readers as a two-way limit on time and power.

The focus of the novel, post-video-based racial viewing, platform commentary and counter-posters by youth fans is centred on Rue in Susanne Collins' Hunger Game (* The Hunger Games*, 2008). The methodology should combine text/scenario reading, historical platform sampling and fan re-creation, while avoiding copying harassment statements or using the community of minors as a non-risk-free public language. This object can place the latter critical retrofitting, the Cool/Racial Study, platform acceptance in the same case as the education system.
X.7 Descriptions of relocation, borders and detention: borders are more than mere occurrences Land

The Lauterpacht Literation Guide (*The Roadstone to Migration Literation*, 2025), which was edited by Gigi Adair, Rebecca Fasselt and Carly McLaughlin, expanded the study from “immigrant writers” to “voluntary and forced relocation, asylum, detention, return, internal relocation and multilingual public use; Sandro Mezzadra and Brett Nelson's Border as a Method” (*Borderas Method, or the Multinational of Labor*, 2013) interpreted the boundary as a device for continuous production of differences in the classification of labour, legal identity, waiting time and knowledge. The literature study thus goes beyond the “cross-border theme” and analyses how documentation, interpretation, queues, detention and mobility organize narrative time and sound.

David Herd, in conjunction with Anna Pincus, editor-in-chief, Gatwick Detaines Welfare Group, Refugee Story (*Refuge Tales*, 2016), has made up an extended literary event of walking, live talk, anonymous/allegation, a collection of publishing and anti-detention initiatives. Research must also assess the power of the voice of the previous person and the voice of the well-known author, document translation, consent, withdrawal, remuneration and security agreements, and avoid converting testimony into a humanitarian aesthetic consumption.
X.8 Literature education and public humanities: the public is not the end user of the outcome

The generous reflection by Kathleen Fitzpatrick (*Generous Thinking: A Radical Approach to Living the University*, 2019) to re-establish the relationship between the University and the public through joint reading and public work; Daniel Fisher-Livne and Michelle May-Curry, editor of the Lawtledge Academic Guide to Public Humanities (* The Roaddge Companion to Public Humanities Scholarchip*, 2024), in turn, define the public language as a reciprocal research, teaching and project collaboration. The Community should not provide stories alone; the choice of issues, budgets, skills, signatures, copyrights and preservation proceeds needed to be redistributed.

The American Prison Writing Archiving, created by Doran Larson, is headed by Vesla Weaver and is carried by the Sheridan Librries, Johns Hopkins University Library. The file corrects external errors in the form of a first-person version of the prisoner ' s non-fiction, poetry and cultural criticism, and the entire re-editorial is subject to the author ' s consent. Research-type courses should record authors ' self-naming, tailoring, location, review marks and copyright conditions to conclude with open presentations, author-readable summaries and feedback mechanisms; not only did the student ' s interpretation go well, but also did the author ' s rights and public availability improve.
X.9 Open science and maintenance research: completion is not complete

Alan Liu, Urszula Pawlicka-Deger and James Smiths, editor-in-chief, Critical Infrastructure Research and Digital Humanities (*Critifical Infrastructure Studies and Digital Humanities*, 2026) further noted that maintaining the multilingual infrastructure, sedentary colonialism, labour justice, technology lockouts, resuscitation, shadow libraries and community networks was an independent front; and Shannon Mattern, Maintenance and Care, 2018, further stated that maintaining the target also meant maintaining supply chains, tools, protocols, social infrastructure and environmental conditions. The light moments of project launch do not shield patches, migrations, documentation, accessibility, user support and replacements.

The TIE P5 Electronic Text Coding and Exchange Guide (TEI P5: Guidelines for Electronic Text Encoding and Interchange, Version 4.1.01.06, 18 February 2026) of the Text Encoding Initiative is both an XML technical norm and a system that is continuously governed by the Technical Committee, Translators and User Community. Five consecutive versions of the “Research for the Preservation of Nationalities+ Disparities” could be conducted: who proposed a change in labels, which languages/text groups made demands, how to discuss approval, whether translations were synchronized, and how to move downstream digital versions. Open science is understood here as being tiered: open metadata, codes, parameters, derivatives and audit records; and maintaining controlled access to copyright, privacy, Aboriginal cultural protocols and wartime safety materials.
X.10 Research design principles across the frontier

The eight above-mentioned directions share a judgement that the “evidence” of literary research is not only the text's content but also the relationship that enables it to emerge, circulate, be understood and continue. The study plan should include at least six additional fields: labour and signature, jurisdiction and licensing, platforms and versions, translation and migration, community decision-making, maintenance and exit. Any project that describes only innovative tools but does not have a long-term perspective on who will maintain them; that claims only openness, but not who has the right to refuse; that merely accounts for dissemination, while ignoring the work of translators, editors and readers; and that analyses witness without recording narratives, rewards and withdrawals, still fails to complete the transition from “subject research” to “responsible chain studies”.
Part III. Regional scanning

The regional chapters do not target the “national ranking” but see each region as a combination of institutional, linguistic, historical and cross-border networks. Each chapter follows the same six-step structure: academic ecology and hot spots, representative scholars-institutions-results, methodological shifts, three deep case studies, 11 thematic maps, arguments and evidence blind. The “strong/weak” in the table refers only to the evidence density visible during this cycle; it is neither a quality judgement nor a real production count.
Figure 7 Comparative evidence intensity of 11 themes in nine regions; scores are derived only from the evidence code of the report and not from global production rankings
Chapter 14 North America: Methodological Reconstruction in Crisis
14.1 Academic ecology and hotspot sequencing

The North American ecology is made of three nodes. The first is the Department of Literature - Computation Laboratories: Stanford Literary Lab, which uses the computational criticism organization as a collaborative experiment and a short “Pamphlet”, and the current project has extended to identity evolution, cross-linguistics, youth literature and narrative space. Second, NLP/AI interface with literary history: The Berkeley team's experiment with the memory of the large model made the measurement of the effectiveness of the cultural analysis of the “model seen text”. (Chang et al., 2023) Third is the critical and public humanist network: DH Network, Toronto, which also accommodates literature, archives, indigenous knowledge, gender and space research, while Colombia links AI, narrative, clinical and justice with Narrative Information Lab and Narrative Medicine, respectively. (Digital Humanities Network, University of Toronto, n.d.; Tenen, 2025; Division of Narrative Medicine, Columbia University, n.d.)

rows: Critical computing literature and AI literary research

There are stabilization laboratories, NLP papers, production of text history collections and new narrative laboratories; the focus is shifted from “calculability” to “training data, effectiveness, authors and responsibilities” (Chang et al., 2023; Tenen, 2025; Bertram et al., 2024)

blocks.text: 14.3 Approach shift

From model outputs to training history audits. Large models should not be viewed as backgroundless readers. Speak, Memoory extrapolates from members of 571 novels whose names were deformed from 1749-2020, that the level of memory is found to be related to the replicability of the network; the model is better off in the “books remembered” when it performs the task of publishing the year, narrative length, etc., and thus creates a false methodological effect. (Chang et al. 2023) This means that each LLM literary study should report at least model versions, call dates, tips, repetitions, text possibilities, manual blinding assessments and non-LLM baselines.

From one person to a team experiment, but retain a debatable chain of interpretation. The Stanford model of a laboratory combines text selection, modelling, visualization, abnormality reading back and short-text validation; Digital Dostoevsky uses TEI/NLP to study character speech, space, time, emotion and fiction, and is suitable for displaying the cycle of "Experience - Near-reading Validation - Text Encoding back". (Stanford Literary Lab, n.d.; Digital Dostoevsky Project Team, n.d.)

From the "fair variables" to the history of power. Data feminism requires not just an increase in female samples, but rather a definition of classification, who bears the burden of maintaining labour, who is visually harmed; Chun, with retroactive relevance and homophily eugenic/separation history, suggests that model deviations may come from the default structure of the method rather than from mere dirty data. (D'Ignazio et al. 2020; Chun, 2021)

From research on disability to research on disability. Crip Autoship considers time, repetition, description, collaboration, care and accessible formats as conditions for knowledge production; Sterne ' s political modulation rejects transparent, self-contained and complete research subjects, allowing uncertainty, assistive technology and physical experience to enter methodological descriptions. (Milles et al., 2023; Sterne, 2022)

From narrative skills training to institutional justice. Narrative medicine incorporates narratives, phenomenonology and emancipatory social theory; on this basis, critical medical humanism translates “who is qualified to describe, what the clinical system makes difficult to hear” into systemic issues that need further examination, rather than conclusions that are directly inferred from the existence of the curriculum. (Division of Narrative Medicine, Columbia University, n.d.; Vinery et al., 2015)
14.4 Three deep case studies
Case A: Laboratory does not amount to "Automated Reading" - Triangular Validation for Stanford and Toronto

The contribution of Stanford Literary Lab is not to provide a larger sample of readings, but to tear literary interpretations down into mutually verifiable steps: building a library, defining features, comparing distributions, searching for abnormal values, returning to text, and modifying models. The Toronto Digital Dostoevsky is more than an illustration: it has only five ministerial and two mid-parts, but uses structured labels and NLPs to track speech, space, time, emotion and fiction; “calculation” is not necessarily the same as mass, but can be small text that is marked with high density. (Digital Dostoevsky Project Team, n.d.)

Replicable research design: first create paragraph/person/narrative level TEI for 30-50 inter-linguistic novels; then model with interpretable features; blindly comment on the anomaly paragraphs by researchers in different languages; and disagree on consistency and interpretation in the final report. The risk is that copyright prevents the publication of language material, that English terminologists are misused in other languages, and that team work is signed only to PI. More than 40 DH projects in Toronto show that healthier ecology must recognize archives, indigenous knowledge, gender, map and literature calculations, rather than shrinking DH to NLP. (Digital Humanities Network, University of Toronto, n.d.)
Case B: Speak, Memoory - Generating AI deprived literary measurements of the Clean Test Set

The real innovation of the study is not to prove that the model will be endorsed, but to transform memory deviations into cultural analysis of effectiveness. Models that appear to be accurate in their judgement of “literacy history” will systematically reward network visibility if they are trained in popular, copyrighted works and less in small languages and marginal publications. The 70 years of Output's text file also shows that research cannot use post-2022 chat models as a machine to write the whole history; the different stages of the rule system, the combination program, the statistical model and the large model need to be analysed separately. (Chang et al., 2023; Bertram et al., 2024)

Minimum audit protocol: 1 to record models and dates; 2 to stratification of three sets of text in public, suspected training, local/minilingual languages; 3 to set text disturbance and name mask; 4 to compare with known language models, traditional taxonomy, manual code for open sources; 5 to use memory probability as evidence of uncertainty rather than “model understanding”; 6 to issue only features and codes and not divulge original language.
Case C: The “hearing” chain of narrative medicine-writers with disabilities-sounding research

The Colombian narrative medicine combines detailed reading, writing and clinical attention; the critical medical humanities further raises research issues: Whether a relationship is always useful and how the medical system screens for a statement that can be recognized. Crip Autoship points out that the timetable, format, signature and care networks of knowledge production themselves exclude handicapped authors; Diminished Faculties explain that “sound” is not a transparent internal export by damaging sound, hearing and assistive technology. (Milles et al., 2023; Sterne, 2022)

The design that can be used for literary/health research is the three-tier “text-sound-system”: analysis of disease narratives; joint decision with the person concerned on the way to interview, transliterate and anonymous; and tracking of how a hospital, insurance, publication or platform screens a story. Evaluation indicators should not be based solely on clinical counts, but should also include narrative control, accessibility, co-author signature and participant withdrawal mechanisms.
Case D: Black file awareness theory and double-directional validation of publishing sociology

Theoretical mechanisms. The “critical fiction” proposed by Saidiya Hartman of Columbia University in “Venus in Two Acts” is not intended to fill a gap, but to re-archite, use conditions and mark inexplicability, making “why enslaved women only come into being on property, crime or death”. (Hartman, 2008) Christina Sharpe of York University, in The Endstream: About Black Sex and Presence (*In the Wake: On Blackness and Being*), understood the “life of slavery” as still shaping the structure of death, care, image and daily air; and “wake work” as tracking violence as well as identifying forms of black life that go beyond violent files. (Sharpe, 2016) It changes the system of evidence, the state of narratives and the ethics of quotations, rather than adding only black text.

Case and methodology. This can be read in conjunction with Dan Sinykin of the University of Emory University, "The Great Fiction: How Corporate Change Changed the Publishing Industry and American Literature" . The Random House was acquired by Radio Corporation of America, profit and loss statements and editorial work to rewrite literature and test the system experience with Toni Morrison ' s "Beloved* " novels. (Sinykin, 2023) Mark James McGurl, from Stanford University; officially signed by Mark McGurl, " Everything and Less: The Novel in the Age of Amazon " , receives text analysis of distribution, type labels, self-published and recommended logic. (McGurl, 2021) Four layers of the chain of evidence are then registered from sources and silence, comparing drafts, versions and by-products, tracking ownership, books, contracts and marketing, and re-analysing and returning to form. Boundaries are the unopening of commercial data and relevance cannot be written as a one-way cause and effect of market mechanisms; the “black experience” also requires a distinction between Canada, the Caribbean and the discrete spectrum. Researchers are not allowed to re-publicate damaging files, fabricate the minds of historical figures, or return aesthetics to market results.

blocks.text: 14.6 Arguments, blind spots and research opportunities

The calculation is over-reprecipitated: characteristics associated with the non-automatic introduction of formal causes and consequences; publicly available text construction, negative results and abnormal values read back.

LLM ' s double contamination: the training text is not transparent and causes test contamination; researchers may also write back cyberbias when they use models to generate labels. (Chang et al. 2023; Chun 2021)

“Common” paradox: Emotional research and critical medical human tips that require marginalized subjects to provide a receptible and curable story can become a new emotional labor; this should be demonstrated as a systemic research issue, not directly by the narrative medicine course page. (Yao, 2021; Division of Narrative Medicine, Columbia University, n.d.; Viney et al., 2015)

English and Copyright Blind Zones: Large-scale projects make public English the most accessible, leading to structural exclusion of contemporary, small languages, First Nations and commercial publications.

The emerging agenda should be interpreted with caution: the new media public humanities project in Toronto, which links AI to influencers, virtual skin, race, dissipation and emotion, suggests that “synthetic body/synthetic emotion” is taking shape, but is currently mainly in the direction of the project rather than in the firm schools. (Munk School, University of Toronto, 2026)

Questions of implementation: Compare the performance of the same literary history mission in closed source large models, open source known language models and manual experts; create a copyright-friendly sample of bilingual English-French Canadian and First Nations languages; include disability accessibility indicators in DH project evaluation; and study the chain of responsibility between cloud computing energy consumption, training data and literature education with “involving subjects”.
Europe: Intercultural infrastructure, empirical methods and literature in circulation
15.1 Academic ecology and hotspot sequencing

The primary feature of the European front is not a single theoretical centre, but a transnational infrastructure that brings together literary history, linguistic standards, translation and research training organizations. CLS INFRA views data, tools, methods, skills and access mechanisms as the same research ecology, with a result page that not only lists data sets and software but also documents such methodological documents as skills gaps, replicability and “programmable language”. ELTeC, built by COST “Distant Reading for European Literature”, explores the history of multilingualism in approximately 100 ministerial titles, uniform TEI codes and comparable metadata in each core language; extended text has exceeded 2,000, but “comparable” has always relied on the compilation and interpretation of language teams. (COST Distant Reising Team, n.d.)

The second feature is institutionalized radical collaboration. Living with Machines and others, Alan Turing Institute and British Library put historians, data scientists, geographers, computing linguists and librarians in the same stream of work, although the project was closed at 2023, the signature of their team, the description of the data and the method of calculating the collection still affect the digital humanities of Europe. The third feature is the “circulation” of literature to replace static country containers: Berlin Temporal Communications, with the literary creation time community at its core, showed that this agenda was still operational but could not be used as evidence of funding intensity; the “theoretical circulation” project looked at translation, exile, gatekeepers and global/localization, and Palread understood Palestinian literature as transnational practice beyond nation-States with maps, time lines, archives and sound materials. (Temporal Communities Club of Exchange, 2025; Gamber, 2023; Palestine Project Team, etc., n.d.)

rows: Multilingual computing literature and research infrastructure

CLS INFRA, ELTEC, Living with Machines constitutes a complete chain of “Methods-tools-skills-institutions”; the front line is comparability, recurrence and long-term maintenance (CLS INFRA Consortium, n.d.; COST Distant Reading Team, n.d.; Living with Machines Project, 2023)

blocks.text: 15.3 Approach shift
15.3 From “building a language library” to programmable, versionable, comparable infrastructure

ELTeC's value is not just the number of texts, but the visualization of the sample dimensions of the age of publication, sex, length, re-editing/modely; this enables researchers to examine how the sample of “story of a language fiction” was formed. CLS INFRA goes further by incorporating versions, licences, data files, tools and skills training. The logical cross-language research process should therefore be: the language teams define unattainable places before creating minimal public metadata; the model is validated in the language before cross-language comparisons are made; and finally, countries return to publishing historical interpretations. Translation of all text machines into English into a reclassification that, while convenient, removes phrases, rhymes and text symbols should not be a default scheme.
15.3.2 From technical assistance to “radical collaboration”

Living with Machines shows that the knowledge of the librarians of OCR, the layout, copyright and the library gaps are as critical as the algorithm selection; and historians are not just providing for data science. (Living with Machines Project, 2023) Team results should be written by a contributing role (speech, code, field interpretation, project management, public participation) rather than by mere paper writing. The direct inspiration for literary research is that the work of maintenance of materials, calibration, translation and metadata must be visible, otherwise “sizing innovation” is based on invisible work.
15.3.3 From country comparison to tracking of the mechanisms of communication

The “theory flow” of Temporal Communities does not see theory as a constant concept, but rather as a way to track translation choices, book reviews, courses, publishing houses, migrants and political events. (Gamper, 2023) Samoyault reminds that translation may be a device for colonial naming, forced assimilation and testimony injuries, as well as practice of restoring relationships. (Samoyault, 2020) The method should link text comparison to the edition, sequence, delete, fund, copyright and receiving network.
15.3.4 Layer model from "Ai text with author" to author attribution

The Bajohr 's 'causers' (causal authorship) measure the control/distance between humans and output, while the 'distributed authors ' (distributed authorship) incorporate data authors, model developers, prompters, editors and platforms into the creative network; each avoids leaving the author to the machine or completely abating responsibility. (Bajohr, 2024) Its “Listural Indicative” (deixis) further states that the author's qualifications are not read directly from the statistical characteristics of the text, but are dependent on the text, signature, scene and reader recognition conditions. (Bajohr, 2024) Thus, AI literary cases should be analysed simultaneously with the chain of generation, publication appearances, legal signature and reader acceptance.
15.3.5. Triple from experimental reduction to “model-exposure-history”

Jacobs' neurocognitive poetry provides a multilayered model of vocabulary, visionization, aesthetic and emotional response; Kukkonen's prediction process understands reading as a continuous updating of expected physical activity. (Jacobs, 2015; Kukkonen, 2019) Both are easily lost in long forms, historical semantics and rereading experience if activated with short irritation and average brain zone. Better design is: pre-registered visual/pkin electric/behavioural indicators; retention of complete or longer sections; open recollections after experiments; interpretation of forms by literary researchers, with reporting of individual differences and failed replication. Cognitive poetry in Stockwell can serve as a bridge between concepts and teaching, but cannot be used as a term for a testable hypothesis. (Stockwell, 2020)
15.3.6 From “patient story” to co-production and analysis of the system of critical systems

The critical medical humanist emphasis on health, technology, economy, space and narrative is intertwined and does not accept the division of labour “the medical provider of facts, human value”. Living the Voice joins listeners with literature, philosophy, neuroscience and clinical research; the current Durant platform further institutionalized hidden experiences, neurodiversity and public cooperation. (Institute for Medical Humanities, Durham University, n.d.; Holding the Voice Project Team, 2022) Methodologically, participants should be involved in the study, interpretation and dissemination of issues, rather than providing only “live cases”.
15.4 Three deep case studies
Case A: ELTEC-CLS INFRA - “Comparability” is a consultation, not a natural attribute of data

ELTEC ' s core language material is used as an initial comparable framework in about 100 novels in 12 languages, and language and text are added by expanding the collection; harmonization of the TEI tags allows researchers to compare length, age, author, chapter and vocabulary/styping characteristics. However, the pattern of publication, the literary boundaries, colonial relations and the female signature of the 19th century “novel” in different linguistic traditions are not consistent. If only the same number is sampled, the balance of the database is mistaken for historical balance.

CLS INFRA provides for revision of the version and skills: each data point needs to be kept with its source, editing decision, license and version; the cross-language model reports language errors, not only the overall accuracy rate. The recommended paper structure is “double results”: Part I gives a replicable cross-language model, and Part II provides an explanation by language experts of which models come from the publication history, OCR or labelling system. The question of research could be “when the first person's account is added to the different novel traditions”, but the conclusion cannot be written as a single line of European modernization.
Case B: Palread - How digital maps avoid locking up the literature of the diaspora again into national borders

Palread describes Palestinian literature as “the country of words” (Country of Words): writers, works, places and migration routes are connected by maps, time lines, archives, teaching materials and podcasts. (PALRAD PROJECT TEAM AND OTHER, N.d.) It's a three-point breakthrough: The first is that the lack/dispersion of the national territory is not a data deficiency, but rather a condition for the literary space; the second is that the Arabic language is the common object of the original language, translation, sound and historical events; and the third is that the public-oriented platform allows research findings to remain beyond mere papers.

The risks are equally clear: map locations may re-establish mobile identities; sensitive locations and networks of people may pose security concerns; and the platform ' s English interface may again give priority to the global northern reader. Fuzzy location, hierarchical access, community calibration and multilingual metadata should therefore be allowed. A parallel reading with the “Theory Flow” project would examine how the Palestinian concept was transformed by translation, academic gatekeepers and climate/post-colonial agendas rather than by treating Europe as a reception centre. (Gamper, 2023)
Case C: Holding the Voice -- putting literary metaphors, clinical classifications and experiencer knowledge on the same table

Heating the Voice (2012-2022) studies the phenomenon of “hearing sound”, combining neuroscience, psychiatry, psychology, philosophy, literary history, theology and theaethematic experience. Its literary value is not just to collect “crazy narratives”, but to compare how different times/texts name sound, how subjects relate to sound, and what complexity clinical questionnaires condensate into symptoms. Critical medical humanities provide a layer of institutional analysis, while the current Durant platform extends the methodology to concealing experience and neural diversity. (Institute for Medical Humanities, Durham University, n.d.; Vinery et al., 2015)

Replicable design: Develop agreed narrative narratives for sound; coding relationships, spatial, personal, emotional and cultural interpretations by experienced, clinical and literary researchers; more consistent/conflict areas; and use conflict itself as an outcome. The publication of the study should provide easy-to-read abstracts, audio or alternative formats and prohibit the use of literary expressions of participants as diagnostic evidence.
Case D: Institutional naming of the literature from the digital manuscript production chain to the later

Theory mechanisms and manuscript cases. The text is subject to scientific/genetic criticism (critique génétique), which organizes plans, drafts, transcripts, proofs and published texts as “ex-text” (vant-texte), and studies the writing process and not only in foreign languages. The Institute of Modern Texts and Texts (Institut des textes et manuscries modernes, ITEM) is a joint institution of the Centre national de la recherche scientifique, CNRS and the Institut d ' Etudes pédagogiques de Paris (École normale supérieure, ENS); this shift has been systematized by Pierre-Marc de Biasi ' s “Texte scientique” (*Génétique des textes*). (Institut des textes et manuscries modernes, n.d.; de Biasi, 2011) The Université de Rouen Normandie University, Centre Gustave Flaubert and the Bibliothèque Patrimoniale de Rouen Library, Gustave Flaubert, Mrs. Badame Bovary*, will produce approximately 4,600 pages of electronic manuscripts, and 487 columns will be generated to describe and sequence events. (Centre Gustave Flaubert, n.d.) It can be coded, deleted, replaced, moved and reviewed on a page-by-page basis; the order is uncertain and the author's intent cannot be forced.

Post-immigration spreads with borders. Nazli Hodaie, Schwabengminde University of Education Pädagogische Hochschule Schwäbisch Gmünd, and Michael Hofmann, Universität Paderborn, University of Padborn, defined “post-immigrants” as an anti-hegemonistic paradigm rather than an age label for questioning language, identity and attribution. Dschinns, a six-person perspective, connects labour, migration, intergenerational and child-cool experiences. (Aydemir, 2022/2023) Coding perspective, multilingualism and by-line, not classified by descent. Boundary: The archives are orthodox and the concept cannot be divorced from the history of national systems.
15.5 Themes map

Literature is understood as translation, exile, doormanship, publishing and time community continuous generation (Temporal Communities Cluster of Exchange, 2025; Gamber, 2023; Palread Project Team, etc., n.d.; Samoyault, 2020)
Authors ' causal distance, distributed creation, indicative and machine images, more prominent than simple styles (Bajohr, 2024; Parikka, 2023)
Interdisciplinary research of hearing experience, along with machine visual/operating images, expands the literature medium “other than text” (Hearing the Voice Project Team, 2022; Parikka, 2023)

Critical medical literature, the Wellcome Large Project and the Current Research Platform (Institute for Medical Humanities, Durham University, n.d.; Holding the Voice Project Team, 2022; Vinery et al., 2015)
Cross-forming in neurocognitive poetry, hearing experience, and cooler time and memory studies (Temporal Communities Cluster of Exchange, 2023; Jacobs, 2015; Holding the Voice Project Team, 2022)
Moving from national memory to dynamic memory in travel, migration, media and ecological relations (Erll, 2024; Erll, 2025)

blocks.text: 15.6 Arguments, blind spots and research opportunities

Standardistic paradox: No criteria can be compared, but the uniform classification would treat a country ' s history or colonial language relations as universal. The solution is not to abandon the comparison, but to make public the “uncomparable log” and language errors.

Inequality in project-based systems: EU/UK large-scale funding generates high-quality infrastructure and may also place data collation on less resourced language teams, and the theoretical visibility of central agency harvesting. The signature of contributions and the flow of funds should be indicators of assessment.

Neural reduction risk: brain/behaviour indicators can test some mechanisms and cannot be a substitute for aesthetic history and interpretation debate. “Natural reading” of the experiment must also describe equipment, language skills and educational background. (Jacobs, 2015; Kukkonen, 2019)

AI Author Responsibility: Distributive authors are not “unaccountable”. A distinction should be made between the chain of responsibility for data copyright, model design, alert control, editorial selection and publication fraud. (Bajohr, 2024)

The two-faceted nature of translation: emphasis on repairing cannot wash away from colonial, concentration camps or forced assimilation of translation violence; rather, speaking untranslated can close the inter-lingual alliance. (Samoyault, 2020)

The theme of the conference was a dynamic cross-age, cross-text community but without an analysis of labour, class and publishing systems, “queer” could become a generic non-normative label. (Temporal Communities Club of Exchange, 2023; Gleeson et al., 2021)

The most promising designs include: creating an in-language afore-check and cross-linguistic beyets comparison with ELTeC; establishing a circulation network for the preamble/book review/courses of theoretical translations; implementing a “generation chain audit” of AI literary publications; transposing the common code of Health The Voice to the narrative of mental retardation, chronic disease blogs and neurodiversity novels; and studying carbon, plants, rivers and database carriers of collective memory.
China mainland and Hong Kong/Australia: Digital, Network and Autonomous Knowledge Systems
16.1 Hotspots and Academic Ecology

“Diggle humanities” has shifted from the use of tools to the debate of cognitiveism. The annual official academic review summarized literature in terms of the digital humanities from tool applications to human-engineer synergies, and combined knowledge mapping, language libraries, GIS, social networks, literature computing and large models; and placed the “new popular arts” “global Southern literature” “New popular literature” “in China's autonomous literary knowledge system” on the high-frequency agenda. This review is an agenda-based source that does not determine national consensus but rather recognizes the common attention of policy-relations-higher schools. The ancient literature digital humanities course at Beijing University and the “Technology Humanities” training camp further show that core training has been concretized into problem modelling, regular/programming, tagging languages, knowledge mapping, space humanities, web analysis and AI support rather than being referred to as “interdisciplinary”. (Beijing University, Chinese Language and Literature Department, 2024; Beijing University Institute of Artificial Intelligence, 2024)

Taiwan and Macao have entered the “knowledge base - research tools - open presentation” phase of a long-cycle digital collection. The DADH Conference and the AI/DHD Research Cluster are ongoing organizations of the DHD Centre for Human Studies; the Digital Cultural Centre of the Central Institute of Research has shifted its focus from 2026 to open digital human knowledge base; and the new phase of the Institute of History 2024 - emphasizes cross-courage authority control, ancient navigation and GIS. 2024 The exhibition of results, covering literature, journalism, ecology, ethnicity and history, shows that the digital humanities has shifted from “scan project” to marking, comparison, statistics, decomposition and public display. (CIF, 2024) The University of Macao and the University of Coimbra launched Camêes Lab in 2026 to build a readable, interoperable digital platform for the Portuguese-speaking World Literature Book, which shows that Macao is entering the digital world literary infrastructure with a Chinese-Portuguese cross-linguistic node. (University of Macau et al., 2026)

The online literature became a comprehensive laboratory of platform capital, typologies, cross-media and AI. The Chinese online literature and literature book 2024, which China co-sponsored, documented parallel trends in reality, female subjects, folklore/wildness, science fiction, IP conversion, AI acousticization and hint engineering, and explicitly acknowledged that long auto-generation was still premature. The Institute of CyberLictology of the University of China in China has institutionalized “accompanied criticism”, documentation databases, evaluation systems and industry cooperation. The " Entente of Artistic and Ethical Artistic Intelligence" of 2025, by Oyang Yu, has taken the discussion forward to the level of authorism, value conflict and art ethics. O'Yoo Yang, 2025

Hong Kong's front-line contribution is focused on the “Sinophone”+Cool/Trisex+ Comparison Approach” and eco-humanities. Alvin Ka Hin Wong; also under the signature of Alvin K. Wong, queer Hong Kong as a Sinopone method and unruly Comparison: instead of using Hong Kong as a localized sample of “China case” or Western cool theory, regional theory is rewritten from improbable, friction and “minor-minor”. (Wong, 2024; Wong, 2025) Emily Yu Zong (the author's full name in English; the official Chinese name is not yet authoritative) placed migration, refugees, labour and multi-species ecology in the same political ecological framework; Lo Kwai-Cheung; another, Kwai-Cheung Lo) and Li Hung-chiung (the author's full name in Roman; another, Hung-chiung Li; the Chinese official name is not authoritatively documented) understood Asia as a space where waters, wars, economies and ecology intersect. (Zong, 2026; Lo et al, 2025)

Ecology, medicine and digital narratives are all mixed. The ecological holisticism, ecological responsibility and civilizational criticism of Wang Nord at the University of Xiamen are important sources of ecological criticism on the continent. (Wwanno, 2008) VR/Exhibit project of the Hong Kong Baptist University Emily Zong combines environmental vulnerability, healthy humanities, immigration and digital stories; the Qinghua Narrative Medicine course includes detailed studies, parallel medical records, clinical cases and palliative care as a training method. (Zong, 2026; Guo Li-Ping et al., n.d.)
16.2 Representing scholars-institutions-results

rows: Institute of CyberLictology, Central South University
Artistic Ethics of Artificial Intelligence (2025); e-literate assessment/Yearbook system

Moving the platform literature from phenomenon description to evaluation systems, documentation infrastructure and AI ethics; attention to their institutions and industry cooperation may lead to a position bias. (Ouyang Qui, 2025; Institute of CyberLicterology, Central South University, 2023)
AI Long Experiment "The Angel of the World"

It can be used as an early case of "Phrasing Project+Assisting Late Editors"; the Blue Book claims that about 70% AI, 30% of the manual is self-reported as a project and should not be extrapolated to evidence of creative efficiency. (Center for Online Literature, China Writers Association, 2025)
Ecocritical Europe and America - General Studies in EcoLogical Studies (2008)

The establishment of a framework of ecological literature in Chinese with ecological integrity, responsibility, early warning and civilizational criticism is a new product of the current node rather than 2020s. (Wan No, 2008)
Li Linfang / North Great Digital Humanities
Chinese Language Department, Digital Human Studies Centre, Beijing University
“Data humanist approach in ancient literature research” (2024); “Techno-humanistic” training camp (2024)

Shows the operational methodology of classical literature studies: text tagging, programming, databases, spectrographs, GIS, web analysis and AI. (Beijing University, Chinese Language and Literature Department, 2024; Beijing University Institute of Artificial Intelligence, 2024)
Jeapy Hsiang Jieh and Docusky community
Digital Humanities Research Centre, National University of Taiwan

The feature is the “personal database” - labeled - statistical - visualization” workflow for non-programmers; caution is needed about the platform's default classification alternative interpretation. (Data Humanities Research Centre, Taiwan State University, 2026)
Digital Cultural Centre/Study Team, Institute of Middle Studies
2026 Digital Human Knowledge Bank Programme; 2024 — Digital Deep-Agriculture Humanities Programme

(c) Include open delegation of authority, authority, cross-bank links, GIS and long-term preservation in infrastructure governance. (Digital Cultural Centre of the Central Academy, 2025; Institute of Historical and Linguistic Studies of the Central Institute, 2024; Central Institute, 2024)
University of Macao/University of Coimbra Camoyes Lab Team
University of Macao, University of Coimbra
Camões Lab — Uma Máquina do Mundo（2026）

The digital storage, readability and interoperability of Portuguese-language classics connect Macao, Portugal and the Portuguese-speaking world; the current evidence is mainly from platforms, and academic use remains to be seen. (University of Macau et al., 2026)
Department of Comparative Literature, University of Hong Kong
“Queer Hong Kong as a Sinophone Method”（2024，DOI）；Unruly Comparison（2025，DOI）
Theories of the Cool and China Centers of Resistance to “unordery/irremuneration” Regional Studies; Cross-Literacy, Film, Visual Culture, Law and Civil Society. (Wong, 2024; Wong, 2025)
Planetarity from Below (2026, DOI); VR/Eco-Health Narrative Exhibition

The migration of ecology, refugees, labour, waste, animals and technology is woven as “bottom-up planetary”; the methodology is combined with text/image analysis, interviews, stunting and digital stories. (Zong, 2026)
The water landscape is a substitute for the continental-national national centre map, linking Asian geopolitics, ocean history and human criticism. (Lo et al., 2025)
Beijing University Medical Humanities/Chynic Clinical Medicine School

Translating literary reading into clinical listening, parallel medical records and shared decision-making; when applied to literary research, the efficacy of treatment should be avoided from being equated with the simple meaning of the text. Wait, n.d.

blocks.text: 16.3 New theory/new methodology

Autonomous knowledge systems/new popular arts: The Continental 2025 agenda combines “theoretical localization” with numbers/AI, but its dual political-academic attributes must be clearly stated in the main report and not be considered as neutral theoretical facts. (editorial section of the China Social Science Network, 2026)

Unruly comparison: Recognition of non-conformity and relative violence among the target groups, without pursuing the same scale; study of the diversity of Hong Kong, Taiwan, and Trans-Chinese languages. (Wong, 2025)

Planetary bottom-up (planetarity from below): from the ecological world of mutual vulnerability of migrants/refugees/labour and human partners, rather than from the abstract “human” perspective. (Zong, 2026)
Entangled waterscapes: Recapitulating “Asia” with currents, ports, islands, floods and infrastructure, cross-regional dialogue with hydrocolonism in Africa. (Lo et al., 2025)

The hybrid evidence approach to platform literature: platform data/annual reports, typology, readership studies, industry interviews and fine readings; the need to incorporate platform self-reported indicators, algorithmic visibility and review of the environment into bias analysis. (Center for Online Literature of the Chinese Writers Association, 2025; Institute of CyberLicterology, China-South University, 2023)

Digital version - Knowledge mapping - Space network chain: First version/metadata to be deconciled with entity, then network and spatial analysis, and finally back to reading and validation; no direct inference of ideas from word frequency. 2024, Beijing University, Chinese Language and Literature Department; Beijing University Institute of Artificial Intelligence, etc., 2024; Institute of Historical Languages, Central Institute, 2024)
16.4 Case study
Case CN-A: WebLiction and Generating AI - “Alternative Author” is not the most accurate issue at this time

The Blue Book of Chinese CyberLicter 2024 shows that the more mature application in 2024 is not automatically generating a million-word novel, but rather role recognition, text labelling, multi-role TTS acousticization, inspiration/retrieving/relaying aids and IP processes. The Master of the Huadong Division, the Vigilante, is a long experiment that clearly discloses the artificial post-manage. The CBA Online Literature Centre therefore examines the desirability of designing “AI Can Writing Literature” to be broken down into four levels: quality generation, labour processes, platform governance, author/training rights. The Blue Book is produced by the Industry-Cooperative System, which has the advantage of first-hand material and may dilute the Platform ' s labour-copying conflicts; it should be cross-checked with the author ' s interviews, variations in the version and the Platform ' s provisions.
Case CN-B: Hong Kong as a place of theory, not an example

The 2024 chapters of Wong propose “Cool Hong Kong's Chinese language approach” through the War Women's Convergence, the Closeness of Women Workers in the 1960s, Transregional Sex Sex Desires and the Transracial Desires of the Empire; and the writing of further studies on cross-literacy, film, visual culture, law and post-dissociation 2019. (Wong, 2024; Wong, 2025) The key method is not to encode the text by the already existing LGBTQ category, but to track the friction between “emotional history—dismissal of archives—media translation—legal structure”. This case can be read in conjunction with the queer decolonialities of the Philippines, and the African critical of the gender dimension in the West.
Case CN-C: Eco-Health - Research and Development of Digital Stories

The Zong project has made VR ecological vulnerability of the cattle in Hong Kong a story of the ecological vulnerability of the cattle and links the Healing Atmospheres to the Hong Kong Medical Ethics/Humanist Unit. (Zong, 2026) Rather than treating the exhibition as “dissemination of results”, it is more appropriate to view it as a research method: text/image selection, spatial organization, participant experience and cross-professional collaboration itself to generate information. The risk is that the feeling of immersion may create the illusion that “the other understands”, so that records of decision-making, user feedback and failure should be kept.
Case CN-D: China ' s original theory and online literature cross-media version chain

Theoretical mechanisms. Zhang Jiang of the University of China Science and Social Sciences, who opposes the use of a priori theory of interpretation and “public interpretation”, requires individual interpretations to be translated into public knowledge through text evidence, public reason and rebuttable discussion; this is a set of interpretative responsibility agreements that should not be used to close down multidimensionality. Cai Shuqing of the Sichuan University (Sichuan University) has raised the heterogeneity, increase or delete, misreading and recoding of trans-linguistic, cross-cultural transmission from “noise” to a central target in the Comparative Literation of Literature. (Cao, 2013) Shen Dan of Beijing University distinguishes between explicit circumstances and the “hidden process” (covert process) that crosses the text, complements or subverts each other. (Shen, 2023) The three can form a complementary chain of “evidence constraints—cross-media variants—reverse narratives”, not just a “China Theory” directory.

Cross-media cases and methods. A version spectrum can be created by Zhang Wei, Tang Jia San Shao and the official English version*Soul Land*. The author page of the Chinese Writers Association confirms her original name, writing name and novel-animation-television; the Chena Literaute Limited has placed novels, cartoons, animations, games and WebNovel translation overseas in the same chain of intellectual property. (China Writers Network, 2022; WebNovel, n.d.; Reading Group, n.d.) The research unit is the “version node”: the retention of chapters in the Chinese starting-line network (Qidian.com) and the revision dates, entity books, animation collections/films, games task/pays mechanisms and English glossary; the consolidation of section-by-point coded figures, world rule deletions, gender and eco-ethics, interactive mechanisms and translation and naturalization, and the use of the “hidden process” to check whether resource grabs or subordinations continue to occur in the context of explicit escalation, and to publish counter-references and evidence chains in “public interpretation”. Boundaries are: Platform data and global coverage figures are self-reported by enterprises and cannot be equated with readers; lower shelf, retrofitting and algorithm recommendations can undermine reversibility; variations are not natural for creation, but may also come from censorship, working periods, business classification or translation errors. The platform systems and language markets of the mainland, Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan must be sampled to avoid replacing the Chinese Language Academy with a single super-intellectual property right.
16.5 Arguments and asymmetries

The policy agenda overlaps with, but does not equate with, the academic frontier. The “auto-knowledge system” “New Mass Arts” of the Global South is both academic and policy-oriented; the source nature is to be noted in the reference. (editorial section of the China Social Science Network, 2026)

The continental platform is rich in data and is not sufficiently independently reversible. Users, creative numbers, AI usage rates are mostly provided by platform or industry reports; they are suitable for describing ecology and not for making causal extrapolations without conditions. (Center for Online Literature, China Writers Association, 2025)

Hong Kong/Australian Theory has a high international visibility but is also shaped by the English publishing threshold. Wong, Zong, Lo and others enter the global discourse through English-language academic publishing houses; this does not represent the entire ecology of local Chinese/Single language criticism. (Wong, 2025; Zong, 2026; Lo et al., 2025)

The evidence in Macao still favours infrastructure rather than local criticism of the community. Camêes Lab, which attests to the existence of a Chinese-Portuguese digital world literary node in Macao, does not adequately cover the Chinese-Chinese, Portuguese-speaking and native-Portuguese literature and the network of local scholars, which should be included in the main report as a reserve rather than as a single platform representing the whole picture. (University of Macau et al., 2026)

DDI is not equally sustainable and open. The Taiwan project includes authorization, authority and openness in its funding conditions; and continental projects often have database accessibility, platform closure or long-term information maintenance deficiencies. (Digital Cultural Centre of the Central Academy, 2025; Institute of Historical and Linguistic Studies of the Central Institute, 2024)

The evidence of “validity” of narrative medicine is different from that of literary interpretation. The course certification methodology is used without demonstrating clinical effects; the main report should separate educational practice, clinical results and literature theory. Wait, n.d.
Japan and Korea: Classical A, Disaster Memory and Literature Universalization
17.1 Hot spots and academic ecology

The classical Japanese studies have resulted in the national “image-OCR-speak-generated interactive” tube lines. The NIJL ' s 2024-2033 DDH 10-year plan is centred on Japanese literature and expands image, AI texting and analytical techniques. (National Institute of Japan Literature, 2024) Miwo/KuroNet, KMNIST and Tsukushi advanced from character recognition to IIIF readers, semantic search, modern Japanese question and answer and historical Japanese models. (Center for Open Data in the Humanities, 2025; Clanuwat et al., 2021; Center for Open Data in the Humanities, 2018)

Post-earthquake literature remains the main axis of Japanese memory research and intersects with world literature, gender and the environment. The Riko Kimura considers 3/11 to be a break in the history of literature that needs to be rewritten, while studying the sex/gender structure of the Source Language. The 2023 World Literature Symposium, recorded in 2024, recorded in the University of Tokyo's knowledge base, showed that the world literature of the marshland is still being discussed; Japanese “world literature” is not simply a translation of Damrosch, but is related to the experience of Russia and Eastern Europe, translation practice and Japanese literature location. (Mamano Mitsuyoshi et al., 2023)

The Japanese environmental literature has expanded from natural writing to the coolest ecofeminism, pollution history and more-than-human. The book 2023, which is a beautiful city, and the study 2024, will see the environment as a cross-section of cultural construction, material reality and political economy; the paper by Katoshi Nakamura to Yuko, a child in Zinjima, constitutes “cool ecofeminism” in the form of dead, animals, water and imaginary pregnancy. (Jyoseh Masami, 2023; Murakami Katsunao, 2024)

Cognitive/neurological aesthetics are promoted in Japan by the Psychology Laboratory of the Ministry of Literature, but the study of literary cognitive studies is still adjacent to, not the same field. The University of Kanxi ' s Ishizu-sama laboratory uses brain function measurements, experimental psychology and quantitative comparative studies of beauty, sacrosanct, emotional and well-being; it can provide a method for reading and reaction research, but the available notes are mainly directed at visual arts and general aesthetics. Ishizu Tomohiro, n.d.

The Korean frontier is composed of three cross-lines: “Worldized translation infrastructure + indigenous digital methods + disaster memory”. The Digital Library of the Korea Institute of Literature and Translation, as of 2025-12-31, records 7,832 translated bibliographys, 4,586 author information, 1,158 multimedia materials and 1,528 archive collections; this provides institutionalized data for translation history, bibliographic measurement and global dissemination research. Meanwhile, the TF-IDF study of the tenth anniversary of the Viet Nam has produced a cross-reference to political science of “deprivation”; the Korean poetry study has pushed post-human feminism, care and “living together” to the forefront. (허준행 Heo Jun-haeng, 2024; 정영진 Jeong Yeong-jin, 2024; 황선희 Hwang Seon-hui, 2025)

The unique strengths of the Korean digital literature are humanities, ancient literature knowledge organizations and the Translators Network. The Korea Central Institute of Sciences (AKS) has a training network centred on data design, content compilation and knowledge; AI-Hub provides thousands of-word Korean-speaking training data on the Korean-Hand-on-King OCR; and Choi Eun-Kin uses 912 English translations and 442 translators to build a co-translation network. (한국학중앙연구원, 2023;한국지능정보사회진흥원, 2022; 최은경, Choi Eun-kyung, 2024)
17.2 Representing scholars-institutions-results

rows: The AAI OCR, open data, IIIF and the generator reader are linked to the classical literature research infrastructure. (Center for Open Data in the Humanities, 2025; Clanuwat et al., 2021; Center for Open Data in the Humanities, 2018)
National Library of Literature and Research
Model Building in Humanities through Data-Driven Problem Solving（2024—2033）
A rare national, 10-year literary data programme; it can be seen how infrastructure can reshape the “Japanese literature” boundary. (National Institute of Japan Literature, 2024)
The invisibility of disasters, the fragmentation of unwritten persons and the history of literature; the high flow. Kimura Saeko, 2013
Proceedings of the special seminar on the Theory of the World (2023; 2024 in the library, DOI)

The discussions of several researchers present the world literature, translation and Japanese literature, which are suitable for comparison with the Korean national translation system. (Mamano Mitsuyoshi et al., 2023)
The history of ecological criticism, critical shifts and climate narratives are brought into Japanese public academic. Yuki Masami, 2023)

The reading of the desire for a child, the boundary of life, the animal hierarchy and environmental matter together forms a migratoryly transposable paradigm of the ecofeminism of a child. (Murakami Katsunao, 2024)
University of Kansai, Department of Literature

fMRI/Brain Survey, Experimental Psychology and Aesthetic Well-being; appropriate as a neighbourhood method of literary reading experiments, not a direct substitute for Explanatory. Ishizu Tomohiro, n.d.
“Text-drilling study of the Escuela of the Vigilante” (2024, DOI)
The TF-IDF word/thematic statistics combined with qualitative analysis are clear examples of studies of computing memory. (Hyo Jun-haeng, 2024)
The analysis of the ethics and politics of the Episodes of Viet Nam by Butler/Athanasiou constitutes an amendment to the purely emotional/trauma framework. (Jeong Yeong-jin, 2024)
“The perception of planetary survival and the shift in poetry” (2025)
From the post-human feminism, the subjects of relations, listening, caring and “gravity” were introduced, reading in detail the words of Rohit and Kim Kee-soon. (Hwang Seon-hui, 2025)
Kim Hyeon and AKS Humanist Information Community
Training in the compilation of human and clerical documents
Emphasis on building home/knowledge maps with academic content rather than outsourcing technology to engineering teams. (Phone )
The SNA is used to reveal the collaborative structure of literary translation and to recast the term “individual translator” as a network of institutions. (Choo Eun-kyung, 2024)

blocks.text: 17.3 New theory/method

AI Auxiliary Version: OCR is no longer the end point; images, character frames, text, version metadata, the IIIF page and question and answer interface need to be tracked back. (National Institute of Papanece Literature, 2024; Center for Open Data in the Humanities, 2025)

Generating classical reading: Tsukushi allows for searching for ancient texts in modern Japanese, but research must also preserve model versions, retrieve evidence and uncertain answers to prevent "floating is correct". (Center for Open Data in the Humanities, 2025)

Two-track analysis of disasters: TF-IDF/Themes compares the distribution of terms, the theory of deprivation/trauma and the empirical description of ethical-political structures; the two are not substitutes for each other. (허준행 Heo Jun-haeng, 2024; 정영진 Jeong Yeong-jin, 2024)

The Korean study recasts the verse's characterization as a relationship, and the reader becomes “respondable listener”. This is a front-line concept presented in a single paper and cannot yet be written in a full Korean consensus. (Hwang Seon-hui, 2025)

Networking translation sociology: research on “the universalization of Korean literature” with co-translation, centrality, language flow and funding tagging is better able to recognize brokering power than simply counting the number of translations. (Literature Transport of Korea, 2025; 최은경 Choi Eun-kyung, 2024)

Cool Ecofeminism: Treats environmental elements such as unregulated desires, dead, animals and water/light as common actors linking gender research to the human face of the environment. (Murakami Katsunao, 2024)
17.4 Case study
Case JK-A: From “Reading unlit” to “dialogue with classical books”

Miwo training system for million-degree character data; KMNIST uses a loss of words as a machine learning benchmark; Tsukushi then connects OCR text to IIIF and generate chats. (Center for Open Data in the Humanities, 2025; Clanuwat, etc., 2021; Center for Open Data in the Humanities, 2018) This pipe transforms images that cannot be retrieved on a large scale into text material, while introducing three types of errors: error in character recognition, faulty break/modern interpretation, no source for generating answers. Ideally, the design of the study should sample manual calibration, reporting time-period/book body accuracy, return the answer to the chart of the chain and retain the word “unidentifiable” as data rather than silent correction.
Case JK-B: Tenth anniversary of the Viet Nam — quantifying not to eliminate ethical controversy

Hsu Jun-Hong compared the TF-DF by nouns, adjectives, verbs, distinguishing between “mary/shock” “memory/truth” “future action”, and explicitly recognizing that quantitative amounts are not sufficient to exhaust literary values. (허준행 Heo Jun-haeng, 2024) Zheng Young-jin uses “deprivation” to link his ethics to the State/neo-liberal responsibility. (정영진 Jeong Yeong-jin, 2024) The two combinations explain that the method of calculation is appropriate to establish a reviewable difference, that the theoretical reading is responsible for explaining why the difference is political; and that if only the word frequency is retained, the controversy is desterilized as a list of topics.
Case JK-C: "Saving Together" by the Korean poem

Huang Seng Jie has read the Rhoseid and Kim Hae-soon approaches, comparing the “hearing/care rhythm” with “the Union of the Unhumans/weaving of the ruins”, and has proposed poems that are not mirrors of the world, but rather devices for re-engineered relationships. (황선희 Hwang Seon-hui, 2025) This could create a cross-regional theory with Hong Kong, China, from alignment from below, Africa retaking for water; it should be noted that the evidence is the case of two poets and does not directly represent the entire 2020s Korean poem.
17.5 Arguments and asymmetries

Japan's national projects can provide stable data and standards, but they can also entrench the “Japanese literature” border into a text that can be collected, identified and authorized by institutions. (National Institute of Japan Literature, 2024)

OCR always gives priority to clear fonts and sufficient type data; local manuscripts, hybrid books and female/civil writings may continue to be “low resource” status. (Clanuwat et al., 2021; Center for Open Data in the Humanities, 2018)

The bibliography of the Korea Institute of Literature and Translation is a valuable infrastructure and a product of national cultural policy; the funding of tagging, selection mechanisms and copyright restrictions should be included in the analysis. (Literature Transport Institute of Korea, 2025)

Korea's high level of activity in research in Viet Nam could lead to a difference in the visibility of the “disaster study = Viet Nam”; Guangzhou, comfort women, break/war, migration and industrial disasters required a separate language layer. (정영진 Jeong Yeong-jin, 2024; 황선희 Hwang Seon-hui, 2025)

The subjects of neurotheesthetic experiments, stimulus materials and statistical prominence cannot automatically answer literary history, power and speech; they are suitable as triangulations rather than final adjudications. Ishizu Tomohiro, n.d.
South and South-East Asia: multilingual places, version networks and intellectual activism
18.1 Hot spots and academic ecology

South Asia's theoretical frontier has moved further from “post-colonial” to caste, anti-Brahmin intellectuals, testimony and declassification. The Dalit female testimonio study in Sharma Rege combines experience, coauthorship and resistance knowledge; Divya Dwivedi moves caste/racial criticism, non-natural narratives and “declassification” to 2024-2025. This is not a simple “localization” of post-colonial theory, but rather a question of the English theory classics that downgrade caste to a local variable.

Cognitive literature has a long indigenous line in India. Narrative Gravity of Rukmini Bhaya Nair has combined the theory of the political synthesis of speech, speech behaviour, cognitive science, culture and narratives, and IT Delhi official research notes also list the intercultural programs of emotion, gesture, eye action/fMRI. Its value lies in the matching of cognitive research with post-colonial/cultural differences, rather than in the elimination of linguistic differences by universal brain models.

Multilingualism and translation are not subsidiary themes, but are the basis of disciplines. The Indian Literature of Sahitya Akademi, which is based on the English translation of 23 Indian languages, also highlights Dalit, tribal/oral and LGBTQ poetry with the translation prize and the 2024 Literature Festival. (Sahitya Akademi, n.d.; Sahitya Akademi, 2024) Fakrul Alam of Bangladesh revisits English language research from a post-colonial perspective, Tariq Rahman of Pakistan writes an English literature history, and Neloufer de Mel of Sri Lanka links gender, militarization, performance and memory. (Alam, 2021; de Mel, 2007; University of Colombia Department of Engineering, 2024)

The digital textography case remains Bichitra, but the focus is on differences in versions rather than big data showcasing. The Jadavpur University online book of Tagore contains 47,520 pages of manuscripts and 91,637 pages of authoritative copies, full text transcriptions, retrieval and tertiary proofing. This has led to a shift from a single final version of the work to a network of self-retardable, tailored and bilingual versions.

The digital literature in South-East Asia is centred on endangered manuscripts, community ownership and regional collaboration. DREAMSEA was completed in the first phase by UIN Jakarta/PPIM in cooperation with Hamburg CSMC, with 579,562 images, 8,790 manuscripts, 164 collections and 56 field missions; the second phase was extended to Malaysia, Laos, etc. (DREAMSEA, n.d.; PPIM UIN Jakarta, 2026) Peer Review Study named such work “knowledge activism”, emphasizing that manuscript holders are not passive suppliers. (Shofiyanti et al. 2026)

New ecology emerged in the Philippines, Viet Nam and Singapore, “Cool go to colonization + digital environment humanities + localization”. J. Neil C. Garcia introduced the curricular nationalism and queer decolonialities into the Philippine literary classroom in 2024; Fulbright Vietnam established DH Lab and data courses for Vietnamese archives, Hanan literature, photographs, maps, sound and old songs; and NUS has digital environment human thong tables and created Journal of South East Asian Ecocritism. (Garcia, 2024; Fulbright University Vietnam, 2025; Fulbright University Vietnam et al., 2025; National University of Singapore, 2024; JSAE Editorial Department, 2025)
18.2 Representing scholars-institutions-results

rows: Harmonization of manuscripts, authoritative copies, transcriptions, bibliographies and tertiary surveys, representing digital versions rather than general digitization. (Jadavpur University Bichitra team, 2013)
Sessions - cognitive - cultural narrative theory; linking linguistic philosophy, emotions, culture and post-colonial politics. (Nair, 2003)
“De-Canonism Theory, Junūn-Ising Canon” (2024/225, DOI); caste/racial criticism
Challenge the geologic hierarchy of theory from within South Asia and link literature, philosophy, healthy humanities, and non-natural narratives. (Dwivedi, 2024)
The project is based on the creation of anti-Brahmin feminist reading and resistance to life writing by Dalit female testimonios. (Rege, 2006)
Reading Literature in English and English Studies in Bangladesh（2021）
Re-examination of the post-colonial English subject, starting with the teaching, language and classical system in Bangladesh, rather than only studying English novels. (Alam, 2021)
Militarizing Sri Lanka (2007); 2024 Meeting in homage
The study of literature, popular culture, gender, militarization, transitional justice and artistic conflict is linked. (de Mel, 2007; University of Colombia Department of Engineering, 2024)
A History of Pakistan Literature in English 1947-1988 (2015 edition)
The establishment of a baseline of history and bibliography of English literature in Pakistan; its endpoints, 1988 also highlight the time gap that needs to be updated. (Rahman, 2015)
• Digitalization of endangered manuscripts, regional fields, open access, cooperation of holders and research funding. (DREAMSEA, n.d.; PPIM UIN JAKARTA, 2026)

Rewriting the Cool Children Literature Classroom from the bakla and the Philippine colonial history, opposing the use of the Western LGBTQ time line as the only measure. (Garcia, 2024; J. Neil C. Garcia, ed., 2020)
DH Lab；“Digital Humanities in Vietnam Studies Contexts”

Training in literature analysis, image processing and machine learning with Hampan archives, maps, sound, old songs and crowdsourcing. (Nguyyễn Nam Nguyen Nam, n.d.; Fubright University Vietnam, 2025; Fulbright University Vietnam et al., 2025)
The Camphor Tree and the Elephant（2023）
Studying the religious-ecological-human world with family history, prayer manuscripts, folk stories and colonial archives, with 2025 Benda Prize. (Zakaria, 2023)

The academic papers, creations, indigenous knowledge, human-terrestrial communication and post-human education are placed on the same journal platform. (National University of Singapore, 2024; JSAE Editorial, 2025)

blocks.text: 18.3 New theory/method
(a) Dalit testimonio: self-reading as community testimony, counter-archiving and theoretical production, emphasizing who can represent and who has the authority to translate and edit. (Rege, 2006)

To go to classical/Junūn-Class: Dwivedi challenges closed borders of the British-American theory selection with junūn; cases can be considered as “theoretical production from the global South” rather than “application to the South”. (Dwivedi, 2024)

Post-colonial cognitive narratives: Nair combines words, speech behaviour, cultural emotions and cognitive mechanisms to provide a framework between experimental universalization and pure linguisticism. (Nair, 2003)

Online schoolbook (online variorum): use the difference between versions as a data structure; the research unit is a “variant relationship” and not a single text after cleaning. (Jadavpur University Bichitra team, 2013)

Living manuscripts/knowledge activism: digitally simultaneously document holders, religious practices, physical environment and local stories, avoiding the extraction of manuscripts from social life. (DREAMSEA, n.d.; Shofiyanti et al., 2026)

The Cube goes to the colonial classes: the curriculum is rewritten from local gender vocabulary, colonial legal order, nationalism and literature, and is not considered to be “increased visibility” as the only narrative of progress. (Garcia, 2024)

(c) Digital environment public humanities: the database serves both research and the public and citizen environment; the methodology is to audit who can upload, who has data and who is classified as a non-person. (National University of Singapore, 2024)
18.4 Case study
Case SA-A: Bichitra How to Change the Body of “Tagore Works”

Bichitra does not put the full set of PDF online, but rather supports a three-tier comparison of chapters/scene, sections/sections and phrases by hand, periodicals/book versions, verbatim transcriptions, bibliography and proof-reading engines. (Jadavpur University Bichitra Team, 2013) This allows for research on how to change the narrative rhythm; how to reorganize the authorship of Bangla and English self-revision; when a word enters/exits. Its limitations are that the website is largely in the 2014 architecture and that long-term software maintenance, interface opening and new version of access capacity need to be reassessed.
