blocks.text: The number missing is not a single "missing data " . Differing coverage, digital aggregation and overall deviation, and partisan/systematic choices provide a transferable environmental scanning method. (Beelen et al., 2025) The absence of global literature studies also includes performances/orals that are not defined as literature by libraries, languages that do not have standard syntax, local platforms that cannot be hosted on a continuous basis, community knowledge that is not licensed, and results that researchers lack invisibility for language skills. A reliable report should keep “unknown” unknown and fill the world map with blanks in English.
23.5 Transparency agreement: what should be disclosed in a reviewable summary?
The report recommends that a long front-line overview be made available at least as follows:
(a) Date of expiry of information, regional and thematic boundaries;
Multilingual search terms, database/institutional portals, inclusion and exclusion criteria;
(b) Type of source and evidentiary function, without using the project page as a peer review conclusion;
(a) Single reference key, DOI/URL, duplicate record processing;
Full search links, annual scope and access limits for trend data;
Area-thematic scoring rules and prohibited uses;
AI Scope of assistance, version/date, manual or independent review chain;
Three rounds of review, amended records and remaining restrictions.

Recurring the synthesis does not imply that all pages are retrieved or copyrighted text republished. The minimum requirement is to enable readers to re-establish the path of retrieval and judgement and to know clearly where only metadata/summary validation is available, where full text is read, and where the agency is self-presentating. The value of transparency lies in allowing errors to be corrected, not in creating an illusion of “final authority”.
Chapter 24 Six Theory: The Moot of Contemporary Literature Research
24.1 Increased interpretation of calculations or measurable literary pressure

The incorrect version of the argument is “Expected to read against detailed reading”. The real difference is whether literary ideas can be reasonably expressed by variables, whether the language represents the range of issues, and whether model errors are treated as theoretical material. Underwood shows how digital evidence supports literary history; Da strongly questions the statistical and interpretational effectiveness of several computational arguments; and Bode puts the debate back to the object of linguistic construction and literary history. (Underwood, 2019; Da, 2019; Bode, 2023)

The acceptable compromise is not to add text examples to the model results, but to create a cycle: reading into the meso-demography -- Small samples are manually marked - model extensions - error clusters - abnormal text is read back - redefinition of text and frame. If the overall content is unknown, the conclusions should be reduced to “digitized pools”; if the cross-language model is validated in English only, it cannot be called “general literary law”; and if the cluster changes significantly with the parameters, the results should be reported as exploratory.

Criteria for determining whether the calculations have created new literary questions that can be refuted. Is the text and error publicly available? Is there a language/historical expert who explains anomalies? If the answer is no, the scale will only magnify the existing bias.
24.2 LLM is a new reader, co-author, or contaminated database interface

All three types of descriptions may be established at different levels. LLM can simulate some type of reader response, participate in the production of candidate texts, or serve as an unauditable database interface for training memory. Research on physical recognition, story-generation and automatic evaluation shows that models can go beyond old indicators in a given task but are influenced by the memory of popular works, pre-training distribution, self-preference and inadequate interpretation. (Hicke et al., 2025; Tian et al., 2024; Chhun et al., 2024)

The authorship is not a dualistic type. “Man/manual” cannot describe the different controls of tipping, selection, rewrite, translation, editing, publishing and marketing; distributed contributions are not the same as unaccountable. The 2025 report of the United States Copyright Authority could serve as the current boundary of United States law, but did not constitute a global rule and did not answer literary values. (U.S. Copyright Office, 2025) UNESCO ' s report on culture and AI reminds that linguistic homogenization, pay for creators and cultural sovereignty must be integrated into value chain analysis. (UNECO Expert Group on Artificial Intelligence and Culture, 2025)

Guidelines for judgement: Reporting models and dates; detection of pollution from training; use of non-LLM and manual baselines; preservation of all, not selective outputs; rebuilding of the contribution and power chain; separation of “flow” from “understanding”. Any “AI reads a writer” who fails to meet these conditions should be downgraded to a model result.
24.3 Whether the decolonization is a global shared framework or must preserve regional unrelativeability

Tuck and Yang opposed the dilution of decolonization as a general social justice metaphor, Smith put the research approach itself into colonial relations, and Mignolo/Walsh emphasized the link between concepts, analysis and practice. These principles are cross-regionally inspired, but they cannot make all imperial history the same colonial template. The Central and Eastern European debate over the Russian/Soviet, Ukrainian and multiple empires, in particular, called for mechanisms such as the fragmentation of land, population migration, legal differences, language repression, resource transfer, intellectual hierarchies and sovereignty denial.

On the other hand, the emphasis on non-translation can also close alliances or mystify local differences. The viable path is “shared issues, mechanism decomposed, local names”: more similar power mechanisms, while allowing for a different combination of history than self-naming. The key indicator at the practice level is not how much Southern theory is cited in the article, but whether there has been a transfer of power, whether there has been problems, funds, headings, data, signatures, servers and feedback.

Criteria for judgement: Are local scholars/institutions merely providing material? What local language is being studied? Can the Community correct mistakes or refuse to make them public? Where are the benefits of knowledge and occupation going?
24.4 Criticism, post-criticality and “reparative reading”

Sedgwick's distinction between paranoia and restorative reading, Felix's reflection on the interpretation of suspicion has facilitated the “post-critical” discussion; Berlant, Brinkema, Ngai and others have shown that attachment, form intensity and conflicting aesthetic judgements do not amount to withdrawal from politics. (Sedgwick, 2003; Felski, 2020; Berlant, 2011; Brinkema, 2022; Ngai, 2020) The controversy should not be reduced to “discovering power” for “love literature”. Critically, it is good at tracking systems and ideologies, and repair/repension research is good at explaining why readers continue to invest; both can explain the different links of the same chain.

The risk is that “repair” may require the presence of a sensitive and curable story from a marginal subject; that “compensation” may become emotional labour for patients, ethnicized or transsex subjects; and, in turn, that only exposure may pre-empt reading experience to be completely controlled by ideology. The better study also asks: What attachments are made in the text? Who is asked to express them? How does attachment connect to institutional interests? Is rejection, silence or inadaptation a legitimate political issue?

Criteria for judgement: What evidence is differentiated by the criticism and the later criticism? Does the individual ' s feelings directly translate into public effects? Are cases of refusal, failure and failure to repair included?
24.5 Whether world literature is read more widely or is it published globally

Damrosch defines universal literature beyond the original cultural flow and reading; Apter emphasizes non-translation, and Walkowitz proposes “breed translation”, while Cheah understands world literature as a world-generated practice. The increase in the 2020s was the combination of these theories with publishing chains, networks of translators, agents, funding, festivals, platforms and machine translations. The number of languages translated into a work cannot be directly probative; it may reflect market gatekeepers, national cultural policies or marketable forms preferred by transnational groups.

South-South flows cannot be inferred from the mere absence of an English translation. Research needs to rebuild the original language - the intermediary - the target language, the contract, the version, the translation sequence, the deletion, the book review, the curriculum and the reader's network. Translation may involve colonial naming, examination and testimony impairments, or may constitute cultural survival and restoration of relationships. The fact that Ukrainian literature in wartime, the classic Arabic bilingual version, the multilingual system of India, the Korean Network of Translators and Jalada have been translated in a pan-African context suggests that world literature must be studied simultaneously in its form, system and non-translator.
The World's Work is a world-wide reader. Which version is being selected? Is the interpreter and editor visible? Is circulation bypassing or re-dependent on the central language?
24.6 Memory research should serve justice or keep the distance from political mobilization

Rothberg ' s multi-way memory is against zero-sum competition, while the underlying subject theory extends responsibility to benefits, inheritance and structural participation; the hirsch ' s post-rememination emphasizes intergenerational and media transmission. (Rothberg, 2009; Rothberg, 2019; Hirsch, 2012) Platform and AI further involve search, recommendation, automatic alarm, generation of images and chat agents in past generation. (Makhortykh, 2024; Smit et al., 2024) Memory no longer belongs to one community, nor is it only in monuments and books.

Critical distance cannot mean false neutrality between the aggressor and the victim; nor can normative positions exempt sources from verification, conceptual differentiation and protection of internal differences. National memory, traumatic experiences, literary forms, platform dissemination and legal evidence are at different levels. Wartime witness is of immediate value, but may include positioning, minors and risk of trauma re-exposure; national propaganda is the subject of research and not the equivalent of all cultures; and generating memory agents may increase accessibility, or may create or transmit errors between systems.

Criteria for determining who is in memory, through what media, in what system? What was deleted or reduced by algorithms? How can we avoid the re-exposure of witnesses? What sources support the facts and what materials support only memory forms?
24.7 Common solutions to the six-way debate: allocation of evidence

Six arguments were not purely one-sided. Comparability and non-translability, openness and rejection, critical and restorative, computing and interpretation can perform different functions in the same study. The key is "Evidence Configuration":
Making the macro model responsible for discovering distribution and not making it meaningful;
(b) Allow for detailed reading of the forms of interpretation and for the cases to be treated as a whole;
(b) To allow the testing of the intermediate mechanism to be tested so that the biological indicators do not determine their value;
(b) To modify the classification of participants' knowledge so that “participation” is not a pro bono contribution;
(b) Allow laws/policies to define institutional boundaries and not to replace literary theories;
Let non-disclosure become a justified ethical design, while open decision-making paths.
Chapter 25 Trends outlook: 2026-2030
25.1 Methods and confidence of projections

The outlook is not a “prediction of the nextist”, but rather a rebuttable judgement based on the trends in metadata 2010-2025, publications 2020-2026, project infrastructure, the proliferation of methods and public issues. Three tranches for confidence:
High: There are three types of indicators of results, institutions and methodologies in multiple regions and there are persistent drivers.
Medium height: Cross-thematic convergence and institutional nodes exist, but regional coverage or methodological norms are not yet uniform.
Medium: Agenda signals are visible, early cases are credible, and institutionalization depends on funding, ethics or replication.
Any projection is accompanied by a counter-argument: if key indicators do not appear, the judgement should be modified rather than the projection retained in vague language.
25.2 Seven Trends and Counter-Indicators

rows: AI literature research from production to training spectrum, copyright, measurement effectiveness, labour and energy auditing

Model memory, automatic evaluation, copyright and cultural governance have developed continuous evidence (Hicke et al., 2025; Chhun et al., 2024; U.S. Copyright Office, 2025; UNESCO Group of Experts on Artificial Intelligence and Culture, 2025)
Model disclosure norms in literary journals; non-English/out-of-training test ratios; and improved transparency in training
Multilingual programming materials become a basic infrastructure for the history of transnational literature, maintenance and language work become bottlenecks
There is a network of TEI, API, Knowledge Mapping and Versions in the Multi-Sectoral Area; lack of research maturity (Fiormonte et al., 2022; Beelen et al., 2025; Koeser et al., 2025)
Long-term funding, version sustainability, language coverage, signature of contribution and local governance
Moving from subject labels to courses, classifications, data, servers, publications and career returns audits
Co-mobilization of indigenous methods, data feminism, Southern theory and regional projects (D'Ignazio et al. 2020; Smith, 2021; Mignolo et al., 2018; Ahmad, 2023)
Whether funding/governance changes are in fact made; whether local language results and co-authors are available; whether concepts are generalized into empty brands
Environmental humanities and health, energy, memory, indigenous research and AI infrastructure are further integrated
Planets, radial geology, pollution, sensory and empirical ecological criticisms have crossed (Chakrabarty, 2021; Liboiron, 2021; Schneider-Mayerson, 2023; Yusoff, 2024)
Whether short-term attitude tables and scientific decision theory are avoided; whether community governance data are avoided
Medical humanities are moving from “dissemination training” to neurodiversity, disability, institutional violence and co-production
Critical medical humanities, crip/trans research and image medicine formation methodological chain (Woods, 2011; Viney et al., 2015; Czerwiec et al., 2025; Sterne, 2022)
The question of whether the experiential is co-signed/decision; whether the outcome is still measured only by “sync promotion”
War, climate and platform vulnerability contribute to the independent norm of “emergency humanities/emergency digital humanities”
Regional cases and loss of archives, such as SUcho, provide a strong signal.
Whether to create sensitivity hierarchy, local governance, long-term hosting and ethical standards
The cognitive literature has moved from short-term stimulation and single brain imaging to physical, predictive, long textual, intercultural multi-methodics
Neural cognitive poetry, predictive processing, eye motion and natural reading provide pathways (Jacobs 2015, Kukkonen 2020, Corcoran et al., 2023)
Increased pre-registration, cross-language reproduction, long text assignments, negative results and effectiveness

blocks.text: 25.3 Design of 15 directly translated papers/topics
Topic 1: Multilingual LLM Stigma Blindness

Classic, copyright-based and locally published versions are selected from three languages in common, with layers of original/manual/machine translations; name mask, spectrosm, traditional taxonomy and expert blind reviews. The main result is training memory and cross-verbal error rather than a single accuracy rate. (Hicke et al., 2025; Li, 2019)
Topic 2: Global production of literary archives (1950s-now)

Collect codes, output, equipment, author interviews, performances and re-enactments, distinguishing rules, randomness, combinations, statistics and large model phases. The absence of records by language/area to avoid writing English electronic literature into the entire history of machine literature. (Bertram et al. 2024; Ernst et al. 2012)
Topic 3: Missing Maps in the Historical Newspaper

Create a digital newspaper for a country/language against a reference catalogue, with a hierarchy of cities, parties, languages, age, layout and OCR errors; judge the range of literary history that can be answered with an Envirronmental Scan, rather than treating undigitized as unpublished. (Beelen et al., 2025)
Topic 4: The history of literature in uncertain times

Presumed publication/creation dates are kept in compartments, using undate or Monte Carlo sensitivity analysis to assess whether trend findings are stable under different date allocations; interpretation of uncertainty itself is of the version and the history of the archives. (Koeser et al., 2025)
Topic 5: Delays in climate novels Reactions

Use random reading conditions, instant/January/June tracking, open interviews and behavioural indicators to distinguish narrative transport, emotions, knowledge, intentions and actual actions; pre-processing leakage and self-reporting. (Schneider-Mayerson et al., 2023)
Topic 6: Island-mine-translation network

Connect Caribbean/Pacific Islands text, colonial mapping, mineral supply chain, translation and publishing network; local researchers determine the openness of sensitive sites and knowledge. (DeLoughey, 2019; Yusoff, 2024)
Topic 7: Non-extracted numbers of Southern movement text Version

Work with communities to deal with debris, anonymity, oral and underground printing; establish TEI, access hierarchy, withdrawal, collective author and local copy; study how the archives structure constitutes theory, not just preservation. (Ahmad, 2023)
Topic 8: Whether South-South translation is bypassed by English

The tracking of contracts, copyright pages, translators' interviews, grants, book reviews and courses, identifying direct translation, intermediate language and reprints; web-based maps must be validated with version evidence and cannot be inferred from the lack of English records in the database alone. (Debjani Ganguly (ed. ), 2021; Li, 2019)
Topic 9: Algorithms and literature rereading

Research on how recommended systems can shape readouts, fan feelings, fatigue and oblivion, using digital journals, Platform data donations, interviews and format analysis; selection deviations and privacy controls for platform users. (Berlant, 2011; Seigworth et al, 2023)
Topic 10: Interlingual poetry for eye reproduction

The multicentre pre-registration is based on the assumption that layout, length and familiarity are matched in different languages; combined with eye moves, open memories and fine reading, the retrospect/suspensive effect is tested for reproduction. (Corcorán et al., 2023)
Topic 11: Transgender Internet at risk files

Research early forums, home pages and terminology history, using community counselling, identity risk ranking and default secrecy; and documentation of the corruption of links, platform migration and re-entry risks. (Dame-Griff, 2023)
Topic 12: Platform-based trauma memory

Long-term observation of how auto-alerts, maturations, chat agents and family albums change memory; recording changes in interface versions and models, obtaining multiple consents from relatives to avoid secondary trauma. (Hirsch, 2012; Smit et al., 2024)
Topic 13: AI Vertical Audit of Historical Responses

Establish a multilingual dispute history group, which will be tested quarterly for different models, preserving sources, rejecting answers, errors and system-wide transmission; and verify by historians/local experts that the in camera harms the hate content of the group again. (Makhortykh, 2024)
Topic 14: Co-creation of Image Medicine

To enable patients, clinicians and cartoon writers to decide on issues and versions, to keep logs of the process and to provide feedback against teaching and quality; to make it clear that the work is not a diagnosis or treatment commitment. (Czerwiec et al., 2025)
Topic 15: Racialization of synthetic sound and hearing

(c) Blind listening and interviewing under conditions that control acoustic characteristics, combined with interface history, synthetic service labour and performance analysis; results are used to reveal how classification is formed and not to reinforce the biological essence. (Stoever, 2016; Eidsheim, 2019; Faber, 2020)
25.4 Post-graduate development and course re-engineering
Future research and training should not completely separate “theory” and “tool” classes. It is proposed that four cross-cutting organizations be organized:
How the object is formed: audit language, version, archive, platform and missing.
How does the methodology claim to know: draw a chain of material - variables/concepts - conclusions and find a counter-proof?
Who bears the cost of knowledge: discuss translation/marking/care labour, copyright, data sovereignty and environmental costs.
What should not be fully public: design hierarchical visits, withdrawals, local copies and long-term maintenance.

Each graduate's introductory material adds a page entitled “Evidents and Ethical Passports”: research issues, material spectrum, language skills, source clearance, method effectiveness, negative examples, data/code availability, sensitive information, co-authorship and maintenance schemes. The passport is more able to judge the feasibility of the project than “how many new theories/software have been used”.
25.5 Five proposals for action in the Chinese literature

(c) To build accessible, accessible, removable infrastructure. Project evaluations cannot be viewed online alone, but need to include data dictionary, clearance, API/export, version, long-term hosting and error correction mechanisms.

Multilingual skills are included in research contributions. Translation, mapping, home design and local knowledge should not be used as invisible auxiliary labour; a CrediT-type contribution role could be used.

Establish minimum disclosure standards for AI literary research. Models/dates, tips, repetitions, training of pollution, manual editing, baselines, copyrights and privacy should be part of the methodology for the paper.

Promoting direct interregional dialogue. China ' s research can be compared with African Water Theory, Latin American Extracting Zone, South Asian Castes/Peeries, Middle East Text Re-use and Central and Eastern Europe Emergency Human Development, without all being brokered by Anglo-American theory.

Distinguishing autonomous intellectual production from closed indigenousism. Autonomy should be reflected in the ability to raise testable questions, to generate shared concepts, to speak with multilingual evidence and to accept rebuttals, rather than rejecting international approaches or replacing academic reasoning with policy slogans.
25.6 Conclusion: the front line is a research responsibility

The most important common change in literature research in the 2020s is not the replacement of one word for another, but the responsibility of researchers for how objects appear, how evidence is formed, how concepts travel, how models are biased, how communities are affected and how results are sustained. The front line is therefore not “the most fashionable topic”, but research practice that can raise new issues, create reversible evidence, preserve non-conformity, allow counter-proofing and change intellectual relations.

A truly global literary study does not create a smooth map of the world. It should show where the information is informative, where the index is hidden, where the concepts are comparable, where differences must be maintained, where they should be open and where they should be rejected. It neither abandons reading, history and theory nor sees computing, experimentation and infrastructure as external tools; it binds them to each other, making local knowledge a source of concepts and recognizing maintenance, translation, care and error as core academic work.

blocks.text: B.2 Minimum-reporting list
[ ] The issue, target, time, regional and linguistic boundaries of the study are clear.
[ ] Description of the location and language skills of the researchers; the cross-language project includes local/linguistic experts.
[ ] The source, version, search date, licence and NPC may be rebuilt.
[ ] Description of the digitalization/OCR/platform/archives missing and their direction.
[ ] There is a clear correspondence between the theoretical concept and the variable/coding.
[ ] At least one counter-example, alternative interpretation or negative examination.
[ ] The calculation studies are open baselines, cut-offs, errors and parameter sensitivity.
[ ] LLM studies open models, dates, tips, repetitions, pollution and manual editing.
[ ] Experimental research on open samples, efficacy, irritation, randomization and extrapolation of boundaries.
[ ] Participatory research on public consent, remuneration/signature, withdrawal, feedback and governance.
[ ] Sensitive material explains why it is open, restricted, vague or undisclosed.
[ ] Clear code/data/material availability and long-term maintenance responsibilities.
[ ] The language of the conclusions corresponds to the strength of the evidence; the “first/single/global lead” without the evidence is not used.
Appendix C. Hotspot coding and trend data methodology
C.1 Retrieving, inclusion and evidence functions

The report uses multilingual evidence mapping (purposive evidence mapping), which aims to identify nodes that can change the problem, concept, methodology or discipline infrastructure, rather than estimating the incidence of a subject in all publications worldwide, and therefore does not claim to be consistent with an exhaustive system overview in the sense of PRISMA. As of 11 July 2026, the search was followed by universities, research institutes, societies, periodicals, publishers, data platforms and projects in nine regions, followed by scholars, results, DOI, bibliographical and cooperative networks. The search combination uses a combination of short/hard Chinese, English and operational French, German, Spanish, Portuguese, Japanese, Korean, Russian, Ukrainian, Polish, Arabic, Indonesian and Vietnamese institutions/topics; only metadata confirmation is done for materials that cannot be independently judged, and priority is given to original pages of local scholars or institutions.

Integration needs to be simultaneously satisfied: directly related to the subject, theory, methodology, archives or system of literary research; at least DOI, official publisher/studies page, university/student/project original page can be validated; it can account for theoretical flows or progress in the 2020s; regional chapters prioritize local knowledge production and co-governance rather than the local location of research objects. Searches of clips, conflicting and irrefutable materials, purely commercial propaganda and only future forecasts without clear subjects of responsibility were excluded. The meeting, course, project release and annual report only prove the agenda/infrastructure and do not accept peer-reviewed papers to support the content conclusions.

blocks.text: The text actually calls 254 source keys; four low-Incremental Research entries are not in the body. The aliases that point to the same work in the cross-module are merged at the end of the text, so that the final reference is 243 work-level records. The text author - The hidden source key after the year quote is used for machine close-up checks, which is not shown in DOCX/PDF; the key - Bibliography - Verifys the contrast. The design improves traceability but is still influenced by language skills, open networks, availability of collections and project self-expression.
C.2 The thematic evidence code for Sector 11

Each cell in the regional heat map uses 1-5 integers: 1= present round evidence weak; 2= credible case; 3= up or nodes in the 2020s; 4= continuous structure and multiple results; 5= extremely strong, multityped and methodological/theory original cluster in the current round of evidence. The ratings are derived from the publication/topics, institutions/infrastructure, innovations in methods, cross-regional proliferation and public issues signals in the regional archives.

Prohibited uses: No total regional score shall be calculated; no actual production, quality or influence of the zone shall be called; no one-point assertion of “no research” in the area shall be used. The visibility, maturity and conceptual breadth of information vary from subject to theme. All 99 cells retain explanation and source keys, with fraction values for navigation and not statistical extrapolation.
C.3 OpenAlex trend data

Data source: Open Alex Works API; grab dates 2026-07-11; annual years 2010-2025; and search title and summary using an exact English phrase for each theme. A total of 11 x 16 = 176 lines, with annual consecutive, non-duplicative, non-negative integers. OpenAlex is an ongoing index of the index, with a potential change in the compound value. (Priem et al. 2022)

The search labels are: digital humanities, AI-generated legalization, environmental humanities, statistical studies, world legalization, effectorty, cognitive legal studies, transport studies, memolly studies, medical humanities, sound studies.

Limitations include: English-language label bias; narrow labels; missing title/summary; non-inclusion of adjacent concepts; possible cross-theme of the same outcome; still lag in indexing; and count not grouped by full-base size or discipline share. The chart therefore uses 11 separate vertical maps, without an absolute ranking of the inter-thematic values and not an incomplete 2026.
Appendix D. Index of accomplishments for scholars-institutions (selected)

This table is a bilingual navigation into the body of deep reading and original sources, not an impact ranking. Names are preferred to be in the complete form of a public statement by the author, university or publisher; the authoritative source expressly retains the official signature when providing only the initial letter or Roman script, without conversely speculating on the middle name or source text. The institutions and universities are also listed in Chinese full-title and full-language official languages; the results are linked to DOI, publisher, university or project official pages.

blocks.text: Full entity name, source language name, romanization of norms, version of works, evidence keys and stable links are set out in appendix F; separate methods, disputes and applicable boundaries are provided in the main chapters.
Appendix E Information, Reproduction and AI Transparency Declaration
E.1 Information and codes

The research area maintains the following machine-readable materials: six consolidated fields, a list of additional sources of information on the regions, themes and topics, with 325 source records, 314 heavy works, and 2010-2025 OpenAlex trend data, thematic scores and case-by-case descriptions of the 11th of the Ninth Region, lists of authority of entities, graphic production scripts, quotations and the construction and rendering of DOCX scripts. Dynamic URLs are based on the information deadline; some pages may be updated or invalidated at a later date.
E.2 AI assistance in disclosure

The report is supported by Codex (GPT-5 series, 2026-07): search design, cross-language source discovery, metadata collation, summary of evidence, cross-regional synthesis, organization of drafts, graphic generation, validation of quotation/structure machines, language editing and DOCX layout. AI is not listed as an academic author; the report clearly discloses its role because the generation of the model may combine sources, miscount years, over-capture or misprove fluid expressions.

Risk control includes: prioritizing DOI, publisher, periodical, university/institution and project original pages; maintaining source keys aligned to text; checking duplicate keys, DOI, URL and works versions; distinguishing the claim that evidence can be assumed by full text, abstract/publishing notes, agency pages; setting dates and status for dynamic facts; and conducting a complete check of Word pages, tables, images, directories and hyperlinks. Users should still read the original source and comply with the AI disclosure policy of their institution before citing specific points in their paper, course or subject application.
E.3 Ethical judgement

The report is a summary of public information, and the IRB/EC review does not normally apply without the collection of data on human subjects and without the inclusion of private identifiers. The dual-use risk is low. In relation to First Nations knowledge, cross-gender files, disease/disability narratives, trauma testimony and wartime information, the report does not provide operational information that would lower the threshold of harm and continues to emphasize relationship consent, hierarchical access, withdrawal, ambiguity of location and local governance.

In terms of equity, the report distinguishes between “local knowledge production” and “extraterritorial research” and does not automatically regard large English-language projects as regional representatives; and between source terminology, factual metadata, theoretical interpretations and judgements of the report on controversial issues such as Palestine, Ukraine/Russia, decolonization, gender and copyright in AI. Area blanks are written as search/visibility limits, not academic absences.
E.4 Conflict of interest and liability

The report is not funded by institutions, publishers, model suppliers or projects, nor is it listed for business purposes. The AI tool, provided by OpenAI, may result in language and visibility deviations; therefore, model outputs are only used as auditable material and cross-checked with non-supplier original sources, legal or international organization materials and independent academic results. Responsibility for the amendment and interpretation of factual errors rests with the author and the end user of the report.
Appendix F General Index of the Entity's List of Authority and the Stabilization of Works

This appendix is generated by re-run generators from source matrices and entity seeds. It only collates available and traceable metadata and does not use algorithms to guess Chinese names, middle names, Arabic/Silier spellings or statutory names of publishing institutions.
I. ENTITLED NORMS AND INLIMINATION

(b) Person, institution and university: the core entity only includes records of the verification status of the torrent list beginning with VERIFIEED; the full Chinese, full source language, and the romanization and stabilization link are exported as the seed.

Works: One for each independent work; first, do weight with DOI, and then use canonical key when no, then run it by the title-year. Multiple proof keys are merged into aliases when pointing to the same work, but the original key is not lost.

Author signature: First, you can use the proven person feed to accurately expand the identifiable aliases, then you can keep the entire author/author string and additional symbols of the source matrix. The signature of the norm, if it remains initial, is marked only by exceptions and is not based on experience; the text is marked as author.

Title of work: Show the full original title contained in the source matrix; this field may be the source language title, the official English title, or the bilingual title provided by the source itself. No authoritative record is available to create a Chinese translation or source text.

Links and publishing institutions: Link priority DOI, sub-publishing/renewal/university library/project official page. The publication agency only automatically carries out the test seeds when they are capable of creating a non-misterial correspondence; the official brand, the intangible abbreviations and the advance copies of untraditional publishing houses retain a clear boundary statement.
Data range: 95 seeds, 86 certified and 9 not included; 325 source records, which are classified as 314 independent works, and 11 aliases consolidated.

blocks.text: IV. Description of the initial letter of name and metadata boundary

The phrase “use the full name” is not the same as the introduction of the initial name with common sense. If DOI, publisher, author ' s home page or university authoritative records are signed only in the form of N. Katherine Hayles, Arthur M. Jacobs, J. Logan Smilges, the index retains the signature of the norm and includes it in the following table. Only when authoritative records of author level appear may the body of the body form be carried out separately. Public career names (e.g. MK Czerwiec) are likewise not authorized to be expanded.
This consensus is divided into 29 original authors/authors, covering 31 independent works.

blocks.text: 4.2 Conservative treatment of the name of the publishing institution

When a proven seed is able to match the abbreviation, the index is expanded to the full name of the official source language in the seed; otherwise, the official published brand or abbreviation contained in the source matrix is retained. The long-forms of brand names may not necessarily exist, so that BMJ, SAGE, Brill, Routledge etc. cannot be translated literally into the statutory name of the company. A preprint or project output, if there is no traditional publisher, is an express carrier.

This batch has 14 works that trigger the “official brand/abbreviation as it is” or “no traditional publisher” border marker; they can be screened in machine-readable CSV’s publicisher name status field by article.

sources.title: Overview of the global literary research frontier
sources.filename: Overview of the global literary research frontier.dc (v5).html

blocks.text: Global literature research, frontier studies. Description
Summary

The report systematically summarizes the latest developments in global literary research from 2023 to 2026, taking into account the theoretical trends. The report identifies a five-heavy paradigm shift -- digital and artificial intelligence shifts, ecological and planetary shifts, decolonization and multilingual shifts, emotional and physical shifts, methodological reconstruction -- - and along two axes: one is the front line of eleven themes (Data Humanities and Computation Literature Research, AI and Literature, Environmental Humanities, Post-colonial and Global South, World Literature and Translation Studies, Internacional Theory and Post-Critics, Cognitive and Empirical Studies, Gender and Cool, Memory and Trauma Research, Medical Humanities, Voice and Media Research), and the second is the academic map of nine regions (North America, Europe, mainland China and Hong Kong, Japan, South and South-East Asia, Latin America, Africa, Middle East and Arab World, Russia and Central and Eastern Europe). Each chapter contains theoretical elements, in-depth readings representing scholars and outcomes, typical case and contentious assessments, supported by comparative tables, spectrographs, time lines and data charts. The report concludes with six major debates and trends outlooks of 2026-2030, with a glossary of terms, a directory of periodicals and societies and references.
Preparation of notes
Scope

The time frame is dominated by the latest developments of 2023-2026, while at the same time providing the necessary retroactives to the theoretical flows to which each party had come (generally going back to the 1990s, individually and earlier). The subject is broadly "literary research", including literary theory and criticism, comparative literature and universal literature, national literary research, and the intersection of literature and science, medicine, environment and media; linguistics, pure history and communication dynamics are addressed only when they are directly related to literary research.
Information base and methodology

Sources include: representative academic monographs and papers; major Institute annual conferences themes (MLA, ACLA, ASEES, CDH, etc.); flagship journal creation and album dynamics; major awards and public events; and public reports and studies in various linguistic communities; and web access up to July 2026. In practice, scholars and terms are first introduced in English or in the original language; the writing is marked with a translation (original or English name, year); and the original is translated and retained by a person who does not have a common translation. Where the data in the text are estimated or there are caliber differences, the source and limit are indicated below the chart.
Limitations

No global overview can be exhaustive: the dynamics of non-universal linguistic communities may lag behind, mainly through the intermediary of English and Chinese literature; the selection of "frontiers" inevitably carries the judgement of the editor; and the conclusions of fast-evolving areas such as artificial intelligence are more time-bound. Users may wish to view this report as a research map and clue index rather than a substitute for the findings of the first-hand literature.
Contents

First the whole picture, then the difference. The title summarizes the global climate of the 2020s in terms of "the five-fold shift" and provides a methodological map that provides a grid for the thematic and regional scanning of more than 20 chapters.
Paradigm shift: the five-fold shift in global literature research in the 2020s

Institutional contraction coincided with the expansion of knowledge: on the one hand, the admission and funding crisis in the humanities of Europe and the United States, and on the other hand, the unprecedented diversity of theory, methods and subjects of research. Understanding this "crisis-prosperity paradox" is the first key to the current literary research.

Global literary research has shown a enchanting paradox over the past decade. On the one hand, the United States-represented humanities complex has been under constant pressure: according to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences Humanistic Indicators project, the number of bachelors in American literature awards fell by about a quarter from their peak in 2012 to 2022, with English-language specializations contracting more frequently at several higher education institutions; in the United Kingdom, Australia and elsewhere, institutional retrenchments have also taken place. On the other hand, the production of knowledge in literary research has never been more dynamic: new publications, new societies, new methods and new subjects, and the problem perception of literary researchers spills over to public topics such as climate, medicine, technology, race and memory politics. "Landology Research is nothing more than "Landology is everywhere."

The present report summarizes the overall changes during this period in a five-fold shift. They are not separate streams, but entanglements: digital tools change the evidence patterns of ecological criticism, go to colonial agendas to reshape the world literature, and emotional research provides a vocabulary for medical humanities, while methodological warfare cuts across all fields.
1.1 Quintular turn

First, numbers and artificial intelligence shifts. From Franco Moretti's "distant reading" to "cultural analysis", the calculation method has been completed from marginal experiments to the institutionalization of conventional options; The outbreak of the Big Language Model (LLM) since 2022 has divided the agenda in two-- Models are used as tools (for labelling, translation, style analysis) and as objects (critical AI research, literary production, "synthetic text" age author theory). Chapters 3, 4.

Second, the ecological and planetary shifts. The "Anthropocene" became the interdisciplinary framework for the overall problem, and ecological criticism became the sub-area of blue humanities, energy, plant research, multi-species research, etc.; The concept of the planet of Dippesh Chakrabarty promotes the re-establishment of the literary historical scale. Chapter 5

Third, decolonization and multilingualism. Decolonisation has expanded from a theoretical platform of Latin American schools to a global, self-provincial movement of subjects - curriculum reform, orthodox re-evaluation, "de-sciplining"; This was accompanied by criticism of English centrality and the rise in the status of multilingual research and translation studies. Chapters 6 and 7 are detailed.

Four, moving with body. The theory of emotionalism (afffect theory) is a common analytical expression, which is combined with medical humanities, disability research, trauma and memory research, creating a community of research centred on body, feeling and care. Chapters 8, 10, 11, 12 are detailed.

Five, the method is reconstruction. The post-critical debate around how to read after criticalism extends to a general reflection on the knowledge patterns of literary research: Jonathan Kramnick, John Gillory and others re-created the basis for "detailed reading" and "criticism" and empirical and computational camps demanded testable evidentiary standards. Chapters 2, 8, 24 are detailed.

Figure 1-1. Five-fold shift and its association 1 Figures and AI Distance Reading Cultural Analytics → Critical AI; Dualisation of Tools and Objects2 Ecology and Planets General Framework for Human Life; Blue/Energy/Multi-Species; Scale Re-set 3 to Colonization and Multilingual Synthetics Reevaluation; Curriculum Reform; Translation and Multilingual Research Evaluation 4 Universalization of Intersension and Physical Complication; Medical Humanities, Disability, Caring Flow 5 Differences in Method Rebuilding Read and write the foundation; argue for the standard of proof, the concept and method of five barlines of mutual supply; and the impact of tracking any one item alone is underestimated.
1.2 Overview of the global landscape

The five-fold shift is not evenly visible across regions. North America is the epicentre of methodological warfare and critical AI research. Europe has long been studying empirical aesthetics, computing literature and memory; digital humanities, cyberliteracy and science fiction in Chinese science are self-sized and are accompanied by the construction of the "autonomous knowledge system"; Japan and Korea have been prominent in the classical AI "worldization" of reading and literature; South Asia, Latin America, Africa, the Middle East are the source of theories on the decolonisation, multilingualism and ecological agenda. The Slavic academic community is going through the most profound self-liquidation in decades after the war in Russia and Ukraine. Table 1-1 gives a summary of the quick-checking, which is detailed in Part III for each region.

blocks.text: 1.3 Three Overall Observations

The agenda is converged and the path is differentiated. Figures, ecology, decolonisation appear on hotspot lists in almost all regions, but the way things are shaped is heavily constrained by the geography: the same "decolonization" , Latin America's colonialization towards power, Africa's focus on language and publishing sovereignty, and the Slavic academic community means liquidation of Russian centralism.

Theoretical production multipolarity. Theories no longer export from Europe and the United States in a single direction: Brazilian "screviv-survival" (escreviv-nencia), Mexican "desapropación" (desapropiación), South African "hydrocolonialism" (hydrocoliaism), Chinese "varicology" and "hidden process" are now in circulation internationally.

Institutional pressures are globalized. The pressure on enrolment, funding contraction and evaluation systems is no longer unique to the United States; It has also led to the expansion of "interfacing disciplines" such as public literature, creative writing and medical humanities, and the formation of literary research is being reorganized.
Methodological map: from fine reading to computation, from interpretation to empirical evidence

The methodological chart of contemporary literature research can be carried out along two axes: the scale of the object (detailed reading of a single text, long-term reading of a large volume of literature) and the orientation of knowledge production (explaining, empirical). Most new methods can be located on this map.
2.1 Method Pocket

Figure 2-1 Places the principal method family in the same coordinates. It is important to emphasize that the range is a positioning tool, and that the front line is more at the pictogramal intersection — for example, the computation of the critics' statistical model to test the subject of the subject, the empirical aesthetic team to study the hymn effect of a single poem in experimental design, and book historians to read each other's published data and case.

Figures 2-1 • The study of the effects of the visual limitation of contemporary literary research • Evidentiary evidence • Calculation of the direction of monotext • Elementary reading • Empirical aesthetics • Neurism • Experiment of ophthalmology/Emporal reading • Empirical criticism – Cultural analysis – Distance measurement and author affiliation – Cultural analysis • LLM Auxiliary labels and modelling • Platform reading data research – Large-scale – Exam reading – New formalism of re-advocacy of reading – Table/Descriptive reading – Post-critical reading – Critical criticism • Critical fiction (archiving) systems and Circulation Research World System of Literature – Translation and Literature Social Studies – World Ecoanalysis – Translating, Translating Studies, and Research – Knowledge-Kiging, and Learning Research Questation – and Research Quoting, and Knowledge-Scre. The borderline (e.g. "experimentally read the puzzle") is the fastest growing area.
2.2 Overview of emerging approaches

Table 2-1 presents a summary of the research methodology that has been in the mainstream for almost a decade, by "Methodologies - Core Issues - Representing Scholars - Representing Results". These include both European and American access and non-English-language original methods (such as mutation, hidden processes) that are being internationalized.

2.4 Consensus and Differences in Methodological Approaches

Over the camp, three points of convergence can be observed: first, there is a general increase in the awareness of evidence. And whether it's a calculator or an interpreter, it's more than 20 years ago that you answered "What is your argument based on?" This is a common legacy of the war on Dalitism and the post-criticism. Second, "size" becomes the core. From a single text to a million volumes of language material, from ethnolitarian literature to planetary scales, researchers are forced to switch sides between scales and to explain the rationale for the scale selection. Third, the expansion of the research audience. Platform literature (webLiction, Wattpad, BookTok), text generation, audio and podcasts, translation of circulation data, material form of books have become the subject of regular research.

The differences are equally clear: whether or not the calculations answer the interpretation (chap. 24, War I); whether critical reading should remain the default core of training (war II); whether empirical results are binding on literary theory; and whether the most acute LLM products after 2023 are legitimate objects of literary research. These tensions constitute the background of the subsequent chapters.

Eleven themes constitute hot spots for contemporary literary research. Each chapter is based on "Theory Source - Recent Developments - Scholars Read In-depth - Case Focus - Disputes and Perspectives", and is accompanied by a list of participants and results.
Studies in digital humanities and computational literature

After more than 20 years of "exploration" and a famous battle of fermentation, studies of computing literature have moved from slogans to the conventional science stage: professional journals, funding schemes, reusable data sets and methods. The Big Language Model is rewriting its toolbox and research subjects.
3.1 From "exploration" to cultural analysis: institutionalization completed

The source is clear: Franco Moretti's 2000 book "Conjectures on World Literature" proposes to address the scale of world literature by "exploration", whose Charts, Maps, Trees, 2005) and the Stanford Literature Laboratory (2010) series "Pamphlets" set out the style of a graphic reflection on the history of literature; Matthew Jockers, Macroanalysis, 2013 introduced the term "macroanalyss." Andrew Piper launched the Journal of Cultural Analysis at McGill University in 2016, with a flagship position for peer review in the marking area.

The key word for the 2020s is institutionalization. The German Research Union (DFG) has established the priority project "Calculating Literature Research" (SP 2207) to finance two clusters of research from 2020; The Evelyn Gius team of the University of Damstadt Industrial created the Journal of Computation Literature in 2022; Fotis Janidis of Witzburg and Christof Schöch of Trier promoted the standardization of metrology and author attribution. On the Chinese side, Digital Manpower (Chonghua University, 2020) and Digital Manpower Research (Chonghua University, 2021) were created in succession, and the Chinese Annual Digital Manpower Conference (CDH) was held in consecutive years since 2019; The Chinese Institute of Literature of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences published the Chinese Literature Digital Human Development Report in 2025, which has combing the 40-year veins into three stages of "Diculating the Documentation - Measurement of Documentation and Measurement of the Humanistics - Calculation of Criticism", and has pointed out that classical literature is the most accepted area.
3.2. Self-consciousness in the way a battle of theory left behind

In 2019, Nan Z. Da published his " Computational Rebution to Computation Literature Research " in Critical Inquiry, which counted well-known studies, asserting that their statistical assumptions were either erroneous or merely complicated the obvious common sense. The long-term effect of this debate is not to win, but to raise standards: data and codes are becoming a common practice; Pepper, Can We Be Wrong?, 2020, introduces the hypothetical test and the "extensibility" framework; Katherine Bode, A World of Book, 2018, emphasizes the historical construction of the language itself — you never counted "Liction" but a digitized sample.

Underwood is the most important model of the history of literature with machine learning. Distant Horizons: Digital Vision and Limited Change, 2019) based on hundreds of thousands of books from HathiTrust, proposing "perspectivaval modeling": not to let algorithms declare truth, but to make models represent a historical perspective and calibrate with human judgement. The survey of English fiction by her colleagues shows that in the middle of the 19th century, female authors accounted for about half of the story, and in the middle of the 20th century, the share declined sharply — an impression that it was almost impossible to discover and subvert the discovery of the narrative of “linear progress”. Since 2023, he has been the first to demonstrate LLM as a "cultural model": it compresses practices and preferences in the big text and is therefore both a research tool and a new object of literary research.
3.3 LLM Age: Dual expansion of toolboxes and objects

After 2023, three new lines emerged in the study of computing literature. One, LLM as a label and modelling tool. After the BookNLP and LitBank data sets of David Bamman's team, researchers began to use LLM to complete complex labels of character relations, narrative perspectives, and plot functions, while the recently introduced NarraBech framework by Piper and others attempted to create a system for "specify benchmarking." Two, LLM as the subject of the study. The Berman team's Archaeology study in 2023 found out which novels were remembered (the copyrighted text is in the list); the study "Lictal Scroll" that systematically measured the mainstream model at the 2025 Humanities Conference: its recommendations and continuations were highly biased towards white Western male authors - the model itself became a new file of orthodox sociology. Third, the platform reads data. The Federico Piantola and others study of the "digital social reading" of platforms such as Wattpad and AO3 turned millions of readers' comments into microscopes for the research.

The path of Chinese science is different: infrastructure and classical literature are the main focus. The literary writing year map of the Wang Ying team, the "knowledgeization" of the ancients advocated by Liu Shih (Chonghua) and the "Chinese classical intellectual engineering" project, the reassessment of the Zhijbong by Li for the age-old poetry system of the megamodel model, the computational criticism of Zhao Wei (China Institute of Social Sciences) and the practice of the "Labour of Arts" form the complete chain from resource construction to the means of criticalisation (see chapter 16 for more details).

The "Dang Song Literature Book Map" (online in 2017) hosted by Wang Ying Peng, binds author tracks, works and historical geographic information, and users can see where poets are and what poems they write in any given year, one of the digital humanities platforms most influential in Chinese literature; Its successor, the National Fund for Social Science, has advanced the timeline of its major project, "Building the Han Wei Six-Year Map Platform for Literature." His methodological argument is "Dual-directional Interaction of Digital Technology with Human Research": both the bibliography and the text of the work can be digitized, and the key is to transform the fragmentation information into structural, relevant data, so researchers can see the hidden relationships that were previously unobservable -- • Quantitative and qualitative aspects are thus complete rather than substitutes for each other.

Richard Jean So, McGill, "Culturing Redline: a Data History of Post-war Novel and Racial Inequality " , analyses the novels of leading United States publishers since 1950, and finds that the share of white authors has remained at 95 per cent for the long term, with reviews, awards and collections being as highly skewed as in the case of collections; Visible reports published by him and his collaborators in The New York Times have brought this conclusion into public discussion. The methodological model is "mixed scale": first, to establish structural facts by statistical means, then to go back to cases like Toni Morrison, and show how structures leave traces at the sentence level. The book became the standard for the combination of "calculation + critical race studies" and responded to the question that "calculation only proves common sense" - - 95% of this number has never been proven before.

blocks.text: 3.4 Synopsis of the methodology toolbox

For the primary users, the current conventional tool chain is roughly five layers. The following are the following: The author's attribution analysis of the "fingerprint" with functional word frequency (Berros Delta distance as a common standard, stylo R package as a universal realization), and the use of the stylist and time styles; The symmetry: The relationship is transformed from thematic model (LDA) to word vector and context embedded to underpin conceptual history and semantic change studies; The narrative: BookNLP, Litbank, a flowline extraction character, event and quotation, LLLMM is taking over the most labour-intensive links; Network analysis: The character composing network, the letter and publishing house-translator network, turning "relation" into an detectable object; The literary geography: Geographical Names Identification and GIS (the Chinese label for the Wang Mixen team's annual map). Community norms are synchronized: data and code opening, text-based description, and visible discussion of the archive "reviolation of survivors" have become defaults for contributions to major journals.
3.5 Disputes and perspectives

The outstanding issues are, in turn, linguistic bias (the survivors of the digitized archives always); attribution of interpretation (statistical prominence is not equal to literary significance); and circular pollution after LLM was introduced - when the model of training in literature began to produce "classic literature" in bulk, and the basic body of literature began to loose by measuring the history of literature in terms of text. It is expected that the "model-oriented criticism" and "specify science of collaboration with humans" will be growth points for the next five years; the construction of non-English, low-resource language materials will determine whether this area can be truly "globalized".
AI and Literature: Generating, collaborating and critical AI research

At the end of 2022, after the publication of ChatGPT, "AI and Literature" was suddenly transformed from a small-group experiment in electronics to a subject of all-disciplinary study: authorism, style, copyright, literature education and the ecology of "synthetic text" were all re-problems. A new field called "Critic AI Research" is taking shape.
4.1 Critical AI research: the formation of new fields

Institutional signal density: The interdisciplinary journal, Critical AI, led by Lauren M. E. Goodlad, Roz, was launched by Duke University Press in 2023; Rita Raley, University of California, and Jennifer Rhee, in the same year, in the American Organization of Literature, Critical AI: an area of development. The common position in this area is to use AI as a tool for cultural technology that requires historical and politicized, rather than neutral tools — to question the source of training materials (including unauthorized shadow library data sets), the labour and environmental costs of producing texts, and the impact of style imitations on author rights. The series of litigations against AI since 2023 by the American Writers Association (Authors Guild) has made the subject of "already calculated literary styles" a dual subject of court and theory.

Another clue is the historicalization of machine writing. Matthew Kirschenbaum, Maryland, Track Changes 2016, already proves how text processors are rewriting contemporary literary production. Its 2023 text "end of text" warns that synthetic text will contaminate human writing ecology. Dennis Yi Tenen, Colombia, Theory for Robots, 2024) puts LLM in the long history of writing manuals, rhetoric, dictionaries and auto-writing devices, advocating that "smart" is never a collective achievement of human collaboration.
4.2 Generating literature and human creation

At the creative level, the spectrum is also clear: from the program poetry of the Electronics and Literature Organization (ELO) and Nick Montfort, to the GPT-3 period of Pharmaco-AI (Pharmako-AI, 2020, K Alado-McDoll and the Model), and Sasha Stiles; in the 2020 Chinese "Symbiotic Qir's Queir's" experiment, writers like Chen Shuk and AI wrote scientific fiction. After 2024, human beings joined the mainstream literature awards (see case box), and the co-optation and publishing houses have been intensely introducing the production of AI usage norms, while "Ai literature constitutes a new literature" became the point of contention: supporters saw it as the beginning of "synthetic literature" and opponents considered it merely a statistical response to style.

Nine-part Lijiang and Tokyo Compassion Tower (Tokyo-Kyoto Compassion Tower, 2024). The author was given the 170th mustard prize, and at the press conference, he told me that about 5% of the sentences of novels were taken directly from ChatGPT production, which itself was also devoted to the creation of AI. The jury did not consider it a flaw, but it triggered a worldwide discussion on "The admissibility of the prize for literature" as a textbook case for the issue of the AI author.

The Chinese teacher training university team, "Abductor of the Omnipotents" (2024). The team, with the National Production Large Model, is working on a tipword project, producing a million-word web novel in months, and self-positioning the experiment "First One-million-word "in-country. Supporters view it as a maximum test of the logic of the industrial production of online literature, while critics claim that it proves that size does not amount to literary value. Two cases coincided with the spectral end: the former was a small borrowing led by the writer and the latter was a full process-led generation; Most of the contemporary debates about AI literature have occurred between these two ends.

The Chinese language response is a context: on the theoretical side, the journal Explore and Moot, The Rencontinental Moot, has been organizing a series of talks since 2023, from "AI Can" to "Amble to "quick" institutional analysis of authorhood, arts education and creative work; on the practical side, Chen Shuan-kian and Lee have combined the "AI Futures" (2022) hybrid of "Scientific Forecast + Fiction" and the Wang Feng Group's One-Mixer Experiment (see box above) has tested the limits of processization production. The Chinese language discussion on what constitutes an "in writing" evaluation of the text generation era, which is more focused on writing than the English language, has become a site where policy and science intersect.
4.3 Post-human criticism and technical text theory

Theories are deeply rooted in post-anthropophilism. Rosti Braddotti, Posthuman Know, 2019, established "critic posthumanities" as a knowledge platform beyond the human and European centres; N. Katherine Hayles gives the most systematic theoretical expression of the term "mechanical awareness" (see the deep reading box). At the theoretical level of the text, the study "materiality of digital text" (Kilshenbaum Bitstream, 2021) reminds the academic community that future manuscripts are designed to weigh hard drives, copy banks and models.

Since How We Be Post-Humans (1999), Hels has continued to provide the framework for "reading and cognisance of the technological age". Unthought, 2017 proposes "cognitive assemblage": cognition is not a patent for human consciousness, but a process that is distributed between people, technological systems and the environment; Postprint, 2021) to demonstrate that books are in a "calculative" production condition as a whole; The new book Bacteria to AI, 2025 further places LLM in the continuum of life-consciousness, advocating for thinking about the future of humanity with the "non-human symbiotics". Direct gifts to literary research are an unmorally panic analysis: rather than arguing that AI "does really understand", it is better to track the distribution of meaning in the human body.

blocks.text: 4.4 Disputes and prospects

The most productive difference in the field at the moment lies in the basic posture: the use of LLM as a cultural archive (compressed lawn for critical reading), the writing device (co-founder partner for new poetry) or the industrial symptoms (text form for platform capitalism)? The three posturings are critical to computational criticism, creative experiments and political economics. It is expected that the demonstration of the physiology of "model style", the theory of evaluation for AI products, and the "what is not an outsourcing ability" in literature education will be the core growth point for 2026-2030.
Environmental humanities: ecological criticism, human life and blue/energy humanities

The climate crisis has transformed the environment from a critical topic into a general condition for literary research. Ecocriticals have been in three stages of the transition in three decades: from natural book-writing to environmental justice and post-colonial ecological criticism to the environmental humanities of rapid fragmentation with the "human world" as the overarching framework.
5.1 Three Waves of Ecological Criticism

The first wave (1990s) was dominated by American-British writings and natural studies, with the organic symbol ASLE, founded in 1992, and the flagship ISL. The second wave introduced an environmental justice perspective, Rob Nixon, Slow Violence and the Environmentalism of the Poor, 2011 is a watershed: toxic leakage, sea level rise and the lack of explosive visibility of "slow violence" such as literature, whose task is to give form to invisible harm. The third wave, the current environment (environment humanities): interdisciplinary, global, with the human race as a whole, the flagship publication Environment Mankind was launched in 2012, and material ecology criticism (Serena Jovino and the main editor of Serepil Operman, 2014) brought the dynamicity of the object into the text analysis.
5.2 Human life and planetary scales

Dippesh Chakrabarty, 2009 Declares that humanity has become a geological force and that the scales of history and literature must be re-established; The historical climate of the planetary age (2021) further distinguishes "global" (global, capital from the realm of empire-made human centres) from "planets" (planet, a non-human-centred Earth system), whereby "planetary humanities" becomes the slogan. Timothy Morton's "hyperobjects 2013," provides aesthetic scope for such things as climate beyond the sensory scale; Donna Haraway, in 2016, advocates multi-species symbiotic narratives in Chthulucene; Anna Tsing, The End of the Day of Fizziness (2015), demonstrates the national aspirations-written approach to the livelihood of multi-species on the ruins of capitalism. Elizabeth DeLoughrey, the Fable of the World (2019), introduced post-colonial and island perspectives, and criticized the words "human life" to level the gap in colonial responsibility.

The Great Derangement, 2016, is one of the most cited texts on climate criticism. Its core argument: modern realism novels are based on "probability" and routine narrative contracts, and statistically rare, measurable events such as natural exclusion of climate disasters — so serious literature is almost lost to climate, not as a matter of preference, but as a form of incapacitation. The Nutmeg's Curse, 2021, using the 1621 massacre in the Banda Islands by the Dutch East India Company as a fable to argue that the root cause of the climate crisis was the mechanistic worldview of colonialism as a resource for the "destroy" of the planet and to call for a return to the "lifeful" narrative tradition. The effect of Gosh is to give literary research a clear mandate: to find and develop narrative forms capable of carrying planetary scales.
5.3 Sub-area Fragmentation: Blue, Energy and Evidence

Blue humanities raise the oceans from background to methodology: Steve Mentz's "Blue Humans Introduction (2023)" and Operman's synopsis systematized, and Astrida Neimamanis' "Bodies of Water," provides a theoretical interface between body-hydrography. The Energy Humanities (energy humanities) asks about the contours of literary forms and energy regimes: Stephanie LeMenager, Living in Oil (2014), Imre Szeman and Dominic Boyer's Energy Manuals (2017) have established the "oil novel" study; "How to describe energy transformation" has become a new topic. Empirical ecological criticisms test the actual effects of environmental narratives using social science methods: the same book (2023) of editors-in-chief, Matthew Schneider-Mayerson, compiles questionnaires and experimental studies of climate novels, and finds that reading climate novels increases risk perception in the short term, but the effects diminish - This provides the first testimonial evidence for the doctrine of "literacy to change environmental consciousness" and also gives rise to a methodological debate on "measurable literary values". In addition, fissions include: extinction studies (the co-analysis of the story of the extinction of the species demonstrated by Heyzer "Imagination" with the database), plant humans (the study of plant activity and plant narratives driven by Michael Mardel "Vestals Thinking", animal narratives (possibles and ethics described from a non-human perspective) and multi-species justice discussions that overlap with indigenous research.

Non-English language is not a follower: China's eco-aesthetics are self-contained (the "breed aesthetics" that were presented by the former Qun-gwan and the "breed" concept of Choyuan, the eco-literacy of the Luentun, the system of criticism of the U.S. and the United States have accumulated over 20 years, and the translation of English and American literature by Cheng-Soon has been supplemented by the translation of the English and American literature); The Japanese academic community responded to the Fukushima nuclear disaster with "post-earthquake literature" (see chap. 17); extractive criticisms from Latin America and Africa, and the study of waters in South Asia bound environmental issues to colonial history (see chap. 18-20).

Anna Qin and the collaborators, an online project, " Wild Atlass" (Assisted by the World Wide Web in 2020) Stanford University Press digitally published more than 70 field reports on "Ruralized Entities" (invasive species, radiation, viruses, cement), and users roamed the path of "How Infrastructures Cause Out of Controlled Ecology" instead of cataloguing. It is also a study, database and experimental publication of the environmental humanities, demonstrating three things: humanities can be presented in a form of a non-linear demonstration; multi-species research requires a joint effort of artists, scientists and humanists; and digital publishing carries the type of evidence that cannot be contained in paper books. The "Data Environment Humanities" project (extensive archives, climate narrative maps) has since been used as a template.
