blocks.text: 5.4 Disputes and prospects

The controversy focused on naming and responsibility: "The world of mankind" was criticized for allocating responsibility for colonial capitalism to abstract human beings, "the world of capital" (A/CONF.191/CRP.1, para. There is a proliferation of alternative words such as Capitalocene, Jason Moore, Plantationocene; empirical evidence has been turned to be questioned as a tool indicator for reducing literary values to attitudinal change; Climate novels also face a literature fatigue - whether the "cli-fi" label prevents us from seeing the diverse forms of climate writing. The next five years are expected to see a further seam between planetary humanities and regional colonial history, the rise of energy transformation narrative studies, and the convergence of environmental and medical humanities on the "Health-Environment" interface.
Post-colonial, decolonizing and global South

Decolonisation was simultaneously a theoretical agenda, curriculum reform movement and institutional politics in the 2020s, whose energy has spilled over the traditional boundaries of post-colonial research, reshaping the geography of orthodox, academic history and intellectual production. At the same time, criticism within the movement is gaining ground.
6.1 From post-colonial criticism to decolonization

The classic post-colonial criticism (Said-Spivac-Babara) targeted the 18-20th century European Empire, using speech analysis. (a) The colonial school has rewritten the coordinates: the "coloniality of power" of Aníbal Quijano advanced the starting point to 1492, arguing that colonization is the dark side of modernity and that it still organizes labour, knowledge and substance after political independence; Walter Mignolo proposed delinking the "decolonization option" from the "delinking" theory, which became a textbook with Catherine Walsh's "decolonization" (2018); María Lugones' "Migration of Gender" reveals the colonial origins of the modern gender system itself; Boaventura de Sousa Santos ' s "South Perceptions" (2014) calls for learning from the intellectual traditions of the cognitive extinction. The direct consequence of literary research is that the research unit has moved from "the Empire and its writing" to "the knowledge system and its absentee", with the consequent rise in the status of translation, oral and multilingual.

The 2015 South African "Rotz must fall" campaign and the British campaign "Why is my curriculum white?", which translates theory into curriculum politics; Priyamvada Gopal, The Empire of Rebels (2019), rewritten the history of the empire in reverse — how anti-colonial resistance teaches the traditions of dissent in the sovereign State.

Winter is the most thorough theorist "rediscovered" since 2010. Its central proposition: Western modernity has made a local art of "human" - "humans" (Man, first theological, then economic-biology) - the more the race system becomes human itself, the mechanism of execution of the transgression; liberation is therefore not the inclusion of more people in "Man", but the creation of a new "genres of the human." She defined people as "homo Narrans": people coded them by narratives, and literature was therefore not the core of the decorative but the core of human science. (a) Silvia Winter: As a Practice (2015), editor-in-chief of Catherine McKittrick, promotes her ideas as the public ground for black research, Caribbean research and decolonisation criticism; The conventional re-evaluation of English in the last decade, the discussion of what is human in the AI era, is all over the voice of Winter.
6.2 Global South: From Location to Methodology

The "global South" has moved from economic geography to analytical scope. Anne Garland Mahler, From Three Continents to the Global South (2018), traced its history to anti-imperialism at the 1966 Havana Conference of the Three Continents, advocating that the global South should point to a transnational political body rather than a map hemispheric. The downside patterns in literary research include South-South comparisons (direct read-in-hand from Latin America-Africa-South Asia, bypassing the Euro-American intermediaries), reinterpretation of world literature by the Warerick Research Collective (WREC) in the form of "joint and unbalanced development" (a semi-marginal experiment with the most visible capitalist world system), and the institutionalization of university centres and specialized publications. Achille Mbembe 's work is another pillar: Black Rational Criticism (French 2013) argues that the invention of "Black" is a prototype of capitalism, "Death Politics." 2019) The "necropolitics" concept — power operates by allocating death and reversibility — has become a common tool for analysing war, borders and plantation literature; and its recent call for Africa to become a place to think about the future of the planet, more recently the "planetary library".

The continuous proliferation of the Caribbean spectra is a paradigm shift from "case to theoretical field". Édouard Grissant's "relationship poetry" and "right to opacity" became high-frequency quotations from the age of data monitoring and identity politics - "untransparent" was reread as resistance to "readable sexual violence" and the English translation and reprint of his writings formed a small renaissance; The "tidal dialectics" of Kamau Brathwaite are gaining methodological expression with the rise of blue humans (chap. 5) and archipelagic thinking (chap. 18). Together with Winter's "humans" (see box above), the Caribbean has been upgraded from the subject of post-colonial research to the first site of theoretical production.
6.3 De-disciplinaryization: self-reformation of de-generational research

The most profound structural effect of the colonial agenda is within the context of disinheritance and country studies, collectively called "revocation of discipline". The 2020 proclamational article " Undoing the discipline of Victorian research " , Victorian Research, triggered a ripple effect: periodical restructuring, new books, rewriting of the curriculum. The counterpart of medieval and early modern research is Premodern critical race studies: Geraldine Heng, "Emergence of Medieval Races in Europe" (2018), which argues that racial thinking is much earlier than enlightenment science; The RACEB4Race Conference Community (2019-) of Arizona State University provides an institutional platform for medieval scholars of colour. The common logic of these efforts is not to add "a little diversity" to the old discipline, but to re-write its foundational concept and archives boundaries.

Ngugui Wa Thiong'o died in May 2025, returning the "linguistics problem" to the centre of African literary research. In Decolonizing Minds (1986) he renounced English and converted to Kikuyu, advocating language as the battlefield of spiritual colonization. The 2016 special translation of the Pan-African Writers Group, Jalalada, translated his short "Current Revolution" into dozens of African and world languages, known as the most widely disseminated short pages in African literature -- through digital networks that he had been advocating half a century ago. The agenda of African language publishing, mother tongue creation courses and "for translation" was reactivated in the context of the discussion of the retroactivity of their heritage (for more details see chapter 20); This is also the best observation window for "decolonization" from theory to publishing and teaching infrastructure.
6.4 Internal criticism: decolonizing self-examination

The expansion of the movement was accompanied by a sharpening of internal criticism. Eve Tucker and K. Wayne Young, " Decolonization is not a metaphor " in 2012, warns that when "decolonization" is used to name all agendas for progress, indigenous land restitution claims are again erased. Bolivian sociologist Silvia Rivera Cusicanki criticized the colonial schools for processing the Andean "ch'ixi" experience into the North American Academy clean theory commodity. The Nigerian philosopher Olufemi'mi Táwò (2022), who opposed decolonization, suffered from within: the colonization of all modern things, which deprived Africans of their mobility and choice. These arguments (see chapter 24, section IV) mark the beginning of the maturity of the field - Decolonization was no longer a form of freeing itself from criticism, but rather a programme of research that needed to be justified in its specific claims.

blocks.text: New phase of study of world literature and translation

The first 20 years of the World Literature War ended with "circulation" and "untranslated"; The key words for the new phase are multilingualism, the visibility of translators and machine translation — the study of world literature and translation is being merged into the same problem area.
7.1 Twenty years of war: circulation, systems and irrelacticability

Three foundational works at the turn of the century established the pattern of war: Pascal Casanova, The World Republic of Literature (1999), depicting the literary capital market, with Paris as the centre of liquidation; Moretti's World Literary Conspection (2000) proposes the scale of the world literature to be studied from a distance; David Damrosch (2003) provides the most operational definition — that world literature is not a classic list, but rather a mode of circulation for “benefiting translation” when the work reaches its source culture. The opposition then arrived: Emily Apter, Anti-World Literature (2013) rejected "untranslationable" negotiatorism; Amir Mufti, Forget English! (2016) reveals the English hegemony behind "Liction of the World" and the Eastern spectra; Pheng Cheah, “What is the World?” (2016) criticizes the "world" downgraded to a market by the "world of post-colonial literature" as the opening of an alternative world of time; The Volik Research Group recast the entire agenda in terms of the world system. Alexander Bicroft, The Ecology of World Literature (2015), offers six types of "literate ecology" from palace literature to global English.

Apt, in cooperation with Barbara Kasan, introduced the European Philosophy Glossary: A Dictionary of Non-Interpretation (English version 2014) into the English-language world and is in Anti-world Literature: The Politics of Non-Translation (English version, 2014). The Untranslatable is a theoretical weapon: the untranslated word is not a failure of translation, but a philosophical event that resists semantic exchange; the "world literature" industry is tacitly premised on the possibility of translation, but is the logic of the equivalent exchange of literature into the global market. She was not opposed to translation — on the contrary, she asked for translation to study the political conditions of facing sovereignty, checkpoints and language ownership. This statement profoundly affected the subsequent multilingual shift: monolingual criticism (which is not the only one who is not a member of the public). The rise in the post-speaker conditions of Yasselmin Ildiz, dialects and Creole studies have all taken Aput as the theoretical anchor. Critics, including Damroche, believe that incomprehensible theory eventually slides to a non-comparisonable and unteachable stalemate.
7.2 New phase: Born translation and multilingual

Rebecca Walkovitz, Born Translated, 2015 captured structural changes in contemporary novel production: the writings of Ishigami and Franket writers pre-set multiple simultaneous publishing, and translation is no longer a posteriori but an intrinsic condition for creation - The term "world literature" has thus been changed from being accepted to being a production category. Francesca Orsini East of Delhi, 2023, offers another kind of correction. Right: To take Northern India as a case in point, to show the symbioticism of Urdu, Hindi, Persian, and multilingual languages in the same place - world literature research that tracks transnational flows alone will miss the true shape of most of the world's literary life. The two books represent the two wings of the new phase: research on the global publishing system and multilingual literature. The "Librology of the World" discussion (Wang Ning et al.) and Cao Xingqing's variants in Chinese academic circles over the same period emphasized the inevitable creative variation in the circulation of civilizations, echoing and challenging the theory of circulation (see chapter 16).
7.3 Translation studies: outward transfer and interpreter visibility

Translation studies "outward turn" (Suzanne Basnet): from language-to-language issues to translation's place in political, publishing and technological systems. Lawrence Venuti's "anti-toolism" (2019) continues his criticism of the ideology of "transparent translation" and the research on "activism translation" (Mona Baker, etc.) focuses on subtitle groups, crisis translation and protest translation. The most visible changes occurred in the industry-academic context: the International Book Award was changed in 2016 to split the prize between authors and translators; the Open Position of the "Signation of the Translator" campaign and Jennifer Crosser in 2021 "No cover to live," which promoted the publication of the names of translators on the cover. The translation has evolved from invisible intermediaries to research subjects and public figures, and the Translator studies have taken shape.

After Korea's first award to the International Booker Award in 2016, Korean academic and media began a round-the-clock review of the translation, pointing to the error and the large rewrite; the defenses considered Smith's "creative translation" to be the condition for the world to be able to circulate. The battle became a classic case for translation research: it involved both faithfulness and readability, the cost of entry to small-language literature, the authorship of the translator, and the role of the national translation support agency (the Korean Institute of Literature and Translation, LTI Korea, long-term funded external translation) in "making world literature." In 2024, Han River won the Nobel Prize for Literature, followed the path of "translator to make the world's author" and gave the debate a new evaluation framework. The globalization of Korean literature has thus become a model for world literature and translation research (see chapter 17 for more details).
7.4 Machine translation: new variables

LLM has pushed literary translation into a new period of controversy. The performance of the "Lictation Field" in machine translation assessments was rated as near-manual in part of the baseline report, but targeted research (see below). As shown by the recent multi-dimensional quality assessment of the English translation of Shen from the border city, the flow of model translations continues to systematically lose narrative perspectives, personal relationships and cultural loads. In 2024, a major Dutch publishing group announced a trial for the translation of AI commercial novels with manual revision, which provoked protests from European translation organizations. The response to translation research is being divided: an empirical study that shifts to the division of "post-editing poetry" and human beings, and an interpretation that insists that literary translation is not an outsourcing practice. What you can tell is the old pattern of translation flows -- English as a hypercentre, translation into less than 3% of the "tricent question" -- – The complex interaction with the cost curve of machine translation is the most worthwhile variable in world literature sociology for the next five years.

blocks.text: The theory of love, reading and "post critical" competition.

(a) The theory of passion provides the most common analytical currency for contemporary criticism; The post-critical debate about how to read after "suspicious interpretation" has evolved into a comprehensive re-examination of the form of knowledge of literary research: two clues converge in "the relationship between the reader and the work."
8.1 Theories of love: moving from the point of view to the point of common sense

"Travel" has two streams: one rereading of the psychologist Silvan Tomkins in 1995 by Eve Sejvik and Adam Frank, leading to the physical and contagious of emotion; One of them, explained by Brian Masumi to Druze, emphasizes that "extra" is pre-named strength, different from social "emotion". Sara Ahmed (2004) has politicized emotional analysis: emotions as values, moving between the body and symbols, " sticky" to a specific target (fear on immigration "kick" and "slut" pride on the flag). By 2020, emotional analysis had become a cross-cutting default, with a new generation of "negative and unmoveable": Xine Yao, studying the nineteenth-century American literature's rejection of common feelings, revalued it as a form of resistance by the oppressed and won several awards for learning -- - The criticism of the cult became a hot spot. There are also systematic responses from Chinese literature, such as Kim Wan. The 18th century European "Felterage Age" and the rise of modern novels (Felter Age, 2024) and their interpretation of the "Felter/Feltership" name and the theoretical spectrum.

The cruel optimism 2011 is one of the most widely cited criticisms of the century: when your attachment to the object is precisely what you want to prosper (the illusion of a good life, the desire for mobility), attachment itself becomes a harm. The concept is the result of the fact that it gives precise form to contemporary universal experience of "know what you want to do and don't let go". The term "love" (2022) has been elevated to social existential terms, continuing its thinking of "floating together."

Engae created the "Final Capitalist Aesthetics" trilogy: Ugly Perceptions (2005) to analyze the weak emotions of impotence, irritation and so on. The Aesthetics of Ours (2012) argues how "funner" (Zany), "cute" (cute), "interesting" (interesting) corresponds to performing labour, commodity attachment and information flows; Theory of the Gimpick, 2020) defines "funner" as a "resort of power-saving devices" that both enticing and discomfort -- overestimateing and underestimating labour -- a aesthetic of capitalist values. Together, they moved the relationship from "talking about feeling" to "political economics through feelings."
8.2 Post-critical contest: knowledge patterns of criticism

Rita Felski, "The limits of criticisms" (2015), has set Paul Leco's "explaining of suspicion" as a target: "critical" in the form of a tacit gesture of exposure, symptoms and phantom, has become emotional style and custom; She uses the actor web theory to advocate a description of the "relationship" between the reader and the work (Press Up, 2020). Previous history includes the distinction between "sorture reading/rehabilitation reading" (2003) and "Far-face reading" (2009) in Bester, Marcus. Reaction is equally powerful: Bruce Robbins snuffs it as "the comic book of criticism"; Anna Kornbuloh, "Instant or late capitalism," "Instant, 2024" puts "anti-intermediate, be present" cultural styles -- automatic fiction, first person, "let the work directly touch you." - Diagnosis as the ideology of the accelerated era of circulation and as a defence to theoreticalization and intermediation, one of the most critical works discussed in recent years.

In parallel, a "reconstruction route" attempts to circumvent the position and recapitulate what literature research produces: John Gillory, Profiting Criticism, 2022, dissects the history of critical professionalization in discipline sociology and its self-examining political imagination by continuing the E.L. (2025) as a technical art study; Jonathan Kramnik, Criticism and Truth, 2023, argues that reading is a "craft knowledge" of a craft like experimental skills, whose truth is tested in practice; Joseph North, Literary Critics: A Concise Political History (2017), advocates the revival of critical "aesthetic education" missions. This discussion of the self-understanding of the discipline forms the subject of Chapter 24, War II.

The post-criticism calls for serious attachment to the average reader, and digital platforms have left hundreds of millions of traces of attachment. Melanie Walsh and Maria Anthony Jacques, a book-based computational analysis marked "classic" on Goodreads (Cultural Analysis Journal, 2021), shows that the "classic" of the platform readers overlaps with the school hierarchy, but is produced by a completely different mechanism -- The curriculum, video adaptation and young people's reading memory are far more powerful than critical authority. TikTok’s #BookTok phenomenon is further recasting the circulation: the cry lens in short video can push back into the top of the best sales list years ago, and publishers are restructuring the marketing sector for this. The reading study thus acquired a new object of tri-partite encounter: love. Tears as money for recommended algorithms, platform economy and literature sociology - "who's philosophies" becomes an issue to be asked.

blocks.text: Cognitive literature, empirical aesthetics and neurobehaviours

"How literature works for the mind" is moving from a question-and-answer programme to an experimental research programme: cognitive science provides theory (mentotheism, 4E cognitive, prognosis processing), laboratory methods (questionnaires, eye, physiological and brain imaging), and literary research offers truly difficult questions.
9.1 Three Generations of Research in Cognitive Literature

The first generation (1980-1990s) centred on cognitive metaphors, considering literary languages as the precision of a universal conceptual map. Theory of Mind: Lisa Zunshine, "Why We Read Novels" (2006) advocates novels as gymnasiums where mental readability is based. The reading of "Mrs. Dallowy" is a tracking exercise for people with multiple layers of embedded psychology; The Oxford Research Manual on Cognitive Literature (2015), which she edited, established a field map to systematize narratives (David Herman and others) over the same period. The key words for the third generation are 4E cognitive (body embodied, embedded, generate inactivated, extended) and predictive processing: mind is not a passive irritating container, but an engine that constantly generates predictions, updates models with errors -- - The literary text can therefore be understood as a fine tune to the reader of the predictive machine (see the deep reading box). Terence Cave (2016) gives an elegant expression to the concept of "accessibility": literature is a mechanism of thought, and the task of criticism is to describe the possibility of action that opens a work to consciousness.

Kukoninen was the first to introduce the theory of predictive processing into literary research. Probability Design: Literature and Forecasts, 2020, says: "Probability Design" for the reader's probative mind. - Textual practices set a priori probabilities, style and circumstance create prediction errors at the right time, and aesthetic aesthetic aesthetic sense is derived from the rhythm of error and renewal. She has used 18th century novels (formerly "4E Cognisance and 18th Century Novel" monographs) as a test ground to prove that history poetry and cognitive science can be mutually binding: how well cognitive models are interpreted, and literary history provides complex incentives that their labs cannot create. The Oslo University's "Living, Cognitive and Emotional" research cluster, facilitated by the University, serves as a hub for European cognitive literature research. The attraction of this path lies in its rejection of the theory that mechanisms for interpreting pleasure do not remove the need for interpretation.
9.2 Empirical and neurobehavioural

The foundation of the empirical wing is the Max Planck Institute of Empirical Aesthetics, founded in Frankfurt in 2013: Winfried Menninghaus, a team that translates the field of rhetoric (hymn, rhyme, vision) into a manageable experimental variable that systematically measures the "aesthetic emotions" triggered by poetry. Earlier traditions include the experimental validation of the effects of "prospecting-de-acquaintance" by David Miol and Don Quaker (Abtta), the International Association for the Study of the Empirical Literature (IGEL) and its journal, Scientific Research in Literature (in its 2011 original edition), and the development of the "story world dilution table" by Monica Cuipels. The neuroaesthetic direction, G. Gabrielle Starr, "Feeling beauty" (2013), is based on a study of brain imaging: a strong aesthetic experience is associated with the involvement of the default model network - "hit by the work" - when the brain is using its own self-directed backtracking with the interior. Atul Jacobs (Frestrial University of Berlin) "Neurocotheque" seeks to create a whole process structure for literary reading.

This is an area where methodological acumen is shared with digital humanities (chapter 3) and empirical ecological criticism (chapter 5), and questions: is the laboratory text or literature? Can statistical averages touch on the uniqueness of individual reading? The main response to this is the "work-sharing theory" — empirical research that answers questions about mechanisms and effects, explaining the meaning and value of the criticism, which are binding on, not substitutes for, the other. Cognitive poetry (which has been in the middle literature since 2000 and was also in the construction of the Quizun) and cognitive narrative studies have formed a stable community and have begun to emerge in indigenous experimental studies.

The Mapp Institute of Physical Aesthetics experiment series allowed for the oral reading of poetry, the simultaneous recording of skin, facial muscle and self-reporting, and the capture of "ficky-pump" with video and sensory footage. The results show that the height of the peak moments of chilling and chicken-skinting by poetry is quite regular - often at the close of the verse-strap and the rhyme; that subjective "being moved" is accompanied by a mixed mood of pleasure and grief, and that poetry can trigger the same reaction on the test of never-reading poetry. The significance of such research is not to prove the common sense of "poetry works", but to turn "what form features, where, and what probability triggers what physical reaction" into cumulative knowledge. - The intuitive instinct of rhetorical "momentation" for 2,000 years, for the first time, obtained line-by-line physical evidence. Critics remind me that the "hearing a single short poem" scenario used in the experiment is far from the long, distracted and rereading of true reading.

blocks.text: 9.3 Disputes and Prospects

The old problem in the field remains acute: ecological effectiveness (experimental vs. real reading), restatement of the doubt, and whether the empirical results are binding on interpretation. The new variable comes from two directions: first, LLM provides an infinity-manipulated "synthetic reader/author" for cognitive literature research, predicting that processing scenarios can be compared to artificial systems for the first time; and second, public anxiety (screen reading, focus economy) with deep reading recession has made this field more relevant than ever - • The meta-analysis of digital reading and paper reading understanding differences has entered into the educational policy discussion. It is expected that cognitive literary research will increasingly play the role of "scientific advocate of literary values", which in itself will trigger new theoretical debate.
Gender Research Front: Cool, Transgender and Anti-Gender Age

The cross-gender study was structured and advanced in the literary history; at the same time, the global "anti-gender" campaign made gender research itself the political storm — a contest for censorship, banning of books and disciplines, which in turn became the subject of research.
10.1 Theory of Cool: After the Classic Period

The internal debate on cool theory has been "negative to utopia." Lee Ederman "No Future" 2004 sank on José Muñoz 'Utopia 2009 as a common heritage: Muñoz's "Cool is the horizon that has yet to come" continues to appreciate behind it, leaving the "brown perception 2020" driving "Create" (crucie of color, derived from Rodrik Ferguson) into a default framework. Jack Halberstam continues to defend "indefeating" intellectual styles with the "low theory" route from "Face of the Cool Art" (2011) to "The Wilderness" (20). The new trend at the methodological level is "cooler formalism": Kaji Amin, Amber Müsser and others advocate a shift from subject identification to formal relationships — rhythm, leapfrogging, posturing — with new forms of formalism. The academic map of "sex spectra" is also being expanded by non-sexy studies, intersex research and so on.
10.2 Cross-gender research: from the margins to the theoretical frontier

The transgender study, marked by the quarterly publication Transgender Studies (TSQ, 2014, hosted by Susan Strake), has completed a shift from the theory of the proselytism to the independent domain and has moved rapidly to the historical depths: C. Riley Snowton Black on Both Sides, 2017, cross-gender history and black slavery files to prove the intersection between gender sculpability and racialization; Jules Gill-Petersen, The History of Transgender Children (2018), which proves in medical records that "transgender children" are not new inventions, and the Short History of Trans-Female Women (2024), which distinguishes between structural violence against transgender women and general fear of violence, is a source of theory for public discussion. On the literary side, the "Turry," "Turkish," baby (2021), the "Tribunal" novels, Grace LaFry's study of transgender narratives and aesthetic effectiveness ( " Happiness and Effectiveness", 2023), and Andrea Long Zhu's sharp public criticism (a Pulitzer Review Award in 2023), were jointly put into the mainstream of literary criticism.

The author of Gender Trouble (1990) gave it to the public domain, Who's Fear of Gender?, 2024. Instead of obfuscating the school's internal argument of improvisation, the book dissects a global phenomenon: from Vatican documents to multinational legislation, how "gender" is conceived as a "phantasm" that absorbs all social anxiety — economic insecurity, family changes, and the threat of a crisis of sovereignty being projected as "gender ideology". Butler's analysis has made the "anti-gender movement" a theoretical object in itself and has offered a response from coalition politics: gender studies are not a match for common sense, but rather an organized production of fear. The book has triggered a debate and a debate in dozens of countries, marking the beginning of a parallel phase between the theory of gender and the creation of a foundation for defence. - It is rare in theoretical history that the conditions for the existence of a discipline become its most pressing subject.
10.3 "Anti-gender" age: review as subject of research

(a) Gender literature research in the 2020s was forced to face the deterioration of the institutional environment: United States multistate legislation limited teaching on gender issues and the proliferation of school book bans (see case box); The Hungarian degree in gender studies was suspended, and Russia listed the International LGBT Movement as an extremist organization in 2023; the anti-gender ideology movement in Brazil, Poland and elsewhere is on the rise. The research community responded by targeting the review itself: data monitoring of the ban on books campaign, teaching methods of "prohibited literature", comparative studies of the history of review and publication (connected with the study of the cold war literature review) constituted new sub-areas. Meanwhile, the re-evaluation of the Women's Order has advanced in several languages: the Seven-volume Korean Women's Literature Selected (KOKL) in Korea. 2024) Re-engineering of the 100-year female literature system in the form of an optimised system; continuous warming of discussions and historical compilations around "New Woman Writing" (Jan Li and others); and publication of the re-editing and re-evaluation of "uneared female writer" in the English-speaking world.

According to PEN America, the number of book bans in United States public schools in the school year 2023-2024 exceeded 10,000, which is a record since monitoring; the banned books are highly concentrated on works related to LGBTQ+ themes, race issues and sex education, with young people being the first to suffer. The response of the literary research community is multi-line: the network of legislative mechanisms and lobbying for cooperation in library and literary sociology to follow up on bans; The pedagogic research developed the "prohibited text" curriculum module; the critical community re-started the theoretical review of history - From the Church's Book Ban catalogue, the Constock Method to the Algérie of the Algérie era, the history of the ban was rewritten as one of the main lines of the history of the struggle for literary values. This case shows the situation of contemporary gender studies: its subjects, teaching materials and institutions are the first to be the subject of political struggle.

blocks.text: 10.4 Disputes and Prospects

The real problems in this field include: how cross-gender research and the continuing conflicts between certain radical feminist schools are dealt with within academic norms; and the tension between the "universalization" of the cool theory and the "rematerialization" of the "re-physicalization" of the physical and legal situation; The Chinese translation of the "cool" and "gay" translations is typical of the history of the two-track translation of the Chinese language. Looking ahead, the intersection of gender studies with platform studies (gender algorithms), AI criticism (gender bias in model generation) and review studies will be the fastest growing interface.
Memory and trauma studies: multi-directional memory and associated subjects

The trauma theory has moved beyond the single paradigm of the Holocaust Centre, where memory research has become the pivotal discipline in dealing with colonial violence, war and transitional justice; From Berlin to Gwangju, every public outbreak of memory politics is rewriting its theoretical agenda.
11.1 Theory of Trauma: Pluralization of Parameters

The classic model established by Cathy Carus, The Uncorruptal Experience (1996), The trauma is a "unrecognized experience" of immediate, flashback returns, literature in its form of a break-up to the indiscretionary-overtight realm, with its cumulative and systematic criticism: Ruth Les: A Spectro spectro (2000) questions its theoretical foundation; and Steve Craps, Post-colonial Witness (2013), points out that the classic model is based on a single European event, unable to deal with the daily, structural, intergenerational "chronic trauma" of colonialism and racism, and implicitly sets Western writing styles as the gold standard for testimony. The current trauma study is therefore complex: beyond the event model, the "inhibitive trauma" (insistious trauma), the "pre-traumatic trauma" of the climate era and ecological mourning have all entered the agenda — Krapps himself was one of the promoters of the "ecological grief" study.
11.2 Memory Research: From Legacy to Action

The institutional maturity of memory research is marked by the journal Memory Research (2008) and the Society for Memory Research (MSA, 2016). Theoretics include Marianne Hirsch's post-memory. The memory of survivors' descendants through photographs, objects and silent "inheritance" has become a standard tool for analysing second-generation writings and family images; Astrid Erll's "cross-cultural memory" emphasizes the need to travel between media and migration routes; and Ann Rigney's "memory activism" research group turns attention from trauma to hope -- how protest movements are being nurtured by memory, how it is nourished by follow-up, and corrects the "negative bias" in the field. Digital Memory (Andrew Hoskins) and Memory Research (MEM) Nikolai Koposov, Memory, Memory Wars, 2017, responds to two main realities: the timing of the rewrite of the testimony by social media; Many countries have legislated to impose or prohibit specific historical narratives - post-war Ukraine, the instant-time archiving of wartime (digital evidence bank, oral project) has made the topic "ongoing memory" a new methodological topic.

Two concepts of Roseberg have reshaped the field grammar. The Multi-Range Memory (2009) rejects the idea of public memory as zero and competing (the Holocaust will crowd in the space of colonial memory and vice versa) and argues that different violent memories are mutually reinforcing in the public sphere: the early post-war Holocaust witnessed the achievement of the decolonization movement. The Entrenched Subject, 2019, is the most named " neither perpetrator nor victim": the implicated, who benefit from historical wrongs, embedded in violent structures, whose pattern of responsibility is different from that of guilt — a concept that is rapidly being misappropriated by climate justice, slavery compensation and war bystanders. In 2021, the framework of German "Hystorians' Competition 2.0" (in the context of Decker Moses' German Church Questions and Answers, arguing whether the theory of Holocaust uniqueness has prevented the settlement of German colonial crimes) was the common reference system of the warring parties - theory rarely stood at the centre of public debate.
11.3 Fields in East Asia

East Asia is one of the most active sites in contemporary memory research: the transnational initiative of "comfort women" memory (the global set-up and controversy of young girls) is the natural casebook of "memory activism" and "multi-way memory"; the literature of the Nanjing Holocaust; the intergenerational transformation of the books of Hiroshima Nagasaki, the authentic literature of cold war violence (Jeys 4-3, Guangzhou), which constitutes a parallel and exotic memory ecology to Europe. In Chinese literature, Michael Berry's "History of Pain" (2008) systematic research on the writing of trauma books in modern Chinese history, and recent discussions on "generational transmission of memory" have brought trauma writing into cross-country comparative view (see chaps. 16, 17).

Han Jiang's "Juveniles" (온다, 2014, Gwangju 1980) and "Don't Say Aide" (않는다, 2021, Jeju 4-3) are the "officials" of literature for victims of national violence. The 2024 Nobel Prize for his "Face-to-Historical Trauma" poetic essays has pushed this memory writing sequence into the world mainstream. For researchers, the value of the Han River case lies in its integrity: on one end is the archives and testimony of transitional justice (how truth commission material enters novels), on the other hand, physical trauma poetry (touching, pain and the material image of snow) and on the other hand, translation and world circulation (translation of chapter 7, the battle of translation, the pursuit of the Nord Prize). Her research has crossed four areas: memory research, trauma poetry, translation sociology and women writing — one writer has become the crossroads of the four front lines, a microcosm of the integration of contemporary literary research agendas.

blocks.text: Medical humanities, narrative medicine and disability studies

This is the most successful interface for the export of literature: the medical school curriculum is being read in detail, disease narratives become a generic literature, and disability studies in turn transform the basic assumptions of literature theory. Following the epidemic, public demand for health narratives reached historic peaks.
12.1 Narrative medicine: clinicalization of readings

The Narrative Medicine (2006) of Rita Charon, a physician and doctor of literature, establishes a programme: clinical competence consists of "care, reproduction, belonging" and training doctors to read patients as carefully as textual reading; University of Colombia, 2009 established the first master's degree in narrative medicine worldwide, "Systems of parallel medical history" (Cyber-System, 2009) The first person to write about the condition of the patient in addition to the standard medical history) became the emblematic teaching method. Its theoretical foundation is Arthur Frank, The Wounded Storyteller (1995), whose division of the disease into three categories (rehabilitation, confusion, search) and Arthur Kleinman's painful narrative study. China has been receiving a remarkable rate of acceptance: under the auspices of the Han Kai-de-Kin University, Beijing University established the Faculty of Medical Humanities in 2018, the journal Narrative Medicine was launched in the same year, and Guo Li Ping and others completed the core document translation, and the subject medicine has been introduced into the curricula of several medical colleges and the training of resident physicians - a method of literary research has been given rare clinical kits.
12.2 Criticism of medical and healthy humanities

The second wave of self-criticism followed. Edinburgh Critical Medicine Humanities Guide (2016, editor-in-chief of Anne Whitehead and Angela Woods) proposes "critical medical humanities": not content with literature to "a little humanism" in medicine, but to study the deep "entanglement" of medical knowledge, body and power. - Medical humanities are not a medical decoration, but rather a critical self-perception. (a) The long-term funding of the Wellcome Trust in England, which has made the United Kingdom a major town in that direction (Durren University Institute for Medical Humanities, etc.); Paul Crawford (Nordingham) advocates a healthy humanistic approach that extends the horizon from doctors to nursing, social workers, sick friends and the public. The criticism also points to the course itself: can it be performed, tested? How strong is the evidence of literary reading to improve the commonality of opinion? — This problem directly connects medical humanities to empirical reading studies (chap. 9).

Garland-Thomson is the founder of literary disability research and the permanent theorist. Its early work established an analytical tradition of "extra-physical bodies": how literature and culture of exhibition makes the physically handicapped a wonder. And she put forward in 2011 and the idea of a big charade in the 2010-2020s was "misfit": disability is not a physical attribute, but a physical and environmental "failure" in specific contact. This relationship definition shifts the focus of analysis from individual impairments to world design, directly nourishing research on universal design, "care aesthetics" and disability justice writing. With her relay to Alison Caver (Femaleism, Cool, Gimp, 2013), Robert McCrull ( "Theory of the Gimp") and others, the disability study has completed a shift from "the image of disability in literature" to "the image of disability as a critical method" (cripping, lame reading): time (the time of the cripple), dependence and self-reliance, accessibility and sexuality are theoretical in themselves.
12.3 Post-emergence: Health narrative expansion

The new coronary disease is a medical "pressure test": the rebirth of the plague's epoch, the emergence of sealing journals and the writing of the "New Crown" life book, and clinically-based stress-reduction exercises have all tested public supply capacity in this area in the short term. The research agenda that has sunk in the aftermath of the epidemic includes the epidemiological narratives (separation, tracking, immunisation narrative), the temporality of chronic diseases and "infirmation" (coherence with cripple time), the writing of moral damage to health personnel, and the narrative evidence of public health transmission. Meanwhile, the writing of "Neuro-Diverse" and the "Mad studies" continue to challenge the default script that "disease narratives must lead to rehabilitation".

"Image Medicine" means the creation and research campaign of comic-books dealing with disease and care experience, with Ian Williams (doctor and cartoonist) as the starting point for the network of the same name and the Image Medicine Declaration (MK Chelwitz, 2015 and others). The media character of the cartoon is highly compatible with disease experience: the partitions can be multiple times (the bodies in chemotherapy coexist with the bodies in memory), the hand-drawing lines carry the subjective sense of pain, and the texture gap is exactly the same as the "unspeakable" part. (a) The Year of Caring, among others, Mother ' s Cancer, has been introduced into the curriculum of the Multinational Medical School; In research, image medicine is the most mature branch of the Cross-Media Health Narrative, with three communities of comics, narrative medicine and disability. It also suggests literary research: the main battlefield for contemporary disease narratives is long beyond mere essays.

blocks.text: 12.4 Disputes and Prospects

The core tension lies in the dual identity of "service" and "critical": to what extent can medical humanities employed in medical schools criticize the employer's knowledge system? Is the quantitative test of mutual training a betrayal of the ethics of narratives? The next five years are noteworthy: AI ' s re-engineering of "hearing" by automating consultations and documentation (new opponents or tools for narrative medicine), the global Southern health narrative (vaccinating politics, narrative studies of traditional medicine) and the theoretical convergence of disability research and environmental humanities in the "body-environment" interface.
Sound, media and material texts: media archaeology and history of books

Literature is never just "text", it's sound waves, paper, ink, file formats and servers. Sound research, media archaeology and the history of new books have combined to materialize the objects of literary research — a reminder that has become more sharp than ever in the era of the media and clouds.
13.1 Sound research: the audio shift in literature

Sound studies are based on Jonathan Stern's " Hearable Past " (2003): Audience Modernity has its own technological history and physical discipline ( "Audience Technology" before a phonograph). The field was completed in the 2010s (reading books, manuals and the Voice Research Journal, 2015 original) and a set of agenda items was exported to literary research: Nina Sun Ezheim, Ethnicity of the Sound (2019), argues that "the race of the sounder" is a traininged habit of listening rather than a sound attribute, opening the way for "racialization of the sound" research; there is a voice book study (Marthieu Lubergh, The Book of Speech, 2016) that tracks reading history from Braille to the time of the podcast; and "Audionartology" (Audionartology) addresses the linguistics specific to the sound media. The Poetry Research Sound Files (PennSund, University of Pennsylvania, etc.) make Poetry's Voice comparable research data.
13.2 Media Archaeology: Irresponsible Technical

German media theory is the engine in this direction: the "writing system" study by Friedrich Kittler establishes the "media determines our situation" analytical style; Wolfgang Ernst's "time critical" medium archaeology insists on starting from the machine's own temporality; Josi Parikka's Media Geology (2015) digs media history downwards down to minerals and energy - the material base of literature, including rare soil and electricity, which allows media archeology and energy to be connected directly to the humanities (chap. 5); and its Operational Image (2023) study images that are not made of human eyes, providing a precursor to the "mechanic readable text" of the AI era. Methodological gifts to literary research are: the return of "work" to "output of record-keeping devices", and the question of how typewriters, text processors, layout software and file formats are involved in writing (Kilshenbaum's text-processing history is a classic demonstration).

Stern's three-part swipes the full arc of sound research. The Hearable Past (2003) proves that "audience modernity" is earlier than recording technology: medical consultations, telegraphers' hearing skills have transformed ears into instruments; MP3: Meaning of a Format (2012) pioneered the "format studies": Compressed algorithms are embedded in assumptions about human hearing, format, theory; The Diminished Faculties, 2021) wrote a "harming phenomenon" from their voice after surgery, welding sound research with disability studies (chap. 12) and attaching a "user manual" for the simulation of academic practices. Stern's model is the transportability of methods: any sense, any format, any "official ability" can be studied like this -- and literary researchers learn to ask: "What is a form of novel?" How is a reading "official capacity" ever made in history?
13.3 New book history: from handwritten copy to cloud

Book history has gained second life in the digital age. Leah Price, "What are we talking about when we talk about books (2019), dissects the myth of "Deep Reading the Golden Age" with historical evidence. - Books are always read, pillowd and displayed; Whitney Tretien, Cut/Copy/Playing (2021), researched the 17th century Clipbook Workshop and used "numbers--" Paper-based co-publishing's argument is put into practice; the global history of books (how customs reviews in Indian Ocean ports shape reading, see Hofmeer, “Water colonialism”, chap. 18) has liberated the history of books from the European Centre. The other is the fan textual study: Transformative Works and Culture (found 2008 issue) and AO3 (rugo Award 2019), which makes the same person's writing a cross-section of copyright, gender and platform research. China’s CyberLicter Study (Chapter 16), together with its twin stars, constitute the Global Platform’s literary research.

Voice books are one of the fastest-growing publishing boards in the last decade, and the entry of streaming media platforms has made listening one of the mainstream reading methods. Research is then hierarchical: at the cognitive level, the general understanding of narrative texts by adults is largely comparable to that of visual reading, but the gap between complex narratives and texts that need to be looked back is widening; at the formal level, the 166-person audio publication (2017) of George Sanders' Lincoln in the Middle Ages proves that audio can be independent art, not derivatives; At the industry level, the bulk of AI ' s synthetic readings (open-combination sound books from the main platform since 2023) triggered a reader union protest and ethical discussion of "sound cloning " . The harvest of literary research is a concept that has been forced to be clarified: "read" has always been a common expression of the monotheistic practice of monotheistics. - I'm sorry. The real legacy of the struggle for reading is to give new visibility to the unique nature of silent reading.

blocks.text: The same global agenda has different shapes in different academic communities. The section scans nine major regions in turn: their respective institutional situation, hotspot orientation, representation of scholars and landmark results, and attention to the two-way flow of theory — who is importing and who is exporting.
North America: Methodological Reconstruction in Crisis

The United States and Canada remain the largest academic market for literary research and the deepest places of institutional crisis. Intermittentity goes hand in hand with innovation: black research becomes the focus of theory, sociology and computations are published to criticize redraw research subjects, and the self-understanding of disciplines is rewrited word for word.
14.1 Institutional situation: decade of contraction

The figures are stark: the American Bachelor of Arts degree has fallen by about a quarter since its peak in 2012 (see chart in chapter 23), with English specializations nearing the point of being cut off in a number of higher education institutions; the abolition of all world language specializations at the University of West Virginia in 2023 as a landmark event; and the continued replacement of tenured teaching posts by double-hatting. The response of the academic community is multi-directional: the public humanization shift (the advent of books and academic revolving doors, "semi-public intellectuals" such as Melvy Amre), the reintegration of creative writing and literary research, and the self-positioning trajectory of the theme of the annual conference of the MLA (2023 "Working conditions", 2025 "visibility"), from the direct expression of the labour situation to the reiteration of the visibility of the discipline and public values.
14.2 Theory Focus: Black Studies

The most original energy of North American literature theory in the last decade came from black research. In the Wake, 2016, Christina Sharpe named the overall situation of black life after slavery as a critical method of "wake work" with the multidimensionality of "wake"/shows — vibe, boat, wake up; The Episode (2023) continues this poetry with 248 chapters. Fred Morten and Stefano Hani ' s Underground Commons 2013, are named by the critical gesture of "in college, not university." The "critical fiction" of Saydia Hartman became the methodical output (see reading box). The common denominator of this group is the high degree of integration of theory, poetry and archival practice — it changes what North American literature is writing and how.

In Venus II (2008), Hartman proposed "critic fiction": slavery archives only preserve enslaved persons in the form of commodity lists and death records, thus replicating the norms of history; critical fictions fill the file with silence in virtual tone and narratives, while maintaining the boundaries of evidence — not fiction, but in a form that would have been written "likely". The Experiment of Indulging Life, Beautiful (2019) used the methodology for young black women who moved to northern cities in the early twentieth century, rewritten the "problem girl" in the sociological archives as a living form of experiment and received the award of the National Association of Reviewers. The method has spread to Caribbean research, First Nations research, Chinese engineering and hardworking archives research and even art exhibitions, making it one of the most successful methodological exports of the twenty-first century; The debate focused on the criteria of domination of the fictional and historical boundaries.

Another original, parallel to black research, is new formalism. Caroline Levine, Form: Whole, Motion, Level, Network (2015) introduces "feasibility" into the form analysis: form is not a container of content, but a model of organization across aesthetics and politics — the form of "pacing" shared by poetry and prison tabulations, and therefore literary criticism is naturally associated with the skills of analysing social forms. The Activist Humanist, 2023 went further by giving formalist organizational wisdom to climate politics and advocating for collective and sustainable action by scholars in the reality. From "political" to "political" the line offers a non-modern way out of post-critical formal analysis.
14.3 Publication of Sociology and the Amazonian Age literature

The new generation, represented by the Post45 Community, has adopted "Contemporary Literature" as an industrial system study. Mark McGall, "Everything Less: Amazonian novels" (2021), argues that Amazons — self-published platforms, algorithms, type breakdowns — have become the true “literacy” of contemporary novels; Dan Sinikin, Big Fiction, 2023, uses the published group-based archives and data notes “authors” as distributed products of editors, agents and marketing in the group age. The 2022 hearing of the United States Department of Justice v. Penguin Landon M & As disclosed internal data on the best-seller economy, providing a rare window of evidence for that direction. This route shares the impulse to "replace literature to the conditions of production" with the computational criticism (Chapter 3) and also shifts the criticism from the text to contracts, prepaids and algorithms.

Daniel Heath Justice, Cherokee Nation; University of British Columbia, The Why First Nations Literature is Important (2018), outlines the paradigm shift in the field: instead of turning around "the image of Indians in white literature" or the narrative of trauma, it is based on "restoration" — First Nations literature is an ongoing knowledge system, answering the question "How we belong to each other". Action 94 of the Canadian Truth and Reconciliation Commission (TRC) called for a direct rewriting of the curriculum structure of higher education institutions, which was followed by institutional formations such as the Institute of Indigenous Literature Studies (ILSA); research ethics were also rewritten: community consultation, intellectual sovereignty (OCAP principles), and oral reference norms. The case is inspired by the fact that an area can change its list of issues by changing the core metaphor — from “dwinding” to “restoration”.

blocks.text: 14.4 Observations

The paradox of North America is that the more disciplined, the more its theoretical production focuses on the "survival problem" — the usefulness of criticism, the existence of universities, and the place of literature in platform capitalism. This has given the North American agenda a strong counter-productive character; its most global proliferation output (critical fiction, awakening work, publishing sociology) has been born out of responses to local crises.
Europe: Continent of empirical, sociological and memory

Europe's comparative advantage lies in the "re-loading institutions": long-funded research institutes, transnational cooperation networks and multilingual academic traditions, which allow for the in-depth development of empirical aesthetics, computing literature, memory research and literary sociology that are difficult to replicate elsewhere.
15.1 German-speaking Community: two "scientific" routes

The German-speaking community offers two institutional programmes for the "re-scientificization" of literary research. One is the laboratory route: the Institute of Empirical Aesthetics (2013- ) in Frankfurt translates the subject of rhetoric into measurable aesthetic emotional studies (see chapter 9 for more details). Second is the language bank route: the German Research Union's priority project “Calculating Literature Research” (SP 2207) has funded two clusters since 2020, with Damstadt (Gius), Würtzburg (Jannidis), Trier (Sheshi) and Berlin as the network for methodological development, with the Journal of Computing Literature (2022-) as its position; Its style and North American "cultural analysis" form an interesting division of labour — more replicability, ascriber theory and measurement of effectiveness, rather than big propositions. The German-speaking region's hot spot on the subject of literary history is post-immigrant literature research: from "visit literature" to "post-immigration" paradigm changes, making multilingual writers (Trezin Mula, Sasha Stanišić, etc.) the focus of the mainstream discussion; Jenny Epenberg, Kairos, 2021; International Book Award 2024, was also pushed back to the research front by the Eastern German Memory and the "Wendeliteratur".
15.2 France: Sociology, Emergence and Self-Fiction

France ' s unique contribution is threefold. The literature sociology Empirical studies of world literature advanced by Sapiro et al. on the extension line of the Budieu field theory (see in-depth reading box). Text-based comedy (critique génétique), centred on the Paris Institute for Modern Texts and Texts (ITEM), studied the drafting, revision and creation process, and the digital age extended its audience to the author's computer files and digital manuscripts, which are identical to the British-American digital material study (chap. 13). The autofiction study, which was fully warmed by Annie Ernaux' Nobel Prize in 2022, claims to write as a "class defector" in the form of a literary debt, and its "écriture policy" sits on the interface between literature and social sciences, as a common case for the three agendas of emotional research, sociology and women. The legacy of the 2007 "littérature-monde" continues to promote the decentrization of French literature from "French literature and its periphery" to the world of plural French.

Sapiro is the champion of literature sociology after Budieu. Its research, which is based on the mass data that flow through translation, reveals structural inequalities in the world book market: English, as the "supercentrism" language, accounts for the largest share of translation output, while the proportion of English translated is chronically very low; To be "world literature", small languages must be crowned by intermediaries and accredited institutions in the central language (book fairs, awards, brokers, foundations). "Can we separate our work from the author?" (2020) intervene in the "No Culture" argument, and have historically turned the "Ethics of Authors and Values of Works" issue; What is a world writer? (Qu'est-ce quin'un auteur mondial?, 2024), the system gives the theory of "transnational literary arenas": the global author is the product of the international publishing system. For the World Literary War (Chapter 7), she provided what was needed in the theory of circulation and the theory of system - verifiable data and institutional descriptions.
15.3 Britain, the Lowlands and Northern Europe: a hub for the agenda

The UK is also the source of the colonial curriculum reform (Chapter 6), critical medical literature (Witcon Trust Funding System, Chapter 12) and the Theory of the World Literature (Worrick); The government has been able to create a new culture of human rights education in the United States, which was the same as the United States, which in 2023-2024, ruled out the humanities departments of many universities and triggered public debate about the death of English. The lowland countries are at the hub of the Utrecht: Ann Riggney's Memory Activism Study Group (Chapter 11) and the European Memory Research Network; Groningen (Pianzola) represents a study of digital social reading. The Nordic output is the study of the "Liction, Cognitive and Emotional" Centre of the University of Oslo (Kukonen, chap. 9) and the climate fiction in Scandinavian; The Italian Ferant research has become a transnational industry (see case box); the Spanish-language literature around "History Memory" (the memory of civil war, the narrative of grave digging) and Latin America form the dialogue between the Atlantic and the Atlantic (chap. 19).

"Who is Elena Ferrant" is the specimen of a digital approach that is in conflict with critical ethics. Italian journalists identified the translator, Laía, as the "Frand" in 2016, with the book publisher's accounts and real estate records, and public opinion was soared — the "bottom of the line" was widely condemned by critics; In 2017, the University of Padova team (Tuzi and Cortero) organized an international physiology workshop, with several teams conducting independent analysis of the characterization of the word frequency and semantics 150 contemporary Italian novels, with the result of a highly consistent orientation towards the writer Domenico Stallone. The debate is moving to the methodological and ethical layers: whether statistical attribution counts as evidence? Even if so, should the academic community respect the anonymous choice of authors? The Felant study then split into two pieces — one continuing the world phenomenon analysis (global acceptance of Naples's four-part song, gender politics in "Ferantje"), and one using the case as a methodological and ethical case for the attribution of authors. It reminds all those who calculate the critics that technical feasibility and academic legitimacy are two issues.

blocks.text: 15.4 Transnational infrastructure: the bottom of the European model

Sustainability of the European agenda is derived from a two-tiered structure of financing and infrastructure. (a) Long-cycle funding by the European Research Council (ERC) has made five-year collective projects such as "Memory Activism" (Rigni) a norm; The EU-level "CLS INFRA, 2021-" integrated language materials, tools and training, and the two major Humanistic Infrastructure Alliances, DARIAH and CARIN, provided data floors; and the Open Access Policy (Plan S) re-engineered the flow of academic publications. In contrast, North America is more dependent on foundations and star scholars, Europe is more dependent on institutions - This is the physical source of the differences in the style of cross-Straits research.
15.5 Observations

The key word for the European model is infrastructure: to sink the methodology into research, funding lines and journals, rather than relying on the personal agenda of star scholars. It is inherently edifying: academic Englishization and multilingual tradition of sawing (the German philosophies are increasingly speaking English, and the linguistic discipline is being reduced); and the pressure on memory research and gender studies (memory law, gender degree retention) by right-wing populist politics — Europe is also a major town and storm eye in these fields.
China Mainland and Hong Kong/Austro: Digital, Network and Autonomous Knowledge Systems

The Chinese academic community operates three sets of agendas: online literature and science fiction research for new audiences, digital infrastructure for classical resources, and theoretically based "autonomous knowledge systems"; The Chinese language is the subject of a deep-seated international debate between the Chinese language and the history of translation.
16.1 Organic: neo-literacy and classical sciences

The policy context shapes the agenda: the New Arts (comprehensively launched in 2020) encourages cross-sections of arts and technology, providing a structured window for the establishment of digital humanities centres (Chonghua, North China, People's Republic of China, South China, Hong Kong and Shanghai); The requirement to "prompt the construction of China's autonomous knowledge system" since 2022 has made the construction of the "three systems" of literature theory (scientific, academic, linguistic) a prominent task. Another remarkable formation is classical: 2024, the first World Classical Congress was held in Beijing in November, and many higher education institutions set up a platform for classical science or research. • The repertoire of the classical resources of the Middle West is explicitly incorporated into the agenda of the mutual understanding of civilizations at the national level. The interdisciplinary aspects of digital humanities (specialized publications, annual conferences, reporting systems) are addressed in chapter 3 and are not redundant here.
16.2 Research in online literature: evolution with objects

Chinese online literature, which constitutes the largest platform literary ecology in the world with hundreds of millions of users and a large annual word change, has been researched from "shouldn't we be studying " to "how to build a paradigm." The team of Shaolin at Beijing University is the logo: the "network" (database writing, fan economy, euphoria mechanism) that proposes the "media revolution" as the overarching framework for understanding online literature, "Break the Wall: Keywords for Cyberculture" (2018) completes the academic code of subcultural vocabulary, and the annual selection for many years has built up a regular mechanism of criticism with the "Annual Report on CyberLiction"; O'Yuyang Qui (Central South University) represents the systematic route and permanently hosts the CyberLicter Study Base and the annual Blue Book. Four growth points on the agenda at hand: the re-engineering of the free reading model and the platform economy; the migration of narratives in IP adaptations of industrial chains (theatrical, animation, games); the "out of the sea" study-- – The original ecologicalism of WebNovel, the scale of AI-assisted translation and the acceptance of national aspirations by the overseas reader community; the competition for micro-trip plays with the media (new target since 2024). The discussions on the study of the same culture (platform governance and fan work after the AO3) and the gender perspective (feminine frequency, delay) are also self-contained.

Song Ming-hyun is a central critic who pushed China's science fiction "New Wave" into the world literary research agenda. He has continued to argue since the early 2010s that contemporary science fiction, represented by Liu Zeok-hyun, Han Song and Wang Jinkang, is not a type of entertainment, but rather a poetry of the most ideologically powerful literary scene in contemporary China, Science Fiction, which presents an experience that realism cannot be handled positively. The English monograph, "See of Seeing, 2023, China Science Fiction" systematizes the proposition that the "New Wave" of science fiction is essentially a form of perception politics that allows invisible people (technology acceleration, history dark side, others) to take form. He and his collaborators have made a selection of English translations of science fiction and a large number of guides, which constitute one of the main routes for China's science fiction into English. The methodological significance of its work is to demonstrate the "text-based" world literature path: the global circulation of type literature, which is rewriting the old narrative about the place of Chinese literature in the world.
16.3 The scene of criticism: the new northeast, the new south and the voodoo writing

The hotspots of contemporary criticism have emerged in recent years. New Northeast Literature is formed around Shenyang writers like Snow-tao, Ban Woo, Jung-Jin, and others. It is read as a "child" account of the lateness of the 1990s' layoff history — the return of the working-class memory through suspicion and black aesthetics; and the "New South Writing" (Yan Qingxiang, etc.) is based on the literary coordinates of the wet, obscurantious maritime senses that are shifting from the centre of the North, using the Qianang, Guangxi, Hainan and even South China. The two names together show the urge of the critical world to reorganize contemporary literary maps with "situation plus generation" and also trigger a debate on the method of "naming before creating". Next to this is the warming of non-fiction and writing: since 2010 when the People's Literature set the paradigm of the "non-fiction" column and the "Liang" series, the expressist Juan Juanjo The "I deliver delivery in Beijing" (2023) workers say that they are becoming public events, and the "spoken writing" intermediary mechanism (publication planning, soybean and podcast communities, writing groups) is becoming a new subject in literary sociology.
16.4 Theory: Explanatory, Diversion and Narrative Learn.

The most organized advance in theory was the discussion of interpretation initiated by Zhang Jiang (China Academy of Social Sciences): from "compulsory interpretation" (2014, which criticized Western literature as pre-emptively imposed on the text) to "public interpretation" (207, which advocates a public rationality of interpretation), which led to "explaination major discussions" on platforms such as the Chinese Social Sciences, which lasted for nearly a decade, and to dialogue with international scholars (as before Hillis Miller was born) whose ambition was to construct a contemporary system of interpretation in China. In comparison, the mutation of Cao Shunqing (chaps. 2 and 7) is the original framework of China, which is a small group of countries that have entered international circulation; Wang Ning has long promoted dialogue between China and the West on the subject of world literature, post-modernity and post-human beings. Narratives is another advantage: the "hidden process/dual narrative drive" of Shindan University has triggered monographs in international journals such as Sylle, which has become a landmark case for the internationalization of original narrative theory by Chinese scholars; The audio narrative study of Fushuyan (Jangxi Teacher Training University) is in line with the International Voice Study (chap. 13). Moreover, literature geography (ex-Dahing, Maxilin) and eco-aesthetics (ex-supplendant, chapter 5) represent the building of disciplines from China's traditional resources.
16.5 Hong Kong, Australia: Chinese language and history of translation

The Sinophone studies are one of the most controversial frameworks in the last two decades: the UCLA version emphasizes "anti-dissemination" and "diversion" and points the finger at various centralisms; Wang Dewey (Harvard) was re-formed as a "Waven" open map of continental literature, and its editor-in-chief, Harvard's New History of Modern Chinese Literature (English 2017), illustrated another literary history with hundreds of time-sliced "scripts" of the "Stars" system. The substance of the debate is the renegotiation of the boundaries and methods of "Chinese literature/Chinese language" and there is a systematic response from the continental community. On Taiwan, the annual conference of several humanities (DADH) has been held since 2009, and the worldwideization of Taiwan literature (the international travel of Wu Myung-li works) has become a hotspot; Hong Kong's advantage is to translate the history of research and translation - Wang Hongji (China University) The Dialogue between Dragons and Lions (2022) rewrites the history of Chinese encounters with the translation files of the Magarni Mission, demonstrating the method of translating history as a history of thought.

The English translation of The Triple (Liu Yukuntue, 2014) won the Hugo Prize in 2015 for the best long title. This was the first time the award was awarded to a translator; the trilogy was translated into more than 30 languages and was put at the centre of global discussions in the 2024 Naify drama. The research community has drawn from it a number of topics: translation studies focusing on Liu Yuqun's translator strategy and the "born translation" mechanism (the cosmic scale of the subject is naturally beneficial for cross-cultural flows); world literature studies test the reverse circulation model of "margin to centre"; acceptance of research to track its misappropriation by the scientific and political community abroad; and national critical wars around "sci-fi realism", technological excellence and human narratives. The structural inspiration of the Three Body case is that the type of literature has become the largest port of transit for the world of Chinese literature, a fact that is challenging the institution of world literature and research centred on pure literature.
