blocks.text: 16.6 Observations

The Chinese issue is still asymmetrical: China’s main route to the English language is the Chinese language and science fiction, while the international acceptance of the original theory of China (variate, hidden processes, public interpretation) is still at an early stage. Two structural changes are of concern: first, online literature and digital humanities give China exclusive data and object advantages on the two tracks of Platform Literature Research and Classical Knowledge Engineering; Second, how to create a virtuous tension rather than isolation between the construction objectives of the autonomous knowledge system and the demands of international dialogue will determine the world position of Chinese literature research in the next decade.
Japan and Korea: Classics AI, Post-earthquake Literature and Universalization

Japan has established two highlands in the classical AI interpretation and post-earthquake literature, while Korea has completed the "K" worldization narrative before and after Hanjiang won the Novo Prize - a joint demonstration of how the national translation and data infrastructure has changed a country's literature.
17.1 Japan: The Eye of the Machine of the Classic

The most spectacular result of Japanese digital literature is the "kuzushiji" IA recognition of the body of grass. The Japanese history of modern books and writings is in the millions, but less than one in ten thousand people can read the dead. - The digitalized images are still "visible, unread" dead files. The Tarin Clanuwat team of the Open Humanities Data Centre (CODH) has identified the entire page of the National Library of Literature Training and Identification Model for Data: KuroNet (2019), which attracted thousands of teams to the Kaggle Global Competition that same year; and the use of Miwo for the public mobile phone application in 2021, which allows anyone to take a photo of an ancient book page. This chain - digitalization of the collection (NijL "History" cause) - labels data sets open competitions public applications - the textbook path that has been widely referred to as "Human AI"; Kranua subsequently joined the Google Sub-Citizen Research Team to continue the classical literature AI research. Its academic legacy is emerging: the large-scale foreign comparison of the Edo version, the author's clues to the writing of the original, and even the entire history of the "unreadable archives" have been made impossible.
17.2 Japan: Post-earthquake Literature and World Literature

The "3/11" East Japan earthquake and the Fukushima nuclear accident have given rise to the category of criticisms of post-earthquake literature: the "Silient Earthquake Literatures" (post-earthquake literature, 2013) by Yoko Kimura University and its follow-up claims that nuclear disasters have forced literature to re-manage invisible pollution, the ethics of asylum and the breakdown of the "security narrative"; Rachel Di Nitto of the English-language community, Fukushima Fiction, 2019, connects it to global nuclear and ecological criticism (chap. 5). The Dowaya Leaver (Lights Presenter, 2014; English translation of The Emissary Literature Award for National Book Awards) became a worldwide specimen of this category. In the field of world literature, the translation and discussion of "world literature" by the rich marshlands has shaped public awareness, while the "world literature" by Autumn Junichiro "is made" (2020) has been shaped in Japanese with book history and selected studies to deconstruct the construction of the "world literature" domain - a systemic criticism of the English community (chap. 7). Aside from this, the World Circulation Study of the Village Spring Tree has become an independent small industry, with the International Literary Library of the University of Preah Vihear (2021 library) providing it with an archive set up; and the 9th Rijiang's 5% ChatGPT's Mustachekawa Prize (Chapter 4) has pushed Japan into the forefront of the AI literary debate. Theoretically, the Scramblewalker has completed its "Screen of Exchange" system and won the Boglu Wisdom Award (first Asian winner) -- The critic who recasts the literary history methodology with The Origin of Modern Japanese Literature once again demonstrated Japan ' s export power in criticizing the world ' s theoretical market.

The academic trajectory of Riko Kimura is itself a microcosm of contemporary Japanese literature research: she started her research in the peace arts and women's court writings, but became a critical term for post-earthquake literature after 2011. The central proposition of the post-earthquake literature: To Think about New Japanese Literature (2013) and its successor, tracking nuclear disaster writing year after year, is that after Fukushima, "if nothing happens" becomes itself an interpretational literary device — writing about nuclear catastrophes and how to place radiation invisibility, becomes an unconvincing form for every writer. She uses the fine reading techniques of the palace literary training for contemporary texts, demonstrating the current path of classical scholars ' involvement; It also makes "disaster and literature" the interface open to environmental humanities, trauma studies (chaps. 5, 11) in Japanese literature. Japanese criticism thus contributes to a portable category: "post-disaster" is not a subject, but a condition for description.
17.3 Korea: "K Literature" research before and after the Novo Prize

The Nobel Prize for Literature (the first female winner in Asia) in 2024 was the coronation of the 20-year project "Lictology Worldization" in Korea: the system-funded LTI Korea, the Vegetables International Book Award (2016) and its translation contest (chap. 7), and the Guangzhou and Jeju Monuary (chap. 11) chapter form the complete chain, where the translation of the world's writers has the clearest archives. After the Nobel Prize, the K Literary Research has rapidly expanded in international science, and is a branch of Korean-Treat Research, combined with K-pop, Korean Theatre Research. The forward agenda of Korean mainland includes: the formalization of feminist literature - a wave of criticisms triggered by the circulation of the Kim Ji-young (2016) phenomenon 82, and the reconstruction of the spectrum with the Seven-volume Korea Women's Literature Selected (2024) (chap. 10); the rise of K-SF (scientific phantom and criticism of new writers such as the Golden Grass); the industrial-narrative studies of web novels (web novel) and webton, and the Chinese online studies (chap. 16), which constitute a double sample of East Asian platform literature; and the continuation of the tradition of literary research that divides institutional and cold war memories in the new generation.

Zhao Nam-joo's 82nd birthday, Kim Ji-young (82nd birthday, 2016) novel, which is based on the life of "mean women" has sold over a million books in Korea and has become a phenomenon-class best seller in Japan, mainland China and Taiwan. Its East Asia travel provides a rare real-time sample of comparative literature: the same text activates different gender debates in different societies (South Korea's idol, "Presentation", Japanese "Resonance" reading, Chinese-language marriage discussions), with readers completing cross-border "relay criticism" with short assessments of bean petals, Twitter topics and online readings. Researchers have extracted topics from them such as the sociological "data novel" style (incorporated statistics and footnotes), the market mechanisms for feminist publishing and the framework for comparability in the structure of the East Asian Confucian family. It is evidence of a new pattern of contemporary world literature research: the real-time occurrence of circulation and theorem in social media, and the shift of critical "archives" from journals to platforms.

blocks.text: 17.4 Observations

The Japanese-Korean case has shown that the "research heat" of a country's literature can be significantly altered by infrastructure and policy — Japan's cataloguing data chain has lowered the threshold for classical research, and South Korea's translation support has sent its contemporary literature to the world's cycle of criticism. The inspiration for other linguistic communities (the text of which is the text) is direct: the status of the world literature is determined not only by the text but also by the continued investment in data, translation and establishment.
South and South-East Asia: multilingual sites and the Indian Ocean

South Asia is the largest test site for the worldwide challenge of "multilingualism": the formalization of Dalit literature, the methodology of multilingualism, the sea-road perspective of Indian Ocean research, and the fierce competition for curriculum politics; The study in South-East Asia contributed a unique framework with cold war, alienation and archipelagic thinking.
18.1 Dalit Literature Studies: From Witness to Poem

The Dalit, "the oppressed" literary research has completed the transformation of the status of the literature from social literature to the subject of literary research over the past decade. Laura Brueck (2014) ' s study of Indian-language Dalit novels was one of the turning points: she argued that the Dalit text was not only a testimony of caste violence, but also a more conscious narrative technique and internal debate (the criticism of male orthodox by the female Dalit author). The agenda has since continued: comparisons of Dalit writings in various languages (Malati, Tamil, Telugu), Dalit feminism (automated theory of Urmila Pawal, etc.), the orthodox effect of English translations, and the "who has the right to study Dalit literature" debate. New generations of memoirs like Yasika Dout, "Dallit Out" (2019), bring issues into the global media; This comparison is further fuelled by the repertoire of Dalit studies and African-American studies (caste-race comparison theory) that are forming an emerging trans-ocean community, Isabel Wilcson ' s Caste 2020 best sell.
18.2 Multilingual, Bibliography and Curriculum Politics Towards the Mass

The "multilingual place" in Orsini represents the greatest methodological contribution of South Asia to the world literary battle: Hindi in North India - Urdu-Possi is a model of literature life that is often multilingual rather than monolingual. It is accompanied by "Book to the Mass": serious research into market printing (bazaar print), brochures, film lyrics, oral epics, and the dismantling of the "classic/common" pre-set border. At the institutional level, the curriculum initiated by India's National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 is re-organised as a debate: Indian Knowledge Systems (Indian Knowy Systems) enters the curriculum, which supporters view as a colonial educational sovereignty practice, and critics fear that it will be used as a monolithic cultural nationalism - the literary curriculum (Shaspia vs. the Sanskrit classics, Urdu status, the Dalit text). This "decolonization or recentrization" saw is an example of Chapter 6 going to the colonial within-the-counter criticism at the national level.

Hofmeier's work spans Africa and South Asia and is one of the definitionaries of "Library Research in the Indian Ocean". The Gandhi Printing Centre (2013) examines how Gandhi's international printing facility in Durban, South Africa, produces a political ethic of " slow reading" — printing, translation and community shaping each other on the Indian Ocean voyage. The Dockside Reading, 2022, proposes a "hydrocolonism" framework: colonial power is exercised through waters — customs, the imperial literary theorists, ban, stamp, classify books on the docks; the history of books must therefore move from the library to ports, cabins and tidal bands. This perspective combines the history of books, environmental humanities and post-colonial studies, and has given rise to a series of agendas (monsoon archives, island literature, media history of submarine cables) on the Indian Ocean humanities. The inspiration for world literature research is that the world is never material, but customs-based - not abstract space, but a network of routes shaped by hydrology and customs.
18.3 South-East Asia: Cold War, Islands and Separation

Theoretical sense of literature research in South-East Asia has increased significantly in recent years. The "world-building" theory of Shen Yongping (Berkeley) (Chapter 7), with post-colonial novels in South-East Asia as its main case, advocates for literature to open up alternative normative forces of a temporal nature; Gini King Watson, Cold War Rekonings, 2021, studies how authoritarianism in the Asian-Pacific decolonization process is being witnessed in literature and received the American Society of Comparative Literature Weylecker Award — the Cold War Cultural Research is thus a pivotal agenda for research in South-East Asia (and East Asia) and echoes the global Cold War aesthetic research network. Country-specific hotspots include the re-evaluation of Indonesian literature inspired by Eka Kuniwan, Indonesia (Beautiful is a scar, Cantik Itu Luka, multilingual translation, nomination for the Buke International Award); The long-term study of the nation, Chinese humanity and literature by Caroline Hau of the Philippine Institute; the institutionalization of Singapore's “SingLit” (State-funded, bi-annual prize and multilingual creative ecology) and its criticism; Viet Nam's discrete literature has become a common quote (chap. 11) for the study of war memory, following the continued heat of the Pulitzer Prize (2016) in Viet Thanh Nguyen, which "all wars are fought twice, once in the battlefield, once in memory". In the regional approach, the "archippelagic thinking" — transposed from the concept of the Caribbean in Grisanne — is being used to reshape the ocean-island literary geography of South-East Asia, echoing the Indian Ocean.

From Johannesburg, "water colonialism" became a specific sample of a cross-regional research programme — a "theoretical multipolarity" (chap. 1). The concept's proliferation path is clear: the Hofmail team has trained multi-country doctoral students in the "Oceanic Humanities for the Global South" project at the University of Witwatersland, where the album and workshop brought the framework to Mumbai, Cape Town and the Caribbean; the research has expanded from customs interdiction to monsoon writing, multilingual printing in port cities, and island narratives of sea level rise; Blue Man (Chapter 5) provides a interface to northern schools. Two points of the methodology are: first, the units of the history of national literature are bypassed from "routing" rather than "territory" ; Second, water is both a metaphor and a material condition — the ship carrying the letter, the docks inspected, the inundated files. The Southern concept is retrogressively entered into the Northern classroom, and is most typical in this case.

blocks.text: 18.4 Observation

South and South-East Asia provides two scarce items for the global agenda: methods to deal with extreme multilingualism (multilinguality, literature towards the general population) and units for analysis of ocean scales (Indian Ocean, archipelagos). The risks are equally clear: the nationalistization of curriculum politics may be a counter-editor of academic autonomy; while the interest of the English community in the region remains concentrated on English writing and separated writers, and the international visibility of indigenous criticism traditions in all languages remains inadequate.
Latin America: Extractive, violent writing and theoretical output

The future generations of the "literacy explosion" no longer explain Latin America to the world, but rather to the world: extractive criticism, deposed writing ethics, writing -- survival and indigenous ideas, along with a wave of dark aesthetics of women writers, constitute the most productive regional agenda at this time.
19.1 Extractive criticism: aesthetics at the front of the resource

Extractivismo - development model with minerals, soybeans, oil, etc. The Macarena Gómez-Barris, the Extractive Zone, 2017, has turned it into a cultural criticism: the colonial "exploitation eye" has reduced land and people to extractable resources on the front lines of resources (the Andean mining region, the Amazon oil fields, the hydroelectric reservoirs); and the local image, behavioural art and literature preserves "submerspected perspective" — a perception that the extraction logic is not exhausted. The framework, along with the Energy Humanities (Chapter 5), Water Colonialism (Chapter 18), constitutes a triangle of ecological criticism of the global South, which has led to a series of studies on soybeans, mine writing, water-coyms; Brazil has also expanded on the same track as the Andean community of science.
19.2 Violence: The Dark Turn of New Goth and Feminism

The most internationally criticized literary phenomenon in contemporary Latin America is the treatment of structural violence by women writers, who write with terror, Goth and body: the ghost of Mariana Enriques (Argentina) is the return of the dictator "disappeared"; Fernanda Melcho (Mexico) Temporada de huracanes, 2017, who displays the fear of female violence in the language of the hurricane season; and Monica Ohda (Ecuador), who writes the fear of teenage girls in the digital age. The main thrust of the theory of the criticalist community, in the context of "Singote Latina" and "Andean Goth", is that Goth is not an imported text, but rather a daily response to disappearances, drug-trafficking wars and feminicidio — where terror is an extension of realism. And in parallel, the feminist theory matures: The analysis of Rita Segator's "Teaching for Power" of female murder has been widely borrowed from literary criticism since the NiunaMenos campaign; The poetry of "go to possess" by Christine Rivera Garza (see reading box) provides an ethical programme for writing about violence.

Mexican writer Vera Garza, theorist, in Los muertos indóciles, 2013, proposes "deposit" (desapropiación) (despropiación) poetry: in Mexico, where the digital age and violence are normalized by everyone, the ethics of writing are not to take possession of the experience of others to achieve "author" but to remove possession — to make the text reveal its community's origin (archives, testimonies, language of others) and to make writing work in collaboration with the dead and living. This theory was validated in her own practice: El invincible summer (El invenciblé verano de Liliana, 2021; English version 2023) restarted the pending murder of her sister by her boyfriend in 1990, and authored a non-fictional masterpiece "Deposit" with a file, letter and friend testimony, which won the Pulitzer Prize (rememberal of memory) in 2024. As the founder of the Dr. Doctors for Creative Writing in Spanish at Houston University, she also converted the poetry into a teaching institution. The three main sources of theory, creation and organization made her one of the most frequently cited authors of contemporary Latin American literature research.
19.3 Entry of Brazilian and Aboriginal thought

Brazil has contributed two independent leads. One is Conceição Evaristo, "Screvivência," a black writer from a slum, who conflates "writing" with "living" as a word — a writing by a black woman that is not a renewal of life, but rather a continuation of survival itself and a transfer of collective memory. The concept has become a programmatic dimension of research in Afro-Brazilian literature and has entered into criticism in Portuguese-speaking Africa; the study surrounding her late regularization (national sales, college specialization) and the barometer of Brazilian race politics. The second is the entry of the theory of indigenous thought: Ideias para adiar o fom do mundo, 2019, asking the realm of "humans" in oral philosophies -- "ends" -- which have already happened to indigenous peoples many times; David Coperawa, "The Falling Sky" (La Chute du Ciel, co-author with the anthropologist Albert), is used as a close-up to the American cosmology. Criticism has begun to quote these texts as theoretical rather than nationalistic material — a shift in knowledge in itself that is mutually confirmed by Chapter 6 “Southern Awareness”.

(a) Evaristo, when he published his work in the annual Black Literature Journal, Cadernos Negros, in the 1990s, the Brazilian Academy almost did not recognize the category "Black Women's Literature"; Thirty years later, "escrevivência" entered the university entrance examination and the postgraduate dissertation collection, and she herself became the central figure in literary festivals and academic conferences - a trajectory that itself is the subject of research. The questions that scholars have drawn from these include: what intermediaries are the intermediaries on which to rely for "late to orthodox" on marginal writing (the black movement, a new generation of scholars after quota policies, diversity in the publishing market)? Is the success of the concept associated with the risk of domestication (from resistance vocabulary to test-standard answers)? The writing of "survival" is similar to the family of North America's "critical fiction" (chap. 14), and suggests a larger picture: black women on both sides of the Atlantic are independently creating "a method of writing to repair file violence" — a comparative study that has just begun.

blocks.text: 19.4 Observation

The digital end of the Latin American regional study (Mexico RedHD digital humanist network, etc.) is relatively low, and its international visibility is concentrated on theoretical and contemporary literary criticism; Ignacio Sanchez Prado et al., "The marginal correction" of the world literature battle (the Mexican case shows that marginal literature fields are neither submissive nor confrontational to the global market, but rather strategic diversion) and the continued supply method. In general, Latin America is the most mature example of the "Theory from the South": its conceptual output is based on a close connection between sports (feminism, indigenous peoples, environmental protection) and writing practice - The theory is cycling between the street and the text, and that is what it is.
Africa: Futureism, ecological media and digital networks

Ngugi's death is the end of a half-century of "linguistics" debate. At the same time, African aforelifeism re-written the textual map, ecological media research re-evaluated the cost of the planet, digital platforms reorganized the production and criticism of literature — and African literature research is at the threshold of the agenda for change.
20.1 Language issues and publishing sovereignty

The global quest for the "language of Africa" that was triggered by the passing of Ngugui Wa Tiango in 2025 brought back to the stage the old question of "what to write Africa" (chapter 6 case box). The new generation of answers no longer rest on the declaration, but on infrastructure: the Cassava Republic, Lagos/London, founded by Bibi Bakare-Youssuf, represents the "publication of sovereignty" route — African books should be priced, fixed, sealed and re-exported from Africa; The revival of publishing and awards in Swahili, Hausa and Amharic, and research into the "market literature" in Hausa and the "literature" of Yoruba images-literacy, constitute the empirical basis for the "multilingual African literature study". In academic institutions, the Association of African Literature (ALA) has been critical of the Community's ongoing debate: whether the global success of English African literature (Adiche and others, "New Wave of African Literature") has broken out or is it new to be attached to-- The criticism of what Edoro calls "the marketability of a single African story" is the latest form of this argument.
20.2 African Futureism and Literature Rewriting

Nnedi Okorafor openly distinguished between African Futureism and the American language of AfroFuturism: the former is rooted in the continent's cosmological vision and technological reality, without the need to bypass the middleman of the African black experience. This term was rapidly adopted by the academic community, which led to systematic research into science fiction in Africa: from the reinterpretation of Tutoola and the tradition of "ghostology" to criticism of selected science fiction publications such as Omenana and Africanfutust, to transnational networks of writers-authors such as Wole Talabi. Parallel to this is the African Goth and Eco-Scientific Fiction - in the climate crisis context, Lagos has flooded narratives, the drought in the Sahel and Sun Punk, and Africa's variants have become a new case bank for environmental humanities (chap. 5). The overall effect of literature research is that African literature research has been disengaged from the old framework of "witness and development" and has acquired critical language for future times.

Ikhka, an ethnic Nigerian scholar, has matured on behalf of African ecological criticism. Naturalizing Africa, 2017, critical of the central human agenda of mainstream ecological criticism, advocates for the "split dynamics" of human and inhuman life in African literature - The victims and actors in the oil pollution narrative of the Niger Delta were never human beings. Africa Ecomedia, 2021) goes further by extending the analysis from literature to film, photography and electronic waste: the material costs of global media ecology (coltan mining, Ghana electronic waste fields) are borne by Africa, where video practice is precisely visualizing the "resource history of the medium" — a book that has received several awards from the Institute, making the "ecological media" the pole of cross-cutting environmental humanities and media research (chaps. 5, 13). The work of Ihka demonstrated a structure whereby the blind zone of the Northern theory was modified with African materials and the revised framework was exported as a general tool.
20.3 Digital Networks: The New Public Level of Criticism

Production and criticism of African literature is being reorganized on digital platforms. The blog Brittle Paper, created by Ainehi Edoro, has become a public critical hub of African literature. The book is published here, the debate and the annual list, and its self-location is the "Data Home of African Literature" ; On the academic side, Shola Adnekan, " African Literature in the Digital Age (2021), studies online writing economy between Lagos and Nairobi writers, and Kinianiva Valyaura, etc., tracks Swahili tweets and whatsApp serials. Three main points of the study were: digital platforms bypassed the paper-based colonial legacy bottlenecks (print costs, distribution networks, London gatekeepers); social media criticism formed a "second circle of criticism" without college intermediaries; but platform capital and algorithm visibility created new door-keeping structures. This Digital African Literature Study and China Cybertext Research (Chapter 16) and Platform Reading Research (Chapter 8) constitute three interactive sites for the Global Platform literary research.

In 2021, two events were simultaneously recast to the world map of African literature: Abdulrazak Gulnah received the Nobel Prize for Literature, and refugee and Indian Ocean writers of Tanzanian origin entered the global curriculum overnight from chronic stock-outs - the "Gurna effect" - with the following: how the previously unsuspected works were re-prioritized, and how the Swahili world along the coast of East Africa was able to enter world literature research (with Indian Ocean human flow, chap. 18); and in the same year, Senegalese writer Mohamed Mbougar Sarr became the first sub-Saharan African writer to receive the Zangur Prize - with the most secret memory of people (La plus secrète mémore des hommes, 2021) The novel about the African writer "Burned and destroyed by Paris" is itself a fictional note to Casanova's "Lictatorial Republic of the World" (chap. 7). The two trophys synthesized a study on how recognized machines (the prize, publication, translation) continue to determine the visibility of African literature — and how Africa's critical community regains pricing power.

blocks.text: 20.4 Observation

The uniqueness of the African agenda lies in "criticism and infrastructure": the answer to the language question is publishing houses, the answer to the public criticism is blogs and communities, and the answer to the orthodox question is prizes and returns – the theoretical argument has always been anchored in material formations. The risk for the international academic community is selective absorption: the futureism and English novels receive over-focus, while native criticism, oral research and the rebuilding of the university system (funds, libraries, periodicals) in all languages remain seriously under-funded.
Middle East and the Arab world: digital humanities and classical re-evaluation

Arabic studies are advancing along three lines: digital engineering to rewrite the history of literature in millions of classical literature, a classical re-evaluation of the "depressive" case, and contemporary criticisms of dealing with prison, revolutionary and war experiences - The Palestinian narrative study became the focus of global attention after 2023.
21.1 Digital Arab: Issues of Change in Size

Arabic is one of the largest written traditions of the former modern world, and digitization is transforming this whole volume into a methodological advantage. The KITAB project and Open Islamic Book Initiative, sponsored by Sarah Bowen Savant, and OpenITI, have created thousands of machine-readable text that tracks references, copying and rewrite networks across the millennium using the "text reuse" algorithm -- – For the first time, the "reproduction ecology" of medieval Arabic book culture became measurable: which texts are the hubs, how compilers "repeat" and how memory works work across generations. The accompanying manuscript OCR (specialized identification for Arabic alphabets) and programmatic texts such as the Declaration: The Arabic Digital Manuage, makes Arabic the third classical language after Latin, classical Chinese, with conditions for large-scale computation of the literary history. This project is in an interesting contrast to the Japanese collapse of the word chain (Chapter 17): it's also "unreadable archives" while identifying and reusing the network.
21.2 Classical re-evaluation: farewell to Decay

The old framework of the history of Arabic science reduced the 13-18th century to a "decline" (inhitat), and the 19th century (nahda) to a product of European influence — both of which were systematically overturned over the past decade. Muhsin al-Musawi, Colombia) The Republic of the Islamic Ummah in the Middle Ages (2015) has demonstrated that the post-classical era is a multi-centre knowledge network spanning Cairo, Damascus and Delhi, and that the dictionary, notes and encyclopedia are a dynamic form rather than a rigid symptom; Thomas Bauer, Menst, The Culture of Distinction (German 2011; English translation 2021) suggests that the former modern Islamic society was characterized by institutionalized "tolerance of differences" - – The term multidimensional, law-based and poetry-based duality is the normal pattern of civilization, and monopolization is modern violence. Rehabilitating research: Rebecca C. Johnson's Stranger Novel (2021) rewrites the history of Arab novels into translation — from the multiple arab translations of Robinson to the rewrite workshop that was carried out in the press, "The Century of Translation" to replace "The Century of Simulations"; Hoda El Shacry ' s Koran (2020) demonstrates how Koranic poetry is used as a formal resource rather than a background for North African novels. The group worked together to complete a paradigm shift at the textbook level.

The Lebanese scholar El-Aris provides the most complete theoretical description of "Arabics in the digital age". (a) Test and Modernity (2013) rereading the text of the renaissance with physical and emotional moves, which has shown its orientation towards "de-conception" of the literary history; Leaks, Hacks, and Scandals, 2019, turn directly to the writing ecology around the Arab Spring: when the wiki leaks, tweets and blog novels become a common form of politics and literature, the classical category "fadh" is revived in digital conditions — literature no longer functions as a "exposure" to the public domain, as the author does as a hacker. His editor, Arab Modern Book Selected (2018), provided a toolbox for the revival of the scriptures in English. The methodological point of its work is to change the word "number" from "tool problem" to "literate problem" - – Figures are not a means of studying Arabic science, but rather a condition in which Arabic science is taking place.
21.3 Prisons, War and Palestinian Narratories

The focus of contemporary criticism has been imposed by regional history. (a) Prison literature (adab al-sujun) became a core category after the Syrian civil war: Miriam Cook, "Dancing in Damascus" (2017), "Dignity aesthetics" created during the war, and Sheria Taleghani, "The Syrian Prison Literature Law" (2021), which combes the poetry and human rights archives of this category; Texts such as Mustafa Khalifa ' s " Shell " (al-Qawqaʿa / Qu ' u ' u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u u Palestinian narrative studies have risen sharply since October 2023: the Edward Sayed Memorial Lecture of Isabella Hamad, "Recognition of strangers" (in 2024 book), asks about the political conditions of narrative "recognition"; Gazan scholar — The poem "If I had to die" has become a pressing subject of criticism in dozens of languages around the world, following the death of the poet Rifat Alaril; Sayid's "permission to narrative" concept was generally re-established. At the institutional level, the International Arab Books Award (IPAF) continues to reshape contemporary orthodoxism, and the relationship between the literary infrastructure of Gulf capital (book fairs, awards, translation funds) and criticism of autonomy is a structural issue that the academic community continues to ask.

The flagship presentation of the KITAB team revolved around the 10th century historian, The History of the Prophet and the King: algorithms mark the later text of the long paragraphs shared with them in millions of pages, producing a "reuse map" over 800 years -- who's copying him, which paragraphs, and what has been deleted. The outcome subverted two intuitions: first, the modern half of "original/repeated" was totally inadequate to the culture of copying, and was re-used as a formal mechanism for the survival of knowledge; Second, some of the seemingly marginal compilers are super nodes of the dissemination network. In a methodology, the OpenITI, algorithms (in Arabic for Passim) and visualization set open standards for Arabic digital humanities; in theory, it provides a long-term reference for "authority" studies — when combined with the European "author function" spectrum — the default unit of world literary history (author-work) itself is relativated.

blocks.text: In addition to Arabic, there are other identifiable movements in the Persian language and Turkish linguistic community in the region. Iran: After the protests "Women, Life, Freedom" in 2022, the protests against poetry, writing in prison and research published in exile have dramatically increased, with the separation of the Iranian academic community from Europe as the main position; The translation history of Rumi's "de-Islamization" in the English-speaking world is a classic case of political translation. Turkey: The Pamuk research remains an international entry point, the world circulation in Tapinar leads to a re-evaluation of "Turkish modernism", and Osmanism is re-evaluated using the "Society Culture" framework (see 21.2) and the rescue studies of Kurdish language are being conducted in parallel.
21.4 Observations

The region's deepest methodological output is the unbundling of the narrative of "modernity = westernization": the century of the ambivalent culture, the civil Republic and the translation have demonstrated that another file of modernity of knowledge has existed, waiting for the right framework and tools. The urgent short-scatter is equally clear: the destruction of the lives of universities, libraries and scholars by war (Syria, Yemen, Gaza) makes the "research conditions themselves" an ethical issue — digital archives are not just a method, but also a rescue.
Russia, Ukraine and Central and Eastern Europe: Decolonisation shocks

The full war of 2022 pushed Slavic literature into the deepest self-regulating of decades: the imperial question of Russian literature, the world moment of Ukrainian literature, the re-emergence of exile writing and the tension of the politics of memory in Central and Eastern Europe, which constituted a force for simultaneously rewriting the curriculum and theoretical agenda.
22.1 Self-liquidation of disciplines

After 2022, the Slav Research Decolonization changed from a marginal initiative to a disciplined agenda: Slav, the EEES, has designated the theme of the annual conference for 2023 as "Decolonization", the course of the university reorganization - - "History of Russian Literature" has expanded to include multi-ethnic literature in the Russian Empire, with a significant increase in posts and chairs in Ukrainian, Belarusian, Central Asia and the Caucasus. The theoretical argument is how the post-colonial framework (designed for the overseas empire) fits the land empire and "internal colonization" (the old idea of Etkind gained new life); Tolstoy and Dostoyevsky's empire imagine how to teach (critical reading instead of the bottom, the mainstream consensus); and whether "decolonization" will slide into a new nationalist orthodox. Ukrainian scholars' voices gained unprecedented weight in the process: Oksana Zabulzhko (see the reading box), Vitaly Chernetsky and others, calling for a long-standing "from Ukraine to the Russian Empire", moved from a marginal argument to a conference keynote speech.
22.2 Ukrainian literature: the moment and cost of the world

The war has accomplished the cruelest of "worldization" of Ukrainian literature: Zabzhko, Sershi Zhadan; in 2022, he was awarded the German Books Peace Prize; Andrey Kurkov became a regular guest in the global media; and in 1920-30, the "gunned Renaissance" generation, which was cleansed by Stalin, was discovered on a massive scale - – The story of the Hallkov “Slovo Building” has become a common image of history. Another level of war criticism is archives and mourning: the writer Victoria Ameline, who was transformed into a war crimes evidence researcher, died in 2023, and her unfinished work (English translation, "Looking at Women Looking at War, 2025) became the first-hand literature of "War Writing Conditions"; the list of killed writers and translators, the statistics of the destruction of the library, and the national aspirations of the front-line readings, all entered the research horizon. "The ongoing memory" (Chapter 11) is not a theoretical exercise here, but a daily exercise.

Zabzhko novelists, poets and cultural critics are among the most powerful theoreticalists of the Ukrainian perspective. The Ukrainian Sex Field Survey (Ukrainian) (е 1996 1996 1996 1996), 1996 ( 1996), which is a classic post-Soviet feminist criticism, is based on the dual colonization of the body and language; The abandoned secret museum (Face to the Queen) has been looking for me, and the Queen to the Queen to the Queen, 2009) has dealt with the taboo memory of the Ukrainian Resistance with the history of three generations of women. In April 2022, in a long article published in the Times Literature Supplement, she traced the cultural roots of the war to the unconsciousness of the empire of Russian literature. - "The world has read Russian novels for 200 years, but can't read Butch." The article became one of the triggers for the "Rereading the Russian Classic" debate. The response with Gihunnova-Etkind and others constituted a complete war file. Her public writing exemplifies an ethics of war criticism: both the refusal to literaryize the idea of propaganda and the refusal to avoid the complicity of imperial culture with "literacy innocent". The normality of the Nobel Prize awards list with the international award-winning table marked the arrival of Ukrainian criticisms.
22.3 Russian literature: studies in exile, global Russian and the culture of war

The Russian-owned academic and literary fields continue to shrink under review, and the largest intellectual exile since the third wave has taken shape: Maria Stepanova (Memorials), who came to me for advice, 2017; and In Memory of Memory, who entered the International Book Award shortlist 2021), Mikhail Hiskin, among others, who emigrated to Europe as a contemporary sample of the study of "Licens in exile" and the Russian-language publishing ecology of Berlin, Riga, Tel Aviv. Kevin M. F. Platt, editor-in-chief of the University of Pennsylvania, Global Russian Culture (2019), provides a prescient framework: Russian culture has always been a transnational area, and "Russian sex" is defined by several central competitions - The war has made the framework a practical manual for the reorganization of disciplines. The culture of war itself has been targeted: ideological analysis of the Z-poetry of the Z-war, diagnostic writings such as Etkind's Russia's War on Modernity (2023), and the "good Russian" argument (the moral position of the exiles) together constitute a sharp new sub-area. On the Central and Eastern European side, the battle between memory and memory (Poland, Hungary, chap. 11) is a continuing case; Laszlo Krasnohorkai was awarded the Nobel Prize in 2025, which allowed the Hungarian modernist "disaster sentence" to be re-enacted.

In spring 2022, "Can also teach Pushkin" became a real global classroom issue: the Ukrainian side called for a moratorium on the celebration of Russian culture (a coalition of Zabulzhko, playwright and music), Western media promoted the "elimination of Russian culture" and Russian authorities compiled any criticism as evidence of "the Western enemy". Three years later, academic sedimentation became clear: mainstream practice was not a downside, but an "imperial critical rereading" - - Pushkin's Defamators to Russia, Lemontov's Caucasus, Dostoyevsky's Mysaiahism into colonial language teaching; the curriculum added to the Ukrainian, Georgian and Central Asian versions as a bits of voice; and the "Cultural exception" (Applicable) Russian literature, as a super-political human heritage, was historically transformed into a soft power component of the Empire. The pedagogy legacy of this argument — how to teach the rank of an empire in wartime — will long go beyond the Slav research itself and provide reference to all the classroom of imperial literature.

blocks.text: 22.4 Observation

This region is a real-time laboratory for observing how the discipline of war is reordered: over three years, the curriculum, the position, the subject of periodicals and the theoretical framework have changed more than in the previous three decades. It also sounded a methodological alarm: the cold war structure of regional studies (with Moscow as its centre of resources) has shaped generations of knowledge maps — when political dramas have arrived, disciplines have had to supplement their maps' bias. This lesson is valid for all regional studies (including East Asia) where the default unit is "large countries".

Out of the theme and region, the title is presented in three general collections: a data and graphic perspective on changes in the discipline structure, a real structure of divergences with six battles, and a trend outlook and proposals for action, directed towards 2026-2030.
Data lens: periodicals, organics and discipline temperature

Four sets of data measure the changes described in the present report from different sides: the extent of the contraction of the establishment, the rhythm of institutionalization in new areas, structural imbalances in the flow of translations and the volume of the platform literature. All figures are estimates, and the calibre is shown in the note.
23.1 Institutional contraction: one index

Figure 23-1. Index of Bachelor of Arts Degrees Awards (Bachelor of American Arts) (2012=100) 100 2012 (peak) 93 2015 85 2018 79 2020 76 2022 The index is an estimate, and the calibres of the different disciplines (including/excluding communication, history) will cause a decrease of 20 to 30 per cent. The reduction in the professional specializations in English is generally greater than in the humanities.

The contractions were not limited to the United States: the British University College of Languages and Arts was cut in many universities in 2020, the tuition reform in Australia in 2021 significantly increased humanities fees, and the Korean Arts Democratization discussion re-emerging. At the same time, jobs and projects in the direction of interfaces such as creative writing, medical humanities, digital humanities, etc. are growing in reverse - not in the disappearance of disciplines, but in changing patterns and habitats.

blocks.text: Six major battles: the scene of contemporary literary research

Differences reveal more than consensus the true structure of a discipline. This chapter brings the scattered debates to six battles, each presented in three paragraphs of "Establishing - the camp - the status quo"; they do not have a decision, they are only issues that are being changed.

From: Nancy Z. Da ' 2019, "Calculated rebuttal" (Chapter 3). (b) The suspicion that the intensity of literary language meaning invalidates the statistical inference and the calculation is not trivial or erroneous; The defence (Androwwood, Pepper, So) admits that early research is crude, but the question of size (sorture, literary evolution) is essentially a matter of calculation. The debate has become routine with the "standards lift" end-of-the-ground -- data disclosure, hypothetical testing, and discussion of effectiveness; the true dividing line has moved to the question selection: the computationalists study "Lictionary System", the interpretation of the "works" of the authors, and there is an irreplaceable consensus on the mutuality. The arrival of LLM has re-enacted the new cycle of war: the model is "readable" and is a test.

Destiny: Felski, "Crisis of critical 2015" liquidation of "suspect default slot" (chap. 8). The post-criticalists advocate a description of attachment, repair and resonance; the left-wing rebound (Robins, Cornbour) warns of abdication of criticism and of politics; the "immediate" (2024) more of the "anti-intermediate" sentiment is diagnosed as a terminal form of capitalism; the reconstructionists (Gilory, Kramnik) bypass the position and repeat it as a testable handiwork knowledge. Status: The dual "criticism vs post critical" has been replaced by the "criticism of knowledge" issue — the rewriting of the discipline self-understanding is still going on, and the actual reading practice in the classroom is becoming more diverse.

The conflict between the Darmrosch Circulation doctrine and the inexplicable Aput (Chapter 7) is a serious problem. (b) The democratization of the flow of translation as literature by the negotiator; and the ideology of the critical (Apute, Mufti, WREC) as an English-language market, which is resistance with non-interpretability, monographic criticism and world systemism; The revisionist (Walkovics, Orsini, Sanchez Prado) decomposes the binary: the production end is "born and translated" and the multilingual and marginal strategy is far more complex than the "centre-margin" strategy. The debate has been settled into three heritages - the rise in the status of translation studies, the introduction of sociological data (Sapiro), the revival of multilingual literature; and the next round of machine translations.

Destiny is not a metaphor (2012) for early warning of conceptual inflation (chap. The platformers (Miñoro) demand a complete decoupling of the theory of understanding; materialists insist on the actual return of land and structures; internal criticisms (Tayvo, “No Decolonization”, Rivera Cusikanki) have criticized them for depriving Africa of its dynamism and integrating Southern experiences; and the "No discipline" of the various generations of research have advanced in practice. The debate has transformed decolonization from a posture to a framework of individualized research; the most productive results are in concrete terms — curricula, archives, publications and verifiable modifications for return.

Origin: 2021 German "Hicrows’ Competition 2.0": Moses ' German Church Questions and Answers ' s article questioning the uniqueness of the Holocaust has become the teaching of the State which impedes the settlement of colonial crimes (chap. 11). The uniqueness of the concerns leads to relativization and memory competition; the multi-way memory (Rosberg, Hershi) advocates non-zero-sumness, and memory is generated in mutual knowledge; and the national linguistic community emphasizes that memory politics cannot be separated from local law and politics (memory law, reparations litigation). (a) Current state: the controversy has spread to teaching and cultural policies (a memorial show, a literary prize speech), and "involving the subject" has become a term that is available to a few parties; The war in Ukraine and the Middle East continues to provide them with the most acute testing grounds.

Descending: Bats of text generated after ChatGPT (Chapter 4). The M.E. (Kilshenbaum "The End of the Text") warns synthetic text to pollute the writing ecology; the C.E.L.M. (nine R.I., the S.L.L.A.) sees LLM as a new creative condition; the C.I.L. (Tenan, Hears) rejects the exception — machine involvement in writing has been a problem for centuries, not a panic; the political economy (Critic AI) has moved the focus to data-grabbing and labour. The most advanced in theory is the "authorized distributed description" and "model style" study; And "What is not an outsourcing ability to read and write" is becoming the central argument of literature education.

blocks.text: Trends outlook: 2026-2030

Based on the earlier chain of evidence, this chapter gives seven trend judgements and 10 recommendations for the work of researchers. The judgements are probabilistic: they describe the direction of the force line, not the destined future.
25.1 Seven trend judgements

One, Model Criticism will become the regular branch. The criticisms targeted at LLM — the ordinary reading, the scrutinizing, the examination of its archives — will be consolidated from marginal experiments into courses, periodicals and positions, as was done in the film research; “Synthetic Texts of Arts” and “Archives of Training Data” are the first-formulated subdirectorials.

Second, the culture of evidence continues to harden. The norms of data and code disclosure, description of language calibres, pre-registration of experiments will spill over from the computation and empirical camps, and explanatory studies will more frequently mark the boundaries of their archives - "What is the basis for your claims" as a matter of discipline-wide default.

Third, planetary scales and two-way movement of local archives. The climate agenda promotes the re-engineering of the literary history to planetary scales (deep time, material flow, energy regime), while at the same time generating unprecedented demand for local and multilingual archives — both not contradictory but mutually dependent; the success of Planetary Humanities depends on the pace of building the archives in the South.

Fourth, the multipolarization of theoretical production accelerates. Writing - the "Stand and East Concept" of survival, depossession, water colonialism, mutation, hidden processes will be brought into international circulation more quickly; The English intermediate status will not disappear, but the default equation of Theory = Europe and America will continue to relax.

Five, Triangular flows of platform literature research. The Chinese web site, the African Digital Network and the European and American platforms will combine their approaches (industrial analysis + computing acceptance of research + national aspirations), and the Platform Literature will probably have its own handbook and flagship by 2030.

VI. The restructuring of the formed form continues. The contraction of traditional literature parallels the expansion of the interface disciplines (medical, creative, public, digital); The professional status of literary researchers will be more expensive - a self-presentation of the subjects by the promoter, data manager, narrative consultant, writer and therapist.

VII. Review and academic freedom become a condition for research. Books, memory laws, gender studies bans and war reviews will continue, and "research how" will itself be the subject of more research; the development of transnational academic mutual assistance networks (replacement of exiled scholars, mirroring archives) will be considered as a discipline infrastructure.
25.2 Ten recommendations for researchers
25.3 Concluding remarks

The report begins with the paradox that institutional contraction coincides with the expansion of knowledge. After 25 chapters, the paradox can be replaced by the saying that literary research is transforming its crisis into a method — the suspicion of reading has given rise to the ethics of reading science and reading, the clearing of the orthodox has given rise to new archival, and the anxiety about “literacy dead” has given rise to an unprecedented awakening of the conditions in which literature is produced. The healthiest mark of a discipline is not an argument, but a debate over the continued production of new tools, targets and responsibilities. In this sense, the literature of the 2020s is much more alive than its pessimistic narrative suggests.
